author: Piccirello

title: feat(llma): add BUFFER_ONE overlap policy and execution timeouts to temporal schedules
body:
## Problem

LLMA Temporal schedules (summarization and clustering) used the default SKIP overlap policy. When a coordinator workflow got stuck (as happened today with the trace summarization coordinator running for 7+ hours), all subsequent hourly triggers were silently skipped. There was also no execution timeout on the coordinator workflows, so they could run indefinitely and block the schedule.

## Changes

- Set `ScheduleOverlapPolicy.BUFFER_ONE` on all 4 LLMA coordinator schedules (trace summarization, generation summarization, trace clustering, generation clustering) so at least one run is buffered instead of silently dropped
- Add 55-minute coordinator execution timeout for hourly summarization schedules (under the 1-hour interval)
- Add 12-hour coordinator execution timeout for daily clustering schedules (under the 24-hour interval)

## How did you test this code?

- Existing tests pass (13 summarization tests, 92 clustering tests)
- Verified schedule definitions are correct by reading the updated code

**Post-deploy step required**: Re-register schedules in both clusters:
```bash
kubectl -n posthog exec deploy/temporal-worker -- python manage.py schedule_temporal_workflows
```

## Publish to changelog?

Do not publish to changelog.

title: fix(temporal): reduce realtime cohorts concurrency to prevent ClickHouse overload
body:
## Problem

PostHog production is experiencing ClickHouse query overload with error "Too many simultaneous queries for all users. Current: 504/506, maximum: 500".

Reduced realtime cohort calculation concurrency parameters to alleviate ClickHouse query pressure:
- `DEFAULT_COORDINATOR_PARALLELISM`: 10 → 6 (40% reduction)
- `DEFAULT_WORKFLOWS_PER_BATCH`: 5 → 2 (60% reduction)

These changes maintain realtime cohort functionality while significantly reducing the number of concurrent ClickHouse queries generated by the temporal workflow coordinator.

## How did you test this code?

Testing will be conducted in production by monitoring ClickHouse query load metrics after deployment. Key metrics to monitor:
- Current/maximum simultaneous query counts
- Query failure rates

title: fix(dags): use TIMESTAMP instead of TIMESTAMPTZ in duckling table DDL
body:
## Summary

- Changes the DuckLake table DDL for both events and persons tables to use `TIMESTAMP` instead of `TIMESTAMPTZ`
- Fixes `ducklake_add_data_files` type mismatch error when registering Parquet files

## Problem

The `ducklake_add_data_files` function expects plain timestamps (`TIMESTAMP_NS/TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMP_MS/TIMESTAMP_S`), not `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`. ClickHouse exports `DateTime64` columns as TZ-aware Parquet timestamps, causing a type mismatch error:

```
Failed to map column "created_at" from file "..." to the column in table "persons"
* Expected one of TIMESTAMP_NS, TIMESTAMP, TIMESTAMP_MS, TIMESTAMP_S,
  found type TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
```

## Solution

Changed the DuckLake table DDL to use `TIMESTAMP` instead of `TIMESTAMPTZ` for all timestamp columns in both events and persons tables.

## Test plan

- [ ] Deploy and verify duckling persons backfill jobs succeed
- [ ] For existing tables with the wrong schema, run with `delete_tables=True` to recreate them

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: fuziontech

title: fix(ci): fetch base branch before migration detection in Node.js CI
body:
The migration detection step was failing silently because `origin/master` (or the base branch) wasn't fetched in the shallow clone. This caused the fast path to always be taken, restoring the schema artifact from master even when the PR contains migrations.

CI throws an error:
```
Run if git diff --name-only origin/master...HEAD | grep -E '(migrations/|rust/.*migrate)'; then                                    
  fatal: ambiguous argument 'origin/master...HEAD': unknown revision or path not in the working tree.                                       
  Use '--' to separate paths from revisions, like this:                                                                                         
  'git <command> [<revision>...] -- [<file>...]'
  ```

Add a fetch step to retrieve the base branch before running git diff.

title: feat(llma): replace TracesQueryRunner with lightweight sampling queries
body:
## Problem

The `sample_items_in_window_activity` Temporal activity was using `TracesQueryRunner` (the full UI query runner) to sample trace IDs and timestamps. This runner executes a heavy Phase 2 query that aggregates full event properties, person data, costs, and tokens via `groupArrayIf(tuple(uuid, event, timestamp, properties), ...)`. On high-volume teams, this caused ClickHouse OOM errors (`Query has reached the max memory limit`) even for 1-hour windows requesting only 15-50 items.

The sampling activity only needs trace IDs and first timestamps -- all the heavy data is fetched later per-item by the summarization activity using `TraceQueryRunner`.

## Changes

- Replace `TracesQueryRunner` in `sampling.py` with lightweight HogQL queries that only fetch `trace_id` and `min(timestamp)`
- For generation-level sampling, use a single direct query instead of the previous two-step approach (heavy trace query then separate generations query)
- Remove `TracesQueryRunner` import from sampling module
- Update tests to mock `execute_hogql_query` instead of `TracesQueryRunner`

## How did you test this code?

- Ran existing test suite: `pytest posthog/temporal/llm_analytics/trace_summarization/tests/test_workflow.py` -- all 9 tests pass
- Added new test for generation-level sampling path

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: feat(ph-ai): error issue context on new tab
body:
## Problem

When the `UX_REMOVE_SIDEPANEL` [ff](https://us.posthog.com/project/2/feature_flags/446468) is enabled, PostHog AI opens in a new tab instead of a side panel. This caused the "Explain this issue" functionality in error tracking to break  due to missing error context.

This is currently only for us internally but let's fix it already and make it extensible if needed.

**Before**:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f839718d-a7ea-4744-a1e7-018f42a9ccfb

**After**:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5e176293-e8f6-4f05-8876-248f1dd877b4

## Changes

* Implemented a sessionStorage-based pattern to pass context across tab navigation, in a similar fashion to what I already did with prompts (`PENDING_AI_PROMPT_KEY`).
* Generic context interface if other former contextual tool need this during transitioning phase

I avoid race conditions between reducer updates and selector recomputation givben that context is passed directly to `askMax(prompt, addToThread, uiContext)` instead of updating global state, which is cleaner.

## How did you test this code?

- [x] manual tests
- [x] unit tests
author: tatoalo

title: feat(deduplicator): ignore no-op rebalance events
body:
## Problem
The deduplicator is tracking a lot of unneeded state changes that are no-ops, skewing our rebalance metrics. In sticky assignment mode, the broker sends one of these no-op events with an empty TopicPartitionList to every pod whenever any pods join or leave the group.

While we have safeguards in place to ensure we're not doing a lot of extra work, the pileup of no-op async tasks and metrics being ticked (including high expected cancellation rates and local rebalance-in-flight counts) is confusing and not needed. In this consumer group mode, these events don't mean anything.

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes
* Skip rebalance handling for broker-triggered rebalance events with empty TPL lists
* Related metrics and tests

title: fix(dagster): use correct schema parameter in ducklake_add_data_files
body:
## Summary

- Fix `ducklake_add_data_files` function calls to use the correct parameter format
- The function expects table name and schema as separate parameters, not combined as `'main.events'`
- This was causing the error `Table with name main.events does not exist!` because it was looking for a table literally named "main.events" instead of the "events" table in the "main" schema

### Changes

**Before:**
```python
ducklake_add_data_files(catalog, 'main.events', path)
ducklake_add_data_files(catalog, 'main.persons', path)
```

**After:**
```python
ducklake_add_data_files(catalog, 'events', path, schema => 'main')
ducklake_add_data_files(catalog, 'persons', path, schema => 'main')
```

### Files Changed
- `posthog/dags/events_backfill_to_ducklake.py` - Multi-tenant DuckLake backfill
- `posthog/dags/events_backfill_to_duckling.py` - Single-tenant duckling backfill (events + persons)

## Test plan

- [ ] Re-run the failed Dagster job `duckling_events_backfill` to verify the fix
- [ ] Verify file registration succeeds without `Table with name main.events does not exist!` error

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

title: fix(batch-exports): Fix bug related to race condition in building sessions model query
body:
## Problem

There's a race condition that can occur if two separate batch exports are running concurrently and trying to export sessions models. We do not clone the base HogQL query we're using, which means one batch export can overwrite the where clause of another.

## Changes

- Use `clone_expr` to ensure we return a separate instance of the base HogQL expression

## How did you test this code?

- Added some tests to reproduce the issue and ensure they now pass

title: fix: create posthog client for ingestion acceptance tests
body:
## Problem

Running the acceptance test suite as a Temporal job is not yielding the same results as simply running it manually (through a main). After some digging, the most likely culprit here is the reliance on the "singleton" instance of the posthog client, which might be modified by other parts of the code.

## Changes

Rather than relying on the singleton instance, the test suite now instantiates its own instance of the client, shielding it from external interference.

title: fix(ci): lower artifact threshold to 38 for test timing workflow
body:
## Summary

- Lowers the artifact count threshold from 40 to 38 in the test timing update workflow to match the actual number of artifacts produced by recent CI runs

## Problem

The `Backend CI - Update test timing` scheduled workflow was failing 27.8% of the time (5/18 runs in 30 days) because:

1. The workflow requires >40 timing artifacts to proceed
2. Recent Backend CI runs only produce 38 artifacts
3. Temporal tests (10 jobs) don't upload timing artifacts because they have empty `clickhouse-server-image` matrix values, causing the upload condition to be skipped

## Solution

Lower the threshold from 40 to 38 to match the actual artifact count from Core test jobs. This unblocks the weekly timing update workflow while still ensuring sufficient timing data for pytest-split sharding.

## Evidence

- Failure pattern from [run 21556250004](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/actions/runs/21556250004): "Run 21551925799 has 38 timing artifacts" → "No suitable CI run found"
- [Backend CI run 21551925799](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/actions/runs/21551925799): 50 test jobs executed, only 38 artifacts uploaded (all from Core segment)


---

> [!NOTE]
> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.

title: fix: event definition by_name endpoint filtering
body:
## Problem

Error: https://us.posthog.com/project/2/error_tracking/019c0a74-5196-7c82-b308-d42ee739a2d7?timestamp=2026-02-01%2001%3A14%3A22.670000-08%3A00

The `by_name` endpoint for event definitions was using a different filtering logic than the list endpoint, causing lookups to fail for existing events.

title: chore: add diagnostic logging for shard 38/40
body:
## Summary

Add enhanced observability for Django Core test shards to enable root cause diagnosis for intermittent failures like shard 38/40.

### Changes

- **Environment diagnostics step**: Logs shard number, Python/ClickHouse versions, commit, branch, system resources (memory, disk), and Docker container status before tests run
- **Enhanced pytest output**: Added `-v` (verbose) and `--tb=short` flags to capture test names and stack traces
- **Extra test summary**: Added `-r fEsxX` flag to show detailed summary for failures, errors, skipped, xfailed, and xpassed tests

### Context

Django Core shard 38/40 experienced a sudden spike in failures on Jan 30, 2026:
- Historical baseline: 0% failure rate (353 consecutive successes over 7 days)
- Spike: 4 failures across different commits
- Root cause unknown due to insufficient logging

The failures occurred on unrelated commits (`d3cfd12`, `2ab1e35`), suggesting an infrastructure issue or flaky test rather than a code regression.

### What this enables

After 5-10 failures with enhanced logging, we can:
1. Identify specific test names that fail
2. See stack traces and error messages
3. Check environment state (memory, disk, containers) at time of failure
4. Determine if issue is timeout, assertion failure, or infrastructure problem

### Testing

This change only adds logging and doesn't affect test behavior. The pytest flags are well-established and used in other CI workflows.


---

> [!NOTE]
> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.

title: fix(conversations): gate sidepanel tickets API calls behind feature flag
body: ## Problem We were noticing issues like this ## Summary - Fixes 429 "Too many requests" errors for users who don't have the support product enabled - The `sidepanelTicketsLogic` was making API requests for **all users** because `SidePanelSupportIcon` (always rendered as the Help tab icon) mounts the logic, which triggers `loadTickets()` on mount - The feature flag (`PRODUCT_SUPPORT_SIDE_PANEL`) was only gating the UI, not the API calls ## Changes - Added `isEnabled` selector that checks the `PRODUCT_SUPPORT_SIDE_PANEL` feature flag - Guarded all API-calling actions (`loadTickets`, `loadMessages`, `sendMessage`, `markAsRead`, `startPolling`) with `values.isEnabled` - Guarded lifecycle hooks (`afterMount`, visibility change listener, `sidePanelOpen` subscription) ## Test plan - [x] Verify users **with** the feature flag enabled can still use the support tickets panel - [x] Verify users **without** the feature flag no longer see 429 errors in console - [x] Verify no API requests are made to `/api/conversations/v1/widget/tickets` when feature flag is disabled

title: fix(feature-flags): set default rollout percentage to prevent validation errors
body:
## Problem

When users click "Save" on a new feature flag without making changes to the release conditions, they encounter a "submit feature flag failure" error. This happens because the default `rollout_percentage` is set to `undefined`, which triggers the validation error "You need to set a rollout % value" and prevents form submission.

## Changes

- Set default `rollout_percentage` to `100` in the `NEW_FLAG` definition
- Set default `rollout_percentage` to `100` when adding new condition sets via `addConditionSet`
- Ensures new feature flags have valid rollout percentages that pass validation

## How did you test this code?

- Verified existing tests continue to pass
- Tested that the validation logic now accepts the default 100% rollout percentage

title: feat: Use object_store crate for deduplicator s3 upload
body:
## Problem

The kafka-deduplicator's S3 checkpoint uploads were loading entire files into memory before uploading, which could cause OOM issues with large RocksDB SST files (64MB). They were also not using multipart upload. 

This PR takes advantage of Apache Arrow's `object_store` crate to optimize s3 uploads.

## Changes

- Refactored S3 uploader to use the `object_store` crate for streaming multipart uploads
- Files are now streamed directly from disk to S3 using `BufWriter` with automatic backpressure handling

title: fix(dagster): remove person reconciliation job sensor run_key
body:
## Problem
The `run_key` pattern is preventing us from running the backfill job over the same window using the sensor to coordinate and rate limit chunked job launches.

This pattern is useful for event-triggered or scheduled idempotent runs, but is a bad match for our use case, which is using the sensor to coordinate incident-driven one-off backfills where repeat dry run testing and/or manual triggers over the same team ID ranges will frequently be our goal.

The `run_key` is not used to coordinate paused/resumed sensor state or log/tag which jobs are running which chunks, so this won't remove observability or cause every sensor enablement to start the run fresh. It should be a no-op for our purposes 👍 
<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes
Remove the `run_key` from sensor-launched child jobs/tasks

title: feat(dagster): add _inserted_at column to ducklake backfill
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two issues with the DuckLake backfill job:

1. Adds the `_inserted_at` system column to maintain schema consistency with Kafka Connect
2. Disables Hive partitioning to prevent ClickHouse errors

## Context

### `_inserted_at` column

The DuckLake Kafka Connect ingestor was recently updated to include a `_inserted_at` TIMESTAMP column that tracks when data was inserted into DuckLake (see [ducklake-kafka-connect commits 56db5e7 and 4cb184b](https://github.com/PostHog/ducklake-kafka-connect/commits)).

The backfill job needs to export this same column to maintain schema consistency between:
- Real-time events ingested via Kafka Connect
- Historical events backfilled from ClickHouse

### Hive partitioning issue

ClickHouse was auto-detecting Hive partitioning from the S3 path pattern (`/year=YYYY/month=MM/day=DD/`) and expecting `year`, `month`, and `day` columns in the SELECT statement. This caused the error:

```
All hive partitioning columns must be present in the schema. Missing column: day.
```

Since we use the path structure purely for file organization (not for storing partition columns in the data), we need to explicitly disable Hive partitioning.

## Changes

1. Added `NOW() as _inserted_at` to the `EVENTS_COLUMNS` SELECT list
2. Added `_inserted_at` to the `EXPECTED_DUCKLAKE_COLUMNS` validation set
3. Added `use_hive_partitioning=0` to the S3 INSERT settings

The `_inserted_at` column is populated with `NOW()` to represent when the backfill ran, which is semantically correct - it tracks insertion time into DuckLake, not the original event timestamp (which is captured in the `timestamp` column).

## Test plan

- Verify the backfill job runs without the Hive partitioning error
- Confirm that backfilled Parquet files include the `_inserted_at` column
- Check that DuckLake ingestion successfully loads backfilled data with the new column
- Verify schema validation passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: fix(dagster): use DATA node role for ducklake backfill exports
body:
## Problem

The `events_ducklake_backfill` Dagster job was failing with:
```
ValueError: No hosts found with roles [<NodeRole.COORDINATOR: 'coordinator'>]
```

The ClickHouse cluster in the Dagster environment doesn't have any hosts configured with the `coordinator` role, causing the job to fail when trying to export events to S3.

## Solution

Changed the node role from `NodeRole.COORDINATOR` to `NodeRole.DATA` for the export operation. This aligns with the pattern used by other similar jobs in the codebase (e.g., `deletes.py:383,829`).

The export operation (`INSERT INTO FUNCTION s3(...) SELECT FROM events`) doesn't require coordinator-specific capabilities and can safely run on DATA nodes, which have access to the events table.

## Test plan

- Verify the job runs successfully in Dagster after deployment
- Confirm that events are properly exported to S3 for DuckLake ingestion

title: feat(deduplicator): extend rebalance cancellation to individual file import attempts
body:
## Problem
During checkpoint import and store manager setup, we only cancel the high level import attempts when another rebalance is triggered while one is in flight. This can leave long-running S3 `GetObject` and other calls running async until completion, competing with the new rebalance's import attempts, only to drop the results.

title: fix(storybook): disable filesystem cache in CI to prevent heap OOM
body:
## Summary
- Disables webpack filesystem cache when `CI` env var is set to prevent heap out-of-memory errors during Storybook build cache shutdown
- Particularly affects memory-constrained environments like Cloudflare Pages

## Problem
Storybook builds on Cloudflare Pages were hitting heap OOM during the cache shutdown phase. The webpack filesystem cache writes large amounts of data to disk at build completion, which can exhaust available memory on constrained build environments.

## Solution
Conditionally disable the filesystem cache when running in CI (`process.env.CI`). Local development still benefits from the cache for faster rebuilds.

title: feat(infra): make nginx unit request limit configurable
body:
## Problem

looking into query related memory pressure, we found that Unit workers hold onto memory after processing large query results

The `limits.requests: 7500` setting from #21649 was tuned for web. query now deals with much bigger result sets and hits memory limits well before workers get a chance to recycle.

Making this configurable lets us tune it per-deployment without affecting existing behavior.

## Changes

- Add `NGINX_UNIT_REQUEST_LIMIT` env var (defaults to 7500)
- Use it in `unit.json.tpl` instead of hardcoded value

## How did you test this code?

Locally verified envsubst works and default is unchanged. Will test with lower value on query in dev.

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: fix(cdp): Fix grouped logs pagination for non-UTC project timezones
body:
## Problem

Came from a [report](https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/53800). Customers with non-UTC project timezones cannot load more grouped logs in CDP destination logs. Clicking "Load up to 10 older groups" does nothing because `toAbsoluteClickhouseTimestamp` formats the pagination cursor in UTC, but the HogQL backend interprets bare timestamps as being in the team's timezone. For a project in `America/Bogota` (UTC-5), this shifts the `date_to` cutoff 5 hours forward, making the subquery return the same 10 instances every time.

Confirmed by inspecting the generated ClickHouse SQL: `toDateTime64('2026-03-25 20:34:45.084000', 6, 'America/Bogota')` shows the backend applying team timezone to what was intended as a UTC value.

## Changes

Fix `toAbsoluteClickhouseTimestamp` to format in the team timezone instead of UTC, matching what the HogQL backend expects for bare timestamp strings

## How did you test this code?

- Set local project timezone to `America/Bogota` to reproduce the bug
- Verified the fix resolves the issue (10 new groups load on each click)
- Updated unit tests to cover team timezone formatting via mocked `teamLogic`

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: dmarchuk

commits.message: fix(cdp): fix grouped logs pagination for non-UTC project timezones

author: dmarticus

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Removes the deprecated tag-based feature flag evaluation models and fully transitions API/tooling/frontend references from `evaluation_tags` to `evaluation_contexts`, while keeping underlying legacy DB tables for safety.

**Changes:**
- Remove deprecated Django models/signal handlers/endpoints tied to `FeatureFlagEvaluationTag` and `TeamDefaultEvaluationTag` (tables retained; FK constraints dropped).
- Rename API/query/body fields from `evaluation_tags`/`has_evaluation_tags` to `evaluation_contexts`/`has_evaluation_contexts` across backend, Rust service, MCP tooling, frontend, and tests.
- Update tag cleanup logic and regenerate affected snapshots/schemas.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 42 out of 50 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| services/mcp/tests/unit/__snapshots__/tool-schemas/v1/feature-flag-get-all.json | Snapshot update for `has_evaluation_contexts` query param. |
| services/mcp/tests/unit/__snapshots__/tool-schemas/common/update-feature-flag.json | Snapshot update to `evaluation_contexts` request field. |
| services/mcp/tests/unit/__snapshots__/tool-schemas/common/create-feature-flag.json | Snapshot update to `evaluation_contexts` request field. |
| services/mcp/src/tools/generated/feature_flags.ts | MCP tool param/body/query key rename to `evaluation_contexts`/`has_evaluation_contexts`. |
| services/mcp/src/generated/feature_flags/api.ts | Zod schemas updated to new field/param names and descriptions. |
| services/mcp/src/api/generated.ts | Generated Typescript API types updated to remove/rename `evaluation_tags`. |
| rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_models.rs | Rust deserialization updated to accept `evaluation_contexts` via serde alias. |
| rust/feature-flags/src/flags/feature_flag_list.rs | Rust tests updated to use `evaluation_contexts` JSON field. |
| products/surveys/frontend/

commits.message:
chore: remove deprecated FeatureFlagEvaluationTag and TeamDefaultEvaluationTag models

Remove the deprecated evaluation tag models and complete the migration
to evaluation contexts. This includes removing old model classes, signal
handlers, API endpoints, and updating all references from
evaluation_tags to evaluation_contexts across the codebase.

The migration uses SeparateDatabaseAndState to remove the models from
Django state while keeping the database tables intact, and drops FK
constraints to avoid TransactionTestCase TRUNCATE issues.

Fixes #50712

fix: add serde alias for evaluation_contexts on Rust flag models

The MinimalFeatureFlagSerializer now writes evaluation_contexts as the
cache key, but the Rust service reads evaluation_tags. Add a serde alias
so both field names are accepted during deserialization.

fix: address PR review feedback

- Fix falsy `or` in cache deserialization that caused empty
  evaluation_contexts lists to fall through to None, triggering N+1
  DB queries instead of using the cached empty list
- Update stale error messages from "evaluation context tag" to
  "evaluation context"
- Fix Rust test that had both evaluation_contexts and evaluation_tags
  fields in JSON, causing serde duplicate field error with the alias

fix: remove team_defaults reference in conversations tag cleanup

Same fix as tagged_item.py — the team_defaults reverse relation came
from the now-removed TeamDefaultEvaluationTag model.

Merge branch 'master' into dmarticus/drop-eval-tags

Also address PR feedback:
- Add else branch to discard legacy evaluation_tags key when
  evaluation_contexts is present (prevents stale keys)
- Update stale comments referencing "evaluation tags"

fix: use correct Django FK constraint names in migration

Django auto-generates truncated constraint names with CRC32 hash
suffixes, not the simple `tablename_column_fkey` pattern. The
previous names silently no-opped via DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS,
leaving the FK constraints in place and causing TRUNCATE failures
in TransactionTestCase teardown.

fix: address PR review feedback

- Preserve request order for evaluation context names instead of using
  non-deterministic set comprehension
- Add FeatureFlagEvaluationContext signal handler to flags_cache.py
  (HyperCache) so the Rust service cache is invalidated when contexts
  are added or removed from a flag

title: feat(feature-flags): replace flag type dropdown with selectable cards and improve variant readability
body:
## Problem

The feature flag creation/edit form had usability issues surfaced by internal feedback:

- **Flag type selector** was a dropdown, making it hard to see all three options (Boolean, Multivariate, Remote config) at a glance
- **Variant collapse panels** were hard to read in dark mode and content wasn't aligned with the variant lettermarks

## Changes

- Replace flag type `LemonSelect` dropdown with a 3-column grid of selectable cards, each showing the icon, label, and description at a glance. Selected card gets a highlighted background and accent border with a check icon
- Add proper ARIA `radiogroup`/`radio` semantics and keyboard navigation (Enter/Space) for accessibility
- Add subtle background (`dark:bg-surface-secondary`) and inter-panel borders to variant collapse panels for better dark mode readability
- Indent variant panel content (`pl-[2.5rem]`) to align with the variant lettermarks rather than sitting under the chevron

## How did you test this code?

- Verified TypeScript compiles with no new errors
- Visually tested in both light and dark mode
- Tested keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Space) on the flag type cards

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed.
author: dmarticus

reviews.body:
The UI looks really good.

The AI comment is legit, it's impossible to navigate between options using tab (it's possible to navigate using arrow keys though, after using tab to get to the field). Not sure if this was the behavior that was expected.

I think it's valid in this PRs that are changing the UI (especially these smaller ones), to add an image to the description to illustrate the change.

commits.message:
feat(feature-flags): replace flag type dropdown with selectable cards and improve variant panel readability

Replace the flag type LemonSelect dropdown with a grid of accessible radio
cards showing Boolean, Multivariate, and Remote config at a glance. Add proper
ARIA radiogroup semantics and keyboard navigation. Improve variant collapse
panel readability in dark mode with subtle background and border styling, and
indent panel content to align with variant lettermarks.

fix(feature-flags): address PR feedback on flag type cards

- Extract IIFE into top-level variables (currentFlagType, onSelectFlagType,
  FLAG_TYPE_OPTIONS) for cleaner JSX
- Fix conflicting border-width utilities by conditionally applying border vs
  border-2 instead of both
- Implement proper ARIA roving tabindex pattern: only selected card gets
  tabIndex={0}, others get tabIndex={-1}, with arrow key navigation
fix(feature-flags): make flag type cards selectable via tab focus
fix(feature-flags): allow tabbing between flag type cards without selecting

title: chore(flags): migrate super_groups flags key (phase 2)
body:
Part of #47929 

## Summary

Phase 2 of the `super_groups` → `feature_enrollment` migration. The Rust flag service now reads the new `feature_enrollment: true` boolean format, with fallback to legacy `super_groups`.

Phase 1 (merged) had Django dual-writing both formats. This PR makes the Rust evaluation prefer the new format when present.

### Changes by file

**`rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_models.rs`**
- Added `feature_enrollment: Option<bool>` field to `FlagFilters` struct, with `#[serde(default)]` so existing JSON without this field deserializes cleanly.

**`rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_matching.rs`**
- Rewrote the super condition evaluation block in `get_match()` as an `if/else if`:
  - `feature_enrollment == Some(true)` → derives `$feature_enrollment/{flag_key}` from the flag key, checks person properties directly. Much simpler than parsing the property filter structure from `super_groups`.
  - `else if super_groups` present → legacy path, unchanged (fallback).
- Existing `is_super_condition_match` method and `SuperConditionEvaluation` struct left intact for the legacy fallback (removed in Phase 4).

**`rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_filters.rs`**
- `requires_db_properties` now takes a `flag_key` parameter. If `feature_enrollment == Some(true)` and overrides don't contain the derived enrollment key, DB lookup is required.

**`rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_operations.rs`**
- Updated the single callsite of `requires_db_properties` to pass `&self.key`.

**`rust/feature-flags/src/flags/test_flag_matching.rs`**
- Added 3 new tests:
  - `test_feature_enrollment_matches_enrolled_person` — enrolled person matches, non-enrolled falls through to regular conditions.
  - `test_feature_enrollment_takes_precedence_over_super_groups` — both formats present, new format wins.
  - `test_feature_enrollment_false_value_does_not_match` — enrollment property set to "false" does not match.

**All test/struct files (9 files)**
- Added `feature_enrollment: None` to all `FlagFilters` struct literals.

## Test plan

- [x] 3 new feature enrollment tests pass
- [x] 6 existing super condition tests pass (legacy fallback works)
- [x] 12 flag_filters tests pass (requires_db_properties changes)

title: fix: improve secret API key validation
body:
## Problem

`ProjectSecretAPIKeyAuthentication.find_secret_api_token` enforces the `phs_` prefix for tokens in the `Authorization` header but accepts any value from `data["secret_api_key"]` in the request body. This means an attacker can use a public project token (`phc_*`) — exposed in frontend JS snippets — to authenticate against secret-key-gated endpoints (`local_evaluation`, `remote_config`, `active_breakpoints`).

All three affected endpoints are read-only (GET), so the impact is data exposure (feature flag definitions, remote config values, debugger breakpoints), not integrity or availability.

## Changes

- **Enforce `phs_` prefix on body-extracted tokens** (`posthog/auth.py`): Apply the same `phs_` prefix + alphanumeric regex validation to body tokens that already exists for header tokens. Non-`phs_` tokens in the body are now silently rejected.
- **Normalize header token extraction** (`posthog/auth.py`): Refactor the header regex to capture the full token first, then validate format separately — consistent with the body path.
- **Add deprecation metric** (`posthog/auth.py`): `SECRET_API_KEY_BODY_COUNTER` Prometheus counter tracks body-based secret key usage for future deprecation planning.
- **Parameterized tests** (`posthog/api/test/test_authentication.py`): Covers public tokens, non-prefixed tokens, empty strings, and integer values in the body — all correctly rejected.

## How did you test this code?

- Added parameterized auth tests for invalid body tokens (public token, non-prefixed, empty, integer) — all rejected
- Ran existing `TestProjectSecretAPIKeyAuthentication` suite — all pass
- Manually tested with curl.

Do not publish to changelog

## Docs update

No docs changes needed.
author: haacked

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Fixes an auth bypass in `ProjectSecretAPIKeyAuthentication` caused by accepting arbitrary body tokens and by sharing a cache keyspace between public (`phc_*`) and secret (`phs_*`) project tokens. **Changes:** - Enforces `phs_` prefix for body-provided `secret_api_key` and adds a Prometheus counter for body-based secret key usage. - Segregates team token cache entries into `team_token:public:{token}` vs `team_token:secret:{token}`. - Adds/extends tests to cover invalid body tokens and cache segregation behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | posthog/auth.py | Adds body token prefix validation + Prometheus counter for body usage. | | posthog/models/team/team_caching.py | Introduces token-type-specific cache keying for teams. | | posthog/models/team/team.py | Routes secret token lookups/writes through the secret cache keyspace. | | posthog/test/test_team.py | Adds test verifying public/secret cache segregation. | | posthog/api/test/test_authentication.py | Adds parameterized tests ensuring invalid body tokens don’t authenticate. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
It's a good thing this was caught before we expanded the functionality of Project Secret API keys 😬

commits.message:
fix: prevent secret API key auth bypass via prefix validation on body tokens

When a secret API key is provided in the request body instead of the Authorization
header, validate that it has the phs_ prefix before accepting it. This prevents an
attacker from using a public token (phc_*) to bypass authentication on secret-key-gated
endpoints (local_evaluation, remote_config, active_breakpoints).

Use a pre-compiled regex for both header and body validation to enforce consistent
token format checking and improve performance on the hot authentication path.

Add a deprecation counter (SECRET_API_KEY_BODY_COUNTER) to track how often the body
path is used, enabling future migration of callers to the Authorization header.

title: chore: tag cache_key in query log_comment
body:
## Problem

I noticed identical SQL queries re-execute on ClickHouse 1-3 seconds apart despite the cache staleness threshold being 5+ minutes and the execution mode being `blocking` (which means it goes to the cache first). 

https://metabase.prod-us.posthog.dev/question/2001-duplicate-blocking-queries

To diagnose whether this is caused by different cache keys vs cache write failures, we need the `cache_key visible in the query log.

## Changes

Adds `tag_queries(cache_key=cache_key)` to `QueryRunner.run()` 

## How did you test this code?

N/A

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: andyzzhao

reviews.body: Gates denied this PR because the change matched the crypto_secrets deny-list pattern (due to the `cache_key` variable being tagged). This requires human review to confirm the cache_key value doesn't risk exposing sensitive data in query logs.

Adds cache_key to the ClickHouse log_comment tags so we can diagnose
why blocking-mode queries bypass the cache and re-execute identical SQL.

title: feat: Bigquery integration with impersonation support
body:
## Problem

Currently, we require users who want to batch export data to BigQuery to provide us with a service account key file. Storing long-lived credentials is not secure.

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes

Implement a new integration model `GoogleCloudServiceAccountIntegration`. This integration model supports both the current way of authenticating with a full service account key file, and a new way that does not require exchanging credentials.

With the new authentication mechanisms, we only require users to provide us with a service account email and a project id. Notably: We do not require the  keys of the service account, in fact, no key even has to be created. In return, we give users a PostHog-owned service account and ask them to grant our service account permission to impersonate their service account.

Once granted, in the BigQuery batch export we can authenticate with our service account using AWS credentials, and immediately impersonate our user's service account, and continue as normal.

Our user's service account still needs to have the BigQuery permissions we normally require for batch exports, that has not changed.

Additionally, we require that the service account we impersonate contain a description including `posthog:{organization_id}`. This is done to verify that the owner of the google cloud service account wants batch exports from `organization_id` using it. This helps mitigate the confused deputy problem: An evil organization could sign up with a service account belonging to a good organization, and potentially flood them with garbage data. At runtime, we check the description matches the organization of the current team id. As the evil organization cannot control this context of the batch export, this would prevent the attack.

Of course, if the evil organization has breached good organization's google cloud account, then they can update the description. But if they have achieved such a breach, they can do much more damage in other areas than just batch exporting garbage data from PostHog. 

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->
<!-- If a reference design was involved, include a link to the relevant Figma frame! -->

## How did you test this code?

Ran all bigquery tests. Added new ones and extended existing ones.

<!-- Briefly describe the steps you took. -->
<!-- Include automated tests if possible, otherwise describe the manual testing routine. -->
<!-- If you are an agent writing this, do NOT include manual tasks you have NOT completed. You can clearly outline you're simply an agent and you haven't tested this manually except for code-based unit/integration tests -->

👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._

## Publish to changelog?

Yes, but maybe wait until the feature flag is enabled? We'll roll it out progressively, so it will not be immediately available upon merging. 

<!-- For features only -->

<!-- If publishing, you must provide changelog details in the #changelog Slack channel. You will receive a follow-up PR comment or notification. -->

<!-- If not, write "no" or "do not publish to changelog" to explicitly opt-out of posting to #changelog. Removing this entire section will not prevent posting. -->

## 🤖 LLM context

I pinged the robot to fix an issue with tests not working due to updates to service account descriptions taking a while to propagate in google cloud. I didn't yet understand the eventual consistency nature of description updates. The robot then decided to use a parameter in the request which not only was deprecated but didn't fix the tests. I ended up implementing a retry loop to account for some delay and that seemed to do the trick. In a real context, we wouldn't expect descriptions to be updated very frequently and very closely to execution time, this seemed more of a problem with test design (where we do want everything to happen in quick succession so we can test quickly).

I did also ping the robot to format the integration modal and it added some lists. I am not good enough at frontend stuff to decide if it's good, so I'll happily accept comments to redo it (with pointers). I did rewrite all the text parts, so that's all human made (had to remove em-dashes). 

Anyways, all the bugs here are human made. 

<!-- If an LLM agent co-authored or authored this PR, uncomment this section and leave any relevant context about the session, tools used, link to the session, or anything else that may help reviewers. -->

title: chore: remove --invalidate-first from hypercache warm commands
body:
## Problem

We've made recent changes to focus on pre-calculating as much of the `/flags` request processing as possible such as dependency graphs, cohort definitions, etc. This makes the old Rust code that resolved dependencies as dead code if the cache entry exists. We want to remove this dead code and require that the cache entries exist.

With that in mind, the `--invalidate-first` flag on HyperCache warm commands becomes a big foot gun. Invalidating the cache, then warming the cache would break `/flags`. Also, its unnecessary. The warm operation overwrites existing cache entries, making pre-invalidation redundant. Removing it simplifies the command interface, the base command class, and the `hypercache_manager` module.

## Changes

- Remove `--invalidate-first` argument from `_base_hypercache_command.py` and all three warm commands
- Remove `invalidate_all_caches()` function and `HYPERCACHE_INVALIDATION_COUNTER` metric from `hypercache_manager.py`
- Remove `_confirm_invalidate()` method from the base command
- Remove `invalidate_first` parameter from `run_warm()` and `warm_caches()`
- Remove associated tests
- Update documentation in `hypercache-system.md`

## How did you test this code?

Existing tests continue to pass after removing the invalidation-related tests and parameters.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: haacked

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Removes the `--invalidate-first` option from HyperCache warming workflows to prevent accidental cache invalidation that could break `/flags`, simplifying the management commands and the HyperCache management layer.

**Changes:**
- Removed `--invalidate-first` CLI option and confirmation flow from the shared warm-command framework and all warm commands.
- Removed full-cache invalidation implementation + associated Prometheus metric from `hypercache_manager`.
- Deleted invalidation-related tests and updated internal docs/task docstrings to match the new command usage.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| posthog/tasks/team_metadata.py | Updates task docstring to remove `--invalidate-first` from warm command guidance. |
| posthog/tasks/feature_flags.py | Updates task docstring to remove `--invalidate-first` from warm command guidance. |
| posthog/storage/test/test_hypercache_manager.py | Removes invalidation tests and import; keeps stats/warm coverage. |
| posthog/storage/hypercache_manager.py | Deletes `invalidate_all_caches` + invalidation metric; removes warm invalidation path and parameter. |
| posthog/models/feature_flag/test/test_flags_cache.py | Removes warm-command invalidation tests tied to the deleted flag/confirmation flow. |
| posthog/management/commands/warm_team_metadata_cache.py | Drops reading/passing `invalidate_first`; removes usage docs mentioning it. |
| posthog/management/commands/warm_flags_cache.py | Drops reading/passing `invalidate_first`; removes usage docs mentioning it. |
| posthog/management/commands/warm_flag_definitions_cache.py | Drops reading/passing `invalidate_first`; removes usage docs mentioning it. |
| posthog/management/commands/_base_hypercache_command.py | Removes `--invalidate-first` arg, confirmation prompt method

commits.message:
Remove --invalidate-first from warm cache commands

The /flags service requires cache entries to function correctly. The
--invalidate-first option on warm commands deletes all cache entries
before re-warming, creating a dangerous window where the service has
no cache and fails. Removed the option from all warm_* commands and
deleted the now-unused invalidate_all_caches() function.

title: fix(sessions): use range-based chunking for distinct_id in experimental backfill
body:
## Problem

The experimental sessions backfill chunks events by `cityHash64(distinct_id) % N`, but the events table ORDER BY includes `cityHash64(distinct_id)` as a sorted column:

```sql
ORDER BY (team_id, toDate(timestamp), event, cityHash64(distinct_id), cityHash64(uuid))
```

With modulo, matching values are uniformly scattered across the entire UInt64 range, so ClickHouse cannot use the primary index to skip granules — every chunk reads essentially all the data and filters at scan time. With 64 chunks this means ~64x total I/O over the source table.

## Changes

Switches `distinct_id_chunks` from modulo to contiguous range-based chunking. Each chunk now targets a contiguous slice of the `cityHash64(distinct_id)` space (e.g. `>= low AND < high`), which aligns with the physical sort order so ClickHouse can skip granules outside the range. Each chunk reads ~1/Nth of the data instead of all of it.

`team_id_chunks` is left as modulo — we don't know the distribution of rows across teams.

## How did you test this code?

Existing snapshot tests updated and passing — they confirm the generated SQL uses range predicates for `distinct_id_chunks` and still uses modulo for `team_id_chunks`.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code (Opus). The idea and rationale came from a discussion about ClickHouse granule skipping efficiency.
author: robbie-c

commits.message:
fix(sessions): use range-based chunking for distinct_id in experimental backfill

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: store chunk_where_fn result in local variable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: skip redundant >= 0 condition for first chunk

cityHash64 returns UInt64, so >= 0 is always true — only emit
the upper bound for chunk 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(insights): skip redundant /insight_variables API calls on dashboard scroll
body:
## Problem

Each SQL insight card on a dashboard mounts its own keyed `variablesLogic` instance when it scrolls into view. Each instance's `afterMount` explicitly calls `getVariables()`, hitting `/api/environments/:team_id/insight_variables/` every time, even though the data lives in the shared singleton `variableDataLogic`. Scrolling a dashboard with many SQL tiles fires dozens of redundant identical requests.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a85679fa-8d0b-40ba-bd5e-9401612398c7

Dashboard in the video: https://us.posthog.com/project/2/dashboard/97140

When `variableDataLogic` was migrated from `loaders` to `lazyLoaders` in #32534, the `afterMount` was correctly removed from `variableDataLogic` (since `lazyLoaders` auto-fetches on first selector access). But `variablesLogic` still had its own `afterMount` calling `getVariables()`. These explicit calls bypass the lazy loader's built-in dedup, so every mounted instance triggers a separate fetch.

## Changes

Remove the `afterMount` from `variablesLogic` entirely and let `lazyLoaders` handle the initial fetch as intended. The lazy loader on `variableDataLogic` already ensures variables are fetched exactly once on first access. 
Explicit`getVariables()` calls from `variableModalLogic` (after variable create/update) are unaffected.

## How did you test this code?

- Manual verification: open a dashboard with multiple SQL insight tiles, observe the network tab while scrolling 

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Opus 4.6. The fix was identified by tracing the mount lifecycle from `InsightCard.tsx` (useInView) → `DataVisualization.tsx` (BindLogic variablesLogic) → `variablesLogic.ts` (afterMount) → `variableDataLogic.ts` (API call).
author: andyzzhao

reviews.body: nice catch

commits.message:
fix(insights): skip redundant /insight_variables API calls on dashboard scroll

Each SQL insight card on a dashboard mounts its own variablesLogic instance
when it scrolls into view, unconditionally fetching the same global list of
insight variables. This adds a variablesLoaded flag to the shared
variableDataLogic so subsequent mounts skip the fetch if data was already
loaded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(insights): set initialVariablesLoaded when no URL variables present

When there are no variables in the URL on dashboard mount,
initialVariablesLoaded was never set to true. This caused the
urlVariables selector to return {} even after the user changed a
variable value via the UI (which updates the URL), making the
override silently ignored.

Previously the removed afterMount in variablesLogic would trigger
getVariablesSuccess which set this flag, but without it the flag
stayed false indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Context

The `PropertyFilter` struct gained a `compiled_regex: Option<CompiledRegex>` field in [#52184](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52184). The test added by [#51795](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/51795) (`test_parallel_path_no_person_with_groups`) was developed against an older version of the struct and was merged without the new required field, causing a compile-time error (E0063) that broke both the **Lint Rust services** and **Test Rust** CI jobs on master.

Fixes: `rust/feature-flags/src/flags/test_flag_matching.rs:9118`

title: fix(cohorts):  self-heal stuck static cohorts and fix OOM in CH→PG sync
body:
## Problem

When a user duplicates a large dynamic cohort as a static cohort, the `insert_cohort_from_query` Celery task populates ClickHouse and then syncs to Postgres. For large cohorts (1M+ people), the PG sync step in `insert_cohort_people_into_pg` loads **all** person UUIDs from ClickHouse into a Python list at once, which OOMs the worker. Since SIGKILL bypasses Python's `finally` block, `is_calculating` is never reset to `False`.

Worse, `reset_stuck_cohorts` explicitly excludes static cohorts, so stuck static cohorts are never auto-recovered — they stay stuck forever with `is_calculating=True`, `errors_calculating=0`, `last_calculation=null`, and no people visible in the UI despite the data existing in ClickHouse.

## Changes

**Fix the OOM** — `insert_cohort_people_into_pg` now uses cursor-based pagination (`WHERE person_id > last_id ORDER BY person_id LIMIT 10000`) via a new `CursorBatchIterator` instead of loading all rows into memory. Peak memory drops from O(n) to O(page_size).

**Make the task resumable** — `insert_cohort_from_query` now checks if ClickHouse already has rows for the cohort (from a previous partial run) and skips the CH insert to avoid duplicates from `generateUUIDv4()`. The PG sync is already resumable since `_insert_users_list_with_batching` deduplicates against existing rows each batch.

**Self-healing for stuck static cohorts** — `reset_stuck_cohorts` now also detects static cohorts with a `query` field that have been stuck for >1 hour. It resets `is_calculating`, bumps `errors_calculating`, and re-dispatches `insert_cohort_from_query`. Stops retrying after `MAX_ERRORS_CALCULATING` (20) failures.

**Move task to correct queue** — `insert_cohort_from_query` now runs on the `long_running` queue instead of the default `celery` queue.

**Add logging** — `insert_cohort_from_query` now logs at each step (task start, CH complete/skipped, PG complete, task finished) so stuck tasks are actually diagnosable in Grafana.

## How did you test this code?

- Added unit tests for `CursorBatchIterator` (basic pagination, empty, single batch, validation, uneven last batch)
- Updated `test_reset_stuck_cohorts` to verify static cohorts are now also reset
- Added `test_reset_stuck_static_cohorts_retriggers_query` to verify the self-healing re-dispatches the task
- Added `test_reset_stuck_static_cohorts_stops_after_max_errors` to verify it stops retrying after 20 failures
- Verified existing `test_insert_cohort_from_query_count_updated_on_exception` still passes with the new CH count check

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was co-authored with an LLM agent while investigating a customer support issue where a static cohort created from a dynamic cohort was stuck in `is_calculating` state with 0 users visible despite data existing in ClickHouse.
author: dmarticus

reviews.body:
Nice work, liked the direction to fix this.

I think the logs could be useful too.

Couple of inline comments, but none blocking.

commits.message:
fix: self-heal stuck static cohorts and fix OOM in CH→PG sync

Static cohorts created from dynamic cohorts (via HogQL query) could get
permanently stuck in is_calculating=True if the Celery worker was killed
during the PG sync step. The PG sync loaded all person UUIDs from
ClickHouse into memory at once, causing OOM for large cohorts.

fix: make static cohort CH insert idempotent instead of skipping

The previous approach checked if ANY rows existed for the cohort in CH
and skipped the entire insert. This broke the legitimate use case of
adding more people to an existing static cohort via a query (e.g., CSV
upload first, then PATCH with query).

Instead, make the INSERT idempotent by excluding already-existing
person_ids. This handles both cases:
- Retry after OOM: query results already in CH → nothing new inserted
- Add more people via query: only new person_ids get inserted

The subquery is efficient because person_static_cohort has a primary
key on (team_id, cohort_id, person_id, id).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat(django): personhog client migration for person reads
body:
## Problem

Django ORM reads against the persons database need to be migrated to the personhog gRPC service so we can decouple Django from the persons DB. This PR migrates the remaining person-by-UUID, person-by-distinct-ID, and person-by-PK read paths.

## Changes

### Core routing layer (`posthog/models/person/util.py`)

New dual-read helpers that all share a common `_personhog_routed` pattern (gate check → gRPC call → metrics → ORM fallback on error):

- `get_person_by_uuid` — single person lookup by UUID
- `get_person_by_distinct_id` — single person lookup by distinct ID
- `get_person_by_id` — single person lookup by integer PK
- `get_person_by_pk_or_uuid` — dispatches to UUID or PK lookup based on key format
- `validate_person_uuids_exist` — batch UUID existence check
- `get_persons_mapped_by_distinct_id` — distinct_id → Person mapping (optimized: no follow-up distinct ID fetch)

### Migrated callsites

| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| `api/person.py` | `delete_property` person lookup → `get_person_by_pk_or_uuid` |
| `api/cohort.py` | UUID validation in `_handle_static` and `add_persons_to_static_cohort` → `validate_person_uuids_exist` |
| `models/cohort/cohort.py` | `remove_user_by_uuid` person lookup → `get_person_by_uuid`; `_get_uuids_for_distinct_ids_batch` → `get_persons_by_distinct_ids` (preserves lightweight `values_list` ORM fallback) |
| `hogql_queries/events_query_runner.py` | personId filtering → `get_person_by_pk_or_uuid` |
| `hogql_queries/sessions_query_runner.py` | personId filtering → `get_person_by_pk_or_uuid` |
| `models/event/query_event_list.py` | `_filter_by_person_id_or_distinct_id` → `get_person_by_uuid` |
| `models/person/point_in_time_properties.py` | `get_person_and_distinct_ids_for_identifier` → `get_person_by_distinct_id` / `get_person_by_uuid` |
| `session_recordings/session_recording_api.py` | Recording list person loading → `get_persons_mapped_by_distinct_id` (preserves lightweight ORM path that only sets `_distinct_ids = [single_id]`) |
| `queries/actor_base_query.py` | `get_people` → inline personhog routing with `_fetch_people_via_personhog` |
| `products/conversations/backend/api/tickets.py` | Ticket person data → `get_persons_by_distinct_ids` |

### Personhog gate middleware (`personhog_client/gate.py` + `middleware.py`)

`PersonHogGateMiddleware` pins the `use_personhog()` decision for the lifetime of an HTTP request. The first call rolls the dice; all subsequent calls on the same thread reuse that decision. This guarantees all personhog-routed reads within a single request consistently use the same backend (all gRPC or all ORM), avoiding mixed-source reads during partial rollout.

## How did you test this code?

### Automated testing approach

1. **Routing tests** (`SimpleTestCase` + mocks + `@parameterized.expand`) — verify gate check, ORM fallback on gRPC error, and metrics counter labels for each routed function. No DB required.

2. **Personhog internal logic tests** (`SimpleTestCase` + `fake_personhog_client()`) — exercise the real proto → model conversion pipeline via a fake gRPC client. Cover happy path, not-found, team mismatch filtering, and deduplication. No DB required.

3. **Dual-path integration tests** (`@parameterized_class` + `PersonhogTestMixin` + `BaseTest`) — run the same test body against both ORM (`personhog=False`) and personhog (`personhog=True`) paths, verifying both produce identical results end-to-end with a real DB.

`PersonhogTestMixin` (`test_helpers.py`) provides `_seed_person()` which creates a real DB record AND seeds the fake personhog client, plus assertion helpers that are no-ops on the ORM path.

### Local development testing

Exercised the following code paths through the respective endpoints:

- `POST /api/environments/1/query/` (EventsQuery with personId) → `events_query_runner.py` → `get_person_by_uuid()`
- `POST /api/environments/1/query/` (SessionsQuery with personId) → `sessions_query_runner.py` → `get_person_by_uuid()`
- `GET /api/environments/1/events/?person_id=<uuid>` → `query_event_list.py` → `get_person_by_uuid()`
- `POST /api/environments/1/persons/<uuid>/delete_property/` → `api/person.py` → `get_person_by_uuid()`
- `POST /api/projects/1/cohorts/` (with `_create_static_person_ids`) → `api/cohort.py` → `validate_person_uuids_exist()`
- `PATCH /api/projects/1/cohorts/<id>/add_persons_to_static_cohort/` → `api/cohort.py` → `validate_person_uuids_exist()`
- `PATCH /api/projects/1/cohorts/<id>/remove_person_from_static_cohort/` → `cohort.py` → `get_person_by_uuid()`
- Static cohort CSV upload with distinct IDs → `cohort.py` → `get_persons_by_distinct_ids()`
- **Error handling**: brought personhog-router/personhog-replica down and verified ORM fallback still returned a person

All changes are behind the `use_personhog()` gate (controlled by `PERSONHOG_ROLLOUT_PERCENTAGE`). With the gate off (default), behavior is identical to before.

title: fix: skip coupons_overview API call for non-admin users
body:
## Problem

Member-level Vercel SSO users visiting the billing page trigger a `coupons_overview` API call. The billing service rejects this with 403 (requires admin), which PostHog doesn't handle gracefully - it throws a 500. 132 occurrences in the last 7 days.

## Changes

Skip the `loadCouponsOverview` API call in `couponLogic` when the user isn't an admin or owner. The logic already connects to `organizationLogic.isAdminOrOwner` - just needed a guard before the API call.

Members see the billing page normally, just without the coupons banner (which they can't act on anyway).

## How did you test this?

- [x] Verified `couponLogic` already has `isAdminOrOwner` from `organizationLogic`
- [x] `activeCoupons` selector returns `[]` when `couponsOverview` is null, so the UI handles it cleanly
- [x] The billing page loading spinner won't block since `couponsOverviewLoading` resolves immediately for non-admins

## Changelog

No - bug fix.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MattBro

commits.message:
fix: skip coupons_overview API call for non-admin users

Member-level Vercel SSO users visiting the billing page trigger a
coupons_overview call that the billing service rejects with 403,
causing a 500 error. Skip the call entirely for non-admin users
since they don't need to see coupon information.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle race condition with org data loading

Only skip the coupons API call when we know the user is a non-admin
(org loaded + membership level confirmed). If org hasn't loaded yet,
let the call through. Retry on loadCurrentOrganizationSuccess if
coupons weren't fetched.

title: fix: Wire up leadership into personhog-router
body:
## Problem

In leader mode, the `personhog-router` watches etcd for partition assignments but no component was responsible for computing and writing those assignments. The coordinator was defined in `personhog-coordination` but never wired into any running process, so partition assignment keys were never created in etcd even when pods were registered and ready.

## Changes

- Added coordinator config fields to `personhog-router` (`coordinator_lease_ttl`, `coordinator_keepalive_secs`, `coordinator_election_retry_secs`, `coordinator_rebalance_debounce_ms`) with helper methods
- Split the single `coordination` lifecycle component into two: `routing-table` (router registration + assignment/handoff watches) and `coordinator` (leader election + partition assignment via `JumpHashStrategy`)
- Each router-leader pod now runs both components; etcd leader election ensures only one instance actively computes assignments at a time

title: fix(llma): avoid column name collisions with non-llm insights
body:
## Problem

A customer reported that using a column named `sentiment` in an insight renders as `–` instead of showing the actual value. This is the same class of bug fixed in #44885, where the LLM Analytics `user` column renderer was colliding with user-defined columns.

The `productColumnRenderers` registry in `renderColumn.tsx` matches columns by name globally across all DataTable instances, with no query-type guard. The LLM Analytics `sentiment` renderer intercepts the column, tries to treat the row as an `LLMTrace`, fails to find a `.id` property, and falls back to `–`. The same issue applies to the `tools` column.

## Changes

Renames `sentiment` → `__llm_sentiment` and `tools` → `__llm_tools` in the column renderer registry, matching the `__llm_person` pattern from #44885.

## How did you test this code?

I haven't tested this manually. The change is a straightforward rename following an established pattern. To verify:
- Open LLM analytics traces — confirm sentiment and tools columns still render correctly
- Create a HogQL insight with a column named `sentiment` or `tools` — confirm the actual values are displayed instead of `–`

## Publish to changelog?
No

## Docs update
No

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Code.

title: feat(data-warehouse): add pagination to ExternalDataJob list in admin
body:
## Problem

The Django admin page for `ExternalDataSchema` previously showed only the last 50 jobs with no pagination, making it difficult to browse older job history.

## Changes

- Replace the hardcoded 50-job limit with Django's `Paginator` (20 jobs per page)
- Add First/Previous/Next/Last pagination controls to the template
- Display total job count in the section header
- Order jobs by `created_at` descending (newest first)

**Files changed:**
- `posthog/admin/admins/external_data_schema_admin.py` — pagination logic in `change_view`
- `posthog/templates/admin/data_warehouse/externaldataschema/change_form.html` — pagination UI controls

## How did you test this code?

🤖 This was authored by an AI agent and has not been manually tested. The changes are straightforward Django admin pagination using built-in `Paginator` — no new dependencies or complex logic.

no

title: feat: Improve success / failure counting in the SLO dashboard
body:
## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

The total count is not correct in the SLO dashboard, it should count the `started` events, not the amount of completed events (as this leaves a gap if things are "stuck"). 

This PR updates the 3 critical paths (success rate, burn rate and the volume table) to correctly count the SLO, which is:

- Total => `started` events
- Failure => `started` events - `completed.outcoume.success` events
- Stuck/lost => `started` events - `completed` events

## Changes

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->

title: feat(logs): track user actions for logs alerts and views
body:
## Problem

As we ramp up our AI tooling for APM/Logs we'll need to see usage broken down by usage source (user, api. mcp e.t.c.)

## Changes

Added comprehensive user action tracking across the logs product:

**Logs API endpoints:**

- Track `logs query executed` with query metadata (results count, filters, pagination status)
- Track `logs sparkline queried` with breakdown and filter information
- Track `logs has_logs checked` with availability status
- Track `logs export requested` with export details and query parameters

**Logs alerts management:**

- Track `logs alert created`, `logs alert updated`, and `logs alert deleted` with alert configuration details (threshold, operator, window, enabled status)

**Logs views management:**

- Track `logs view created`, `logs view updated`, and `logs view deleted` with view metadata (name, pinned status, filter presence)

**Logs explain feature:**

- Track `logs explain requested` with cache status and force refresh flag

All tracking includes relevant context properties to enable detailed usage analysis and breakdown by different usage sources.

## How did you test this code?

Code review and validation of tracking implementation patterns. The tracking follows existing PostHog conventions using `report_user_action` with appropriate event names and property structures.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: jonmcwest

title: fix(workflows): Remove SES Send/Delivery webhook metrics to prevent triple counting email_sent
body: ## Problem Every email sent through workflows is counted 3x in the "Emails sent" metric. This happens because `email_sent` is recorded from three sources: once synchronously when the email is sent (`email.service.ts`), once from the SES `Send` webhook, and once from the SES `Delivery` webhook. A workflow that sends 6 emails shows 18 in the metrics. ## Changes - Removed `Send` and `Delivery` from the SES webhook event-to-metric mapping. `email_sent` is already recorded synchronously in `email.service.ts` when the email is sent, so the webhook events are redundant. - Added a guard to skip SES events that don't have a metric mapping (Send, Delivery, DeliveryDelay) instead of pushing undefined metric names. - Restored a NOTE comment about email client tracking prevention that was accidentally removed in a prior PR. ## How did you test this code? - Confirmed the 3x counting in production (6 emails showing as 18 sent) - All email tracking, SES webhook, and email service tests pass - Updated the Delivery event test to verify it no longer produces metrics ## Publish to changelog? No
author: dmarchuk

commits.message: fix(workflows): Remove SES Send/Delivery webhook metrics to prevent triple counting email_sent

body:

author: oliverb123

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds **Error Tracking** as a new “signals” source by emitting internal signals from the Rust error-tracking pipeline (cymbal) into the Django Signals backend, and extending the shared signal schemas accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Cymbal emits signals for error-tracking lifecycle events (issue created/reopened) and spike detection, using a new internal `SignalClient`.
- Django Signals backend adds an internal-only emit endpoint and extends source product/type choices for error tracking.
- Shared schema updates (Python + generated TS/JSON) add `ErrorTrackingSignalInput` to the signals discriminated union.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| rust/cymbal/src/types/mod.rs | Adds `Default` + extracts stacktrace rendering into `print_stacktrace()` for reuse. |
| rust/cymbal/src/stages/linking/issue.rs | Emits signals when issues are created or reopened. |
| rust/cymbal/src/stages/alerting/spike_detection.rs | Threads issue props into spike detection and emits spiking signals. |
| rust/cymbal/src/stages/alerting/spike_alert.rs | Collects one `OutputErrProps` per issue to feed spike detection/signals. |
| rust/cymbal/src/signals.rs | New internal HTTP client + request shape for emitting signals to Django. |
| rust/cymbal/src/lib.rs | Exposes the new `signals` module. |
| rust/cymbal/src/config.rs | Adds config for signals base URL + internal API secret. |
| rust/cymbal/src/app_context.rs | Initializes `MaybeSignalClient` and stores it in `AppContext`. |
| products/signals/backend/views.py | Adds `InternalSignalViewSet` with `InternalAPIAuthentication`. |
| products/signals/backend/models.py | Extends `SignalSourceConfig` choices and adds `is_source_enabled`. |
| products/signals/backend/migrations/max_migration.txt | Bumps max migration pointer. |
| products/s

Approving as I don't see any blockers (well, other than some linting checks red).
I haven't yet ran this locally! I've taken a cursory look at the pre-existing spike detection logic from error tracking - seems sound to me.
Pondered the ingestion side some more, I would love input from @PostHog/team-error-tracking on scale, but from my experience I do propose making the hot path colder

commits.message: fix(cymbal): set error tracking signal weights to 1.0

title: chore: remove unused sqlalchemy packages and fix bigquery source tests
body:
## Problem

We have several sqlalchemy-related packages pinned as direct dependencies (`sqlalchemy`, `sqlalchemy-bigquery`, `snowflake-sqlalchemy`) but nothing in the codebase imports them. `sqlalchemy` was originally added for `dlt`'s postgres data import source, but that code was converted to async and removed in April 2024 (`ec79157d`). The dependency pins were never cleaned up.

In the process, I noticed a couple of other issues:

- `google-cloud-bigquery-storage` was previously only pulled in as a transitive dependency of `sqlalchemy-bigquery[bqstorage]`, but it's directly imported in `posthog/temporal/data_imports/sources/bigquery/bigquery.py` — so it needs to be a direct dependency.
- The BigQuery source tests were also broken on master because `import_data_activity_sync` was recently made async but the tests were never updated.

## Changes

- Removed `sqlalchemy==2.0.38` (main and dev deps), `sqlalchemy-bigquery[bqstorage]==1.12.1`, and `snowflake-sqlalchemy==1.8.2` from `pyproject.toml`
- Added `google-cloud-bigquery-storage==2.32.0` as a direct dependency
- Removed corresponding entries from `.github/dependabot.yml`
- Fixed `test_bigquery_source.py` by making the tests async

(Note: `sqlalchemy` remains in the lockfile as a transitive dependency of `alembic`, `dlt`, and `langchain`.)

## How did you test this code?

Ran BigQuery source tests locally — all pass.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Opus 4.6.
author: rossgray

reviews.body: i'm a simple man: i see dependency removal, i approve

commits.message:
chore: remove unused sqlalchemy, sqlalchemy-bigquery, and snowflake-sqlalchemy

These packages have no Python imports in the codebase. sqlalchemy was
originally added for dlt's postgres source but that code was removed
in April 2024. The pins were never cleaned up.
chore: remove sqlalchemy from dev dependencies
