title: feat(feature-flags): add per-condition-set aggregation validation logic
body:
## Problem

With the per-condition-set `aggregation_group_type_index` field now available in all layers (Rust, Python types, migrated data), we need to update the validation logic to:
1. Normalize flag-level values to condition sets for backward compatibility
2. Derive the flag-level value from condition sets
3. Validate properties against each condition set's aggregation type

Note: Mixed aggregation types (some conditions person-based, some group-based) are explicitly rejected for now because the Rust evaluator doesn't yet support per-condition-set evaluation.

## Changes

### Validation logic in `FeatureFlagSerializer.validate_filters`:
- Distribute flag-level `aggregation_group_type_index` to condition sets that don't have one
- Derive flag-level value: if all conditions share the same value, use it; otherwise reject with clear error
- Validate each condition set's properties against its own aggregation type:
  - Person-aggregated: allow person, cohort, flag properties
  - Group-aggregated: allow only group properties matching the type index
- Also validate the new per-condition-set field with the existing type validation helpers

### Tests:
- `test_validation_mixed_aggregation_types_rejected` — verifies mixed conditions are rejected
- `test_per_condition_aggregation_normalization` — verifies flag-level value distributes to conditions
- `test_per_condition_aggregation_roundtrip` — verifies values persist through create/read
- Updated existing test expectations for new `aggregation_group_type_index` field in responses

## How did you test this code?

- Added comprehensive unit tests for the new validation paths
- Existing tests updated to expect the new field in API responses

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was co-authored with Claude. Part 4 of a 4-PR stack to add per-condition-set aggregation support to feature flags. The validation explicitly rejects mixed aggregation types until the Rust evaluator is updated to support them.
author: dmarticus

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Updates PostHog’s feature flag API validation to support the new per-condition-set `aggregation_group_type_index` field while keeping backward compatibility with the legacy flag-level field, and expands test coverage for these new validation paths. **Changes:** - Normalizes/derives `aggregation_group_type_index` between flag-level and per-condition-set fields and validates properties against each condition set’s aggregation type. - Rejects mixed aggregation types across condition sets (until the Rust evaluator supports per-condition-set evaluation). - Updates and adds unit tests to cover normalization, roundtrips, and mixed-type rejection, plus updates expected API responses to include the new field. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | posthog/api/feature_flag.py | Adds per-condition-set aggregation normalization/derivation in `validate_filters` and expands `$group_key` name resolution to consider condition-set aggregation indices. | | posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag.py | Updates existing expectations to include `aggregation_group_type_index` and adds new tests for mixed-type rejection and normalization/roundtrip behavior. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

I think the greptile comments are legit and blocking.
One additional suggestion, but it's really a nit.
Much better! A few suggestions inline, but nothing blocking.
LGTM. Nice work.

commits.message:
feat(feature-flags): add per-condition-set aggregation validation logic

- Normalize flag-level aggregation_group_type_index to condition sets
- Derive flag-level value from condition sets for backward compatibility
- Reject mixed aggregation types across condition sets (not yet supported)
- Validate group properties match condition set's group type
- Add tests for normalization, roundtrip, and mixed aggregation rejection

fix(feature-flags): address PR review feedback for per-condition aggregation

- Fix empty groups breaking PUT/PATCH with misleading "mixed aggregation"
  error by short-circuiting when condition_aggregations is empty
- Simplify has_any_person/group_aggregation checks to use resolved
  flag-level value directly instead of scanning condition sets
- Eliminate double Property construction using walrus operator
- Add project_id to GroupTypeMapping.objects.create in tests
- Remove stale 1051 migration file

fix(feature-flags): address haacked's review feedback

- Remove duplicate _validate_integer call (rebase artifact)
- Fold flag operator check into existing per-condition loop instead of
  a separate pass that reconstructs Property objects
- Use .exists() instead of .count() > 0 for Early Access Feature check

fix(feature-flags): fix mypy non-overlapping equality check

Use string literal "flag_evaluates_to" instead of PropertyOperator enum
value since Property.operator is typed as OperatorType (a Literal union
that doesn't include flag_evaluates_to).

fix(feature-flags): fix mypy by comparing against raw dict operator

Use prop_dict.get("operator") instead of prop.operator to avoid
mypy's non-overlapping equality check, since OperatorType Literal
doesn't include "flag_evaluates_to".

fix(feature-flags): handle explicit null in condition aggregation normalization

When a client sends aggregation_group_type_index: null on a condition
but sets a non-null value at flag level, the null should be overwritten
with the flag-level value rather than preserved and then propagated
back to overwrite the flag-level field.

fix(feature-flags): update test expectations for per-condition aggregation normalization

Tests across surveys, experiments, early access features, scheduled
changes, and org flag copy now expect the aggregation_group_type_index
field on each condition set and at the top-level filters.

fix(feature-flags): preserve backward compatibility for person-aggregated flags

Only set aggregation_group_type_index on condition sets and flag-level
filters when the value is non-null. This avoids adding new keys to
persisted JSON that would break frontend code using !== undefined
checks (relatedFeatureFlagsLogic, experimentLogic, featureFlagLogic,
filtersToQueryNode, etc.).

Revert test expectation changes for person-aggregated flags since
the field is no longer added when null.
chore(feature-flags): add TODO references for frontend null check follow-up (#52024)

title: fix(workflows): Show error toast instead of onboarding screen when workflow list fails to load

A customer reported this: on refresh they'd see the empty onboarding state, and only on a second refresh would their workflows appear. This is more in line with existing behavior when we fail to load. ## Changes - Added `hasLoadedWorkflows` reducer that only becomes `true` after a successful load. The "Create your first workflow" onboarding screen now requires a confirmed successful empty response, not just "not loading and no data" - Added `lemonToast.error` on `loadWorkflowsFailure` so the user knows something went wrong ### Screenshots **Before:** **After:** ## How did you test this code? title: ci(backend): increase turbo-discover timeout from 5 to 20 minutes
body:
<!-- mendral:session:slack:TSS5W8YQZ:C0A7SH71ETY:1774375172.891899 -->
## Summary

- Increases the `turbo-discover` job `timeout-minutes` from `5` to `20` in `ci-backend.yml`
- The 5-minute timeout is too tight for cold depot cache runs (e.g. after a cache bust) — pnpm setup alone can exceed 5 min, causing the job to be cancelled and triggering a false **Django Tests Pass** failure on master even when all 54 Django test jobs pass
- 20 minutes matches the Rust CI test job timeout and gives sufficient headroom for cold-cache pnpm installs

Fixes: https://app.mendral.com/insights/01KMGFQR59MQR8MQJTQ3W8NX18

author: mendral-app

commits.message:
ci(backend): increase turbo-discover timeout from 5 to 20 minutes

The 5-minute timeout is too tight for cold depot cache runs (e.g. after
a cache bust), where pnpm setup alone can exceed 5 minutes. This causes
the job to be cancelled, which triggers a false 'Django Tests Pass'
failure on master even when all Django tests pass.

Increasing to 20 minutes (matching the Rust CI test job timeout) gives
enough headroom for cold-cache pnpm installs while still bounding
runaway jobs.

Fixes: https://app.mendral.com/insights/01KMGFQR59MQR8MQJTQ3W8NX18

title: fix(cli,cymbal): create per-UUID ZIP for dSYM uploads
body:
## Problem

When a dSYM bundle contains multiple DWARF binaries (e.g. an Xcode debug dylib + app stub), the CLI zipped the entire bundle and shared that same blob across all UUIDs. On incremental rebuilds, stable UUIDs (like the app stub loader) got a new content hash whenever a sibling binary changed, causing `content_hash_mismatch` errors from the server.

## Changes

- **CLI (`cli/src/dsym/`)**: Each UUID now gets its own ZIP containing only its DWARF binary (+ optional source files), wrapped in the `symbol_data` container with Zstd compression. No more shared blob across UUIDs.
- **Cymbal (`rust/cymbal/src/symbol_store/apple.rs`)**: Updated to parse the new format — looks for a root-level `dwarf` file instead of scanning `Contents/Resources/DWARF/`. Removed raw ZIP fallback (requires `symbol_data` container).

**Breaking changes:**
- Existing symbol sets uploaded with the old format will need to be re-uploaded
- Cymbal no longer falls back to raw ZIP

## How did you test this code?

Tested by building the PostHog iOS example app and uploading dSYMs end-to-end:
1. First upload succeeded (3 symbol sets uploaded)
2. Made a source code string change, rebuilt, uploaded again — no `content_hash_mismatch` (1 new UUID uploaded, 2 skipped as already present with matching hash)

Manual verification still needed:
- Cymbal symbolicating crash frames using the new format
- Source bundling (`--include-source`) with per-UUID ZIPs

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: hpouillot

commits.message:
fix(cli,cymbal): create per-UUID ZIP for dSYM uploads

When a dSYM bundle contains multiple DWARF binaries (e.g. an app stub
and a debug dylib), the CLI previously zipped the entire bundle and
shared that same blob across all UUIDs. This caused content_hash_mismatch
errors on the server because stable UUIDs (like the app stub) got a new
hash whenever a sibling binary changed.

Each UUID now gets its own ZIP containing only its DWARF binary, wrapped
in the symbol_data container with Zstd compression. Cymbal is updated to
parse the new format (looks for a root-level "dwarf" file instead of
scanning the dSYM directory structure).

fix(cymbal): keep backward compatibility with old dSYM format

Handle both old format (raw ZIP with full dSYM bundle layout) and new
format (symbol_data wrapper with flat DWARF binary). Also fix path
extraction from dwarfdump output when paths contain spaces.
fix(cli): apply cargo fmt to dsym module

title: fix: make Vercel installation DELETE idempotent
body:
## Problem

When a customer's `OrganizationIntegration` record is already deleted (or was never created), Vercel's `DELETE /api/vercel/v1/installations/{id}` call returns 500. This happens because:

1. US doesn't find the installation, proxies to EU
2. EU doesn't find it either, raises `NotFound` from `_handle_missing_installation` inside the region proxy mixin's `dispatch` override
3. Since this runs before `super().dispatch()`, DRF's exception handler never catches it - Django treats it as an unhandled 500
4. Vercel shows "Uninstall Paused" to the customer and retries indefinitely

Reported via Stripe support for integration `icfg_JgQpHxoXoVHr2smafOPXihWH`.

## Changes

- **`vercel_region_proxy_mixin.py`**: Allow DELETE requests through even when installation isn't found (same as PUT/upsert)
- **`vercel_installation.py`**: Catch `NotFound` in `destroy` and return `{"finalized": true}` with 200 - a DELETE for something already gone is success
- **`test_vercel_installation.py`**: Added test for idempotent delete

## How did you test this?

- [x] `pytest ee/api/vercel/test/test_vercel_installation.py -v` - all 8 tests pass including new idempotent delete test
- [ ] Manual: will verify with ngrok once deployed

## Changelog

No - bug fix for existing integration behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MattBro

reviews.body: Do we have to handle cache invalidation like the bot's suggesting?

commits.message:
fix: make Vercel installation DELETE idempotent

When a customer's OrganizationIntegration record is already deleted,
Vercel's DELETE call would 500 because the NotFound exception was raised
from the region proxy mixin's dispatch (before DRF's exception handler),
causing Django to treat it as an unhandled error. Vercel then shows
Uninstall Paused and retries indefinitely.

Now DELETE requests pass through the proxy mixin even when the
installation is not found, and the destroy viewset method catches
NotFound and returns 200 with finalized: true.

fix: invalidate installation cache after successful delete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: make Vercel connect flow work cross-region
body:
## Problem

The "Link Existing Account" flow in the Vercel Marketplace is broken for EU users. They see "Session expired or invalid".

The connect callback runs on US (Vercel's redirect URL points to `us.posthog.com`). It stores session data in **US Redis cache** and sets a signed **cookie on us.posthog.com**. When EU users get redirected to `eu.posthog.com` (where their project lives), neither the cache entry nor the cookie are available.

Reported in https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C043VJ93L3B/p1774248778460409 and https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/54108.

## Changes

Replace Redis cache + signed cookie session binding with a self-contained signed token:

- The callback encodes all session data into a compressed, signed token using `django.core.signing.dumps` with `VERCEL_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_SECRET` as the key
- This key is shared between US and EU (verified in AWS Secrets Manager)
- The token is passed as a URL parameter - works on any region, auto-expires after 10 minutes
- No more Redis cache lookups, no more domain-specific cookies
- Net -161 lines

## How did you test this?

- [x] `pytest ee/api/vercel/test/test_vercel_connect.py -v` - all 36 tests pass
- [x] Verified token roundtrips correctly (272 chars, well under URL limits)
- [x] Verified wrong signing key is rejected
- [x] Confirmed `VERCEL_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_SECRET` matches between prod-us and prod-eu

## Changelog

No - bug fix for existing integration behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MattBro

commits.message:
fix: make Vercel connect flow work cross-region

Replace Redis cache + signed cookie session binding with a self-contained
signed token. The callback on US encodes session data into a compressed,
signed token (using VERCEL_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_SECRET which is shared
between regions). The token is passed as a URL parameter and can be
verified on any region without needing cache lookups or domain-specific
cookies.

This fixes Session expired or invalid errors for EU users trying to
link their existing PostHog account via the Vercel Marketplace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: encrypt session token and add replay prevention

Address review feedback:
- Use Fernet encryption instead of signing-only, so session data
  (including Vercel access_token) is not readable from the URL
- Add jti nonce with cache-based tracking to prevent token replay
- Simplify except clauses (SignatureExpired is subclass of BadSignature)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sort imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(hogql): handle column alias shadowing in property access
body:
## Problem

HogQL queries where a column alias shadows a table field e.g. `argMin(properties, timestamp) as properties` and then accesses a child `properties.foo` crash with a 500 instead of a user-facing error.

Reported: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/46099

## Changes

Catch NotImplementedError in the resolver's field chain resolution and surface it as a QueryError with a helpful message.

## How did you test this code?

Unit tests

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: georgemunyoro

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR improves HogQL’s resolver error handling for cases where a SELECT column alias shadows a table field name and a chained property access (e.g. `properties.foo`) would previously crash, by converting the internal failure into a user-facing `QueryError`. **Changes:** - Catch HogQL `NotImplementedError` during field-chain traversal and rethrow as a helpful `QueryError`. - Add a unit test covering alias shadowing with property access (`properties.foo`) to ensure a user-facing error is raised. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `posthog/hogql/resolver.py` | Wraps `get_child` chain resolution to convert internal `NotImplementedError` into an exposed `QueryError` with guidance. | | `posthog/hogql/test/test_resolver.py` | Adds regression test asserting the new error behavior and message hints. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: fix(hogql): handle column alias shadowing in property access

title: fix(remote-config): update on disable
body:
## Problem

The `HogFunction` signal only refreshed remote config when the saved instance was still enabled, so disabling a notification bar (`enabled=False`) skipped the refresh entirely. That left stale data in `config.js` until
  some unrelated refresh or the daily `sync_all_remote_configs` job in `posthog/tasks/scheduled.py:547`

## Changes

Site-app HogFunctions now trigger remote-config refresh on save and delete, including the disable path. 

## How did you test this code?

New tests in `posthog/models/test/test_remote_config.py`

no

title: feat(flags): support sub-1% rollout percentages in the UI
body:
## Problem

Large-scale customers need to roll out feature flags to less than 1% of users (e.g., 0.15%). The backend hashing algorithm already supports arbitrary float precision, but the UI was truncating to integers via `parseInt()` and `step={1}` on the slider, preventing sub-1% rollouts.

## Changes

- Updated the rollout percentage slider from `step={1}` to `step={0.01}` in release conditions
- Changed `parseInt()` to `parseFloat()` in both the release conditions and variant rollout percentage inputs
- Added `step={0.01}` to both text inputs so the browser stepper increments by 0.01
- Added a test case for decimal rollout percentages (0.01%, 0.15%, 33.33%)

No backend changes needed — the API already validates and stores floats.

## How did you test this code?

- Added unit test for decimal rollout percentages — passes
- Manual testing needed: verify slider and input accept decimal values on the feature flag edit page

## Publish to changelog?

Yes — feature flag rollout percentages now support sub-1% values (e.g., 0.15%) in the UI.

## Docs update

Feature flag rollout percentages now support decimal values (e.g., 0.15%) in the UI. The docs should reflect that rollout percentages can be set with up to two decimal places of precision, down to a minimum of 0.01%.
author: dmarticus

reviews.body: No showstoppers. Both bot comments are resolved and the diff addresses them: the test was refactored to use `it.each`, and `Math.round(raw * 100) / 100` enforces two-decimal-place precision in onChange handlers. Author is on the owning team for this area.
I'm questioning if the slider should also be updated though.

commits.message: feat(flags): support sub-1% rollout percentages in the UI

fix(flags): round rollout percentage to 2 decimal places and parameterize tests

Address CI review feedback: enforce 2 decimal place precision via
Math.round in both rollout inputs, and consolidate duplicate test
cases into a single parameterized it.each block.

fix(flags): resolve merge conflict with master's drag-and-drop changes

Re-apply sub-1% rollout percentage support (step=0.01, parseFloat,
rounding) on top of master's new drag-and-drop condition reordering.

fix(flags): use integer slider steps and hide native number spinners

Slider now moves in 1% increments to avoid floating point display
artifacts. Text input still accepts 0.01 step for sub-1% values.
Hide native number input spin buttons in LemonInput to prevent them
from crowding the displayed value.

title: fix(messaging): remove kafka key from precalculated person properties backfill
body:
## Problem

Currently, when backfilling precalculated person properties, events are emitted to Kafka with a key set to the distinct_id. This causes all events for a particular distinct_id to be routed to the same partition, which can lead to uneven partition distribution and potential performance issues.

## Changes

- Removed the `key` parameter from the Kafka producer call in the backfill workflow
- Events will now be distributed across partitions in round-robin fashion instead of being partitioned by distinct_id
author: gustavohstrassburger

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adjusts the Temporal workflow that backfills `precalculated_person_properties` so the emitted Kafka messages are no longer keyed by `distinct_id`, reducing the risk of partition hot-spotting during large backfills. **Changes:** - Removed the `key=event["distinct_id"]` argument when producing backfill events to the `clickhouse_precalculated_person_properties` Kafka topic. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(messaging): remove kafka key from precalculated person properties backfill

Remove the key parameter when emitting events to the precalculated_person_properties
Kafka topic during backfill to allow round-robin distribution across partitions
instead of partitioning by distinct_id.

title: feat(logs): add inline notification destinations for log alerts
body:
## Problem

Users need to be notified when log alert thresholds are breached - without notification destinations, alert rules are silent. This PR adds inline notification configuration (Slack + Webhook) to the log alert modal form, so users can set up "alert me when X happens via Y" in a single flow.

## Changes  
![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/e720b568-dded-48a8-af53-1bd0339f9580.png)

- **Type registration:** Added `'logs-alerting'` to `HogFunctionConfigurationContextId` and `'logs-alert-firing'` to `HogFunctionSubTemplateIdType` in `types.ts`
- **Sub-templates:** Registered `logs-alert-firing` in `sub-templates.ts` with Slack and Webhook variants (pre-filled message templates, feature-flagged behind `LOGS_ALERTING`)
- **Filter options:** Added `'logs-alerting'` case in `HogFunctionFiltersInternal.tsx` for the trigger event dropdown
- **Constants:** Added `LOGS_ALERT_FIRING_SUB_TEMPLATE_ID` and `LOGS_ALERT_FIRING_EVENT_ID` to `lib/constants.tsx`
- **`logsAlertUtils.ts`:** Helper functions for building HogFunction payloads (Slack/Webhook) and filter configs, following `alertUtils.ts` pattern
- **`logsAlertNotificationLogic.ts`:** Kea logic for managing pending/existing notification destinations — accumulates pending notifications in form state, creates HogFunctions on save via `Promise.allSettled`
- **`LogsAlertNotifications.tsx`:** Inline notification UI — existing destinations with edit/delete, pending destinations with remove, add-new panel with Slack channel picker or webhook URL input
- **Updated** **`LogsAlertForm`:** Added collapsible "Notifications" section rendering `LogsAlertNotifications`
- **Updated** **`logsAlertFormLogic`:** Two-step save — create/update alert, then create pending HogFunctions
- **Updated** **`LogsAlertingSection`:** `BindLogic` for notification logic at modal root, `alertFormChanged` (kea-forms convention) for Save button disabled state

## How did you test this code?

- **`logsAlertUtils.test.ts`** — unit tests for `buildLogsAlertFilterConfig` and `buildLogsAlertHogFunctionPayload` (Slack + Webhook variants)
- **`logsAlertNotificationLogic.test.ts`** — logic tests for pending notification CRUD, `createPendingHogFunctions` success/partial-failure, and `deleteExistingHogFunction`

👉 _Stay up-to-date with_ [_PostHog coding conventions_](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) _for a smoother review._

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

skip-inkeep-docs
author: jonmcwest

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds inline notification destinations (Slack + Webhook) to Logs Alerting so users can configure alert notifications directly within the log alert modal, backed by HogFunction “internal_destination” templates and Kea-managed pending/existing destination state.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `logs-alert-firing` HogFunction sub-templates (Slack/Webhook) and registers the associated context/event mappings.
- Adds `logsAlertUtils` + `logsAlertNotificationLogic` to build destination payloads, manage pending notifications, and create destinations on save.
- Extends the logs alert modal form/UI to display and manage notification destinations inline (including tests).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 12 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/logsAlertUtils.ts | Builds HogFunction filter config + payloads for Slack/Webhook log-alert notifications. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/logsAlertNotificationLogic.ts | Kea logic for listing existing destinations, staging pending ones, creating/deleting destinations. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/logsAlertFormLogic.ts | Saves alert, then triggers creation of pending notification destinations. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/LogsAlertingSection.tsx | Binds notification logic into the modal and updates Save disabled-state logic to account for pending notifications. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/LogsAlertNotifications.tsx | Inline UI for viewing existing destinations and adding/removing pending Slack/Webhook destinations. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/LogsAlertForm.tsx | Adds a “Notifications” collapsible section rendering inline notifications UI. |
| products/logs/frontend/components/LogsAlerting/__tests__/logsAlertUtils.tes

title: chore: add agent skill for modifying TaxonomicFilter
body:
## Problem

Agents modifying the TaxonomicFilter component frequently introduce bugs due to its complexity — three coordinating kea logics, virtualized lists, and a high blast radius across the app. There's no structured guidance for safely making changes.

## Changes

Adds a new agent skill (`modifying-taxonomic-filter`) with:
- **SKILL.md** — entry point with a pre-change checklist and architecture overview
- **references/architecture.md** — component hierarchy, kea logic roles, data flow, and key files
- **references/testing-patterns.md** — RTL testing workflow, rendering helpers, and example test patterns
- **references/common-pitfalls.md** — documented mistakes from prior changes (logic key mismatches, missing mock data, CSS specificity issues, etc.)

No production code changes — this is purely agent instruction content.

## How did you test this code?

Documentation-only change. Verified the skill is correctly discovered via the symlink in `.claude/skills/`.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

title: feat: (flags): optimize is_condition_match with zero-copy filtering
body:
## Problem

`is_condition_match()` performs two `iter().cloned().partition()` calls on every condition of every flag, cloning every `PropertyFilter` (which contains heap-allocated `String` and `serde_json::Value`) unnecessarily. All downstream consumers only need borrowed references.

_Note: see https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/52017_

## Changes

Replace the clone-partition pattern with zero-copy filtered iteration. Filters are now evaluated in a single pass by branching on `prop_type` (a cheap `Clone + PartialEq` enum compared by value). Flag-value and property filters short-circuit immediately on mismatch (in Vec order); cohort filters are deferred and batch-evaluated after the loop. The only allocation is a `Vec<&PropertyFilter>` for cohort filters when present.

- Make `apply_cohort_membership_logic` generic over `Borrow<PropertyFilter>` so it accepts both owned and borrowed filter slices
- Change `evaluate_cohort_filters` to take `&[&PropertyFilter]`
- Inline `match_flag_value_to_flag_filter` and `match_property` calls directly in the evaluation loop

## How did you test this code?

- All previous tests pass
- Clippy clean
- Criterion benchmark (see below)

### Criterion benchmarks (original vs optimized)

Per-flag `get_match` with varying filter counts:

| Filters per condition | Original | Optimized | Change |
|-----------------------|----------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | 747 ns | 587 ns | **-21%** |
| 3 | 1.16 us | 896 ns | **-23%** |
| 5 | 1.66 us | 1.19 us | **-28%** |
| 10 | 2.78 us | 1.90 us | **-32%** |
| 20 | 4.95 us | 3.32 us | **-33%** |

Per-flag `get_match` with varying condition counts (worst-case: only last condition matches):

| Conditions per flag | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---------------------|----------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | 1.19 us | 875 ns | **-26%** |
| 3 | 1.80 us | 915 ns | **-49%** |
| 5 | 2.37 us | 955 ns | **-60%** |
| 10 | 3.79 us | 1.03 us | **-73%** |
| 20 | 6.58 us | 1.17 us | **-82%** |

Rollout-only (no filters, baseline): 375 ns -> 375 ns (no change, as expected).

Realistic `/flags` request (mixed flag types):

| Flags | Original | Optimized | Change |
|-------|----------|-----------|--------|
| 10 | 16.2 us | 13.2 us | **-18%** |
| 50 | 282 us | 264 us | **-6%** |
| 100 | 1.065 ms | 1.032 ms | **-3%** |
| 200 | 4.34 ms | 4.27 ms | **-2%** |

The many_flags improvement diminishes at higher counts because 25% of those flags use regex operators (~70us each), which dominate total time and are unaffected by this change (follow up optimization).

### Production data validation

Queried `posthog_featureflag` to validate benchmark parameters against real usage:

**Filters per condition**: 55% of conditions are rollout-only (0 filters), 35% have 1 filter, ~10% have 2-3. Our benchmark covers the full range, though the dominant case is 0-1 filters.

**Conditions per flag**: 86% of flags have exactly 1 condition, 95% have at most 2. The dramatic multi-condition improvements (49-82%) apply to a long tail of ~5% of flags.

**Top teams by total filters**: Heaviest teams have 300-700 total filters evaluated per request (600-1,400 clones eliminated). PostHog's own team: 966 flags, 1,126 filters, 2,252 clones eliminated per request.

**Estimated real-world impact**: Weighted by production distribution, the improvement should be ~10-15% of total per-request flag evaluation CPU time (and ~35-40% of the property-matching portion specifically). The benefit is most significant on the SDK `/flags` hot path where properties are provided inline as overrides and no DB round-trip occurs.
author: matheus-vb

reviews.body: Nice optimization! One non-blocking suggestion inline.

## Pull request overview This PR optimizes feature-flag condition evaluation in the Rust `feature-flags` service by removing per-condition cloning/partitioning of `PropertyFilter` values and switching to a zero-copy, short-circuiting evaluation loop (while deferring cohort evaluation). **Changes:** - Replace clone+`partition()` filter separation with a single-pass loop that evaluates flag-value and property filters in-order and defers cohort filters. - Generalize cohort membership logic to accept borrowed filters (`Borrow`), enabling zero-copy call sites. - Update cohort evaluation plumbing to pass borrowed filter references (`&[&PropertyFilter]`) instead of owned/cloned filters. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_matching_utils.rs | Removes helper functions that required cloning filters; keeps flag-value matching utility for direct use. | | rust/feature-flags/src/flags/flag_matching.rs | Implements the single-pass, zero-copy filter evaluation path and updates cohort filter evaluation signature/usage. | | rust/feature-flags/src/cohorts/cohort_operations.rs | Makes `apply_cohort_membership_logic` generic over borrowed filters to support both owned and referenced inputs. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

add test pinning new vec order evaluation semantics

title: fix(cohorts): add $internal_or_test_user to taxonomy and fix person property label resolution
body: ## Problem After #51852, new organizations start with a cohort filtering on `$internal_or_test_user`. However: 1. This property wasn't in the taxonomy, so the cohort UI displayed the raw property name instead of a friendly label 2. Even after adding it to the taxonomy's `person_properties` group, the cohort criteria UI still showed the raw name because of a bug in `CohortTaxonomicField` The root cause: when cohort person property criteria are loaded from the API, they don't include an `event_type` field. `CohortTaxonomicField` looked up `criteria['event_type']` which was `undefined`, causing `PropertyKeyInfo` to fall back to the `EventProperties` taxonomy group — where person-only properties like `$internal_or_test_user` aren't found. ## Changes 1. **Taxonomy entry** — added `$internal_or_test_user` to `person_properties` with label "Internal or test user", description, and examples 2. **Bug fix** — in `CohortTaxonomicField`, fall back to the first entry in `taxonomicGroupTypes` when no explicit group type is stored in the criteria. This ensures person property labels resolve correctly for any cohort loaded from the API. ## How did you test this code? - All 114 existing cohort frontend tests pass - JSON validity verified - Agent-authored — manual UI verification recommended Screenshot from the meat pilot (Robbie): Note that this fixes email address too, here's a screenshot from prod ## Publish to changelog? No ## Docs update N/A ## 🤖 LLM context Co-authored with Claude Opus 4.6.
author: robbie-c

commits.message:
chore: add $internal_or_test_user to person properties taxonomy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cohorts): resolve person property labels in cohort criteria UI

When cohort criteria for person properties are loaded from the API,
they don't include an `event_type` field. CohortTaxonomicField
defaulted to looking up `criteria['event_type']` which was undefined,
causing PropertyKeyInfo to fall back to EventProperties group where
person-only properties like `$internal_or_test_user` aren't found.

Fall back to the first entry in `taxonomicGroupTypes` when no explicit
group type is stored in the criteria.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat(feature_flags): add skill for cleaning up stale feature flags
body:
## Problem

Feature flags accumulate over time — flags that are fully rolled out, never evaluated, or tied to completed experiments become tech debt. Users need a way to identify and safely clean these up, both in PostHog and in their codebase.

## Changes

Adds a new agent skill (`cleaning-up-stale-feature-flags`) in `products/feature_flags/skills/` that teaches agents how to:

- Identify stale flags using MCP tools (usage-based and configuration-based staleness)
- Assess each flag for experiment dependencies and other flag dependencies
- Present findings with recommendations (disable, delete, or keep)
- Safely disable/delete flags with user confirmation
- Generate tailored code cleanup instructions based on each flag's rollout state (fully rolled out, not rolled out, partial)

The skill uses PostHog MCP tools (`feature-flag-get-all`, `feature-flag-get-definition`, `update-feature-flag`, `delete-feature-flag`, `execute_sql`) rather than direct API calls, so it works across all distribution channels (PostHog Code, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.).

## How did you test this code?

- Synced the skill locally via `hogli sync:skill -- --name cleaning-up-stale-feature-flags` against a local PostHog instance with the MCP server running
- Verified the skill activates correctly and walks through the full workflow
- Ran `hogli build:skills` and `hogli lint:skills` to confirm the skill passes validation

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed — the skill is distributed automatically via CI after merge.

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code. The skill content was written iteratively — started from the existing code cleanup AI configuration used in the feature flags UI delete flow, then expanded into a full agent workflow.
author: dmarticus

reviews.body: Pure documentation additions — a new skill guide (SKILL.md) and a handbook tweak — with no production code, data model, or API contract changes. All bot-raised inline concerns are marked resolved and the current diff reflects those fixes (disable-before-delete in the example, bullet points instead of numbered steps).
Awesome

commits.message:
feat(feature_flags): add skill for cleaning up stale feature flags

Adds a new agent skill that guides users through identifying and safely
removing stale feature flags. The skill teaches agents to use PostHog
MCP tools to list flags, detect staleness (no evaluations in 30+ days
or fully rolled out with no conditions), check experiment dependencies,
and generate code cleanup instructions tailored to each flag's rollout
state.

fix(feature_flags): use active=STALE filter instead of manual staleness detection

The feature-flag-get-all MCP tool supports active: "STALE" which does
server-side staleness detection in a single request. No need for the
agent to craft SQL queries or check flags one by one.

fix(feature_flags): address PR review comments on stale flags skill

- Remove unreachable not_rolled_out sub-case (null last_called_at +
  not fully rolled out never surfaces as stale)
- Fix example to follow disable-first safety workflow before deleting
- Switch numbered steps to bullets in example interaction

chore(feature_flags): add posthog: prefix to MCP tool references

Aligns with the convention used by other skills (query-examples,
signals) where MCP tools are referenced with the posthog: namespace.

title: fix(cohorts): add update_fields guard to all cohort post_save signal handlers
body:
## Problem

When a cohort recalculates, `_safe_save_cohort_state` calls `self.save()` without `update_fields`, triggering every `post_save` signal handler. This causes unnecessary cache invalidations (feature flag cache, dependency cache, hog function recompilation) on every recalculation cycle, even though only bookkeeping fields like `is_calculating` and `last_calculation` changed.

## Changes

- Define `COHORT_RECALCULATION_FIELDS` frozenset (`is_calculating`, `last_calculation`, `errors_calculating`, `last_error_at`) as the set of fields touched during recalculation
- Pass `update_fields` to `self.save()` in `_safe_save_cohort_state` so Django's `post_save` signal includes the field list
- Add an early-return guard to all three `post_save` signal handlers (`dependencies.cohort_changed`, `local_evaluation.cohort_changed`, `hog_function.cohort_saved`) that skips processing when only recalculation fields were updated
- Refactor tests out of the factory class and add assertions verifying `update_fields` is passed correctly on both success and error paths

## How did you test this code?

Unit tests verify `update_fields` is passed to `save()` on both the success path and the error path. Existing tests for cohort dependency tracking, feature flag cache invalidation, and hog function recompilation continue to pass.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: haacked

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR reduces unnecessary downstream work (cache invalidations and hog function recompilation) triggered during cohort recalculation by ensuring recalculation-only saves include `update_fields`, and by teaching cohort-related `post_save` signal handlers to no-op when only bookkeeping fields changed. **Changes:** - Introduces `COHORT_RECALCULATION_FIELDS` (a frozenset of recalculation bookkeeping fields) and uses it as a shared guard. - Updates `Cohort._safe_save_cohort_state` to call `save(update_fields=...)` on both success and error paths (including retry). - Adds `update_fields` early-return guards to cohort `post_save` handlers (dependency cache, feature flag local evaluation cache, hog function recompilation), and refactors/adds unit tests to assert `update_fields` behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `posthog/models/cohort/cohort.py` | Defines `COHORT_RECALCULATION_FIELDS` and ensures `_safe_save_cohort_state` saves only recalculation fields via `update_fields`. | | `posthog/models/cohort/dependencies.py` | Skips dependency cache invalidation on recalculation-only cohort saves. | | `posthog/models/feature_flag/local_evaluation.py` | Skips feature-flag cache updates on recalculation-only cohort saves. | | `posthog/models/hog_functions/hog_function.py` | Skips hog function recompilation triggers on recalculation-only cohort saves. | | `posthog/tasks/test/test_calculate_cohort.py` | Refactors tests and adds assertions that `_safe_save_cohort_state` passes correct `update_fields` on both success and error paths. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more gu

A small inline suggestion.
Otherwise, LGTM.

commits.message:
fix(cohorts): prevent signal handlers from firing during recalculation

Save cohort state with explicit update_fields so that post_save signal
handlers (dependency cache rebuild, flag cache update, hog function
recompile) can detect and skip recalculation-only saves. Extracts a
shared is_cohort_recalculation_only_save helper and COHORT_RECALCULATION_FIELDS
constant, and adds tests to confirm no downstream signals fire on
_safe_save_cohort_state.
fix(cohorts): update _safe_save_cohort_state docstring to reflect actual behavior

title: fix(vercel): use correct webhook event type for deauthorization
body:
## Problem

The `DEAUTHORIZATION_EVENT` constant was `"integration.configuration-removed"` but Vercel sends `"integration-configuration.removed"` (hyphen vs dot). All deauthorization webhooks were falling through to the non-billing catch-all and being ignored.

## Changes

Updated `DEAUTHORIZATION_EVENT` to `"integration-configuration.removed"` to match what Vercel actually sends.

## How did you test this?

- [x] `pytest ee/api/vercel/test/test_vercel_webhooks.py -v` - all tests pass

## Changelog

No - bug fix for existing integration behavior.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: MattBro

reviews.body: rofl, this is a great typo

commits.message:
fix(vercel): use correct webhook event type for deauthorization

Vercel sends `integration-configuration.removed` but we were matching
against `integration.configuration-removed`. Every deauthorization
webhook was being silently ignored as a "non-billing event", meaning
Vercel disconnections never deleted the OrganizationIntegration record.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make Vercel connect flow work cross-region

Replace Redis cache + signed cookie session binding with a self-contained
signed token. The callback on US encodes session data into a compressed,
signed token (using VERCEL_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_SECRET which is shared
between regions). The token is passed as a URL parameter and can be
verified on any region without needing cache lookups or domain-specific
cookies.

This fixes Session expired or invalid errors for EU users trying to
link their existing PostHog account via the Vercel Marketplace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- https://us.posthog.com/project/2/error_tracking/019ce8a8-99be-79c1-94cd-481f1a83a9b2?timestamp=2026-03-24%2007%3A34%3A35.832000-07%3A00&searchQuery=offset%20overflow%20while%20concatenating%20arrays%2C%20consider%20casting%20input%20from%20%60string%60%20to%20%60large_string%60%20first

title: perf: Related groups query optimization
body:
## Problem

The related actors query for groups needs optimization to improve performance when querying multiple group types. The current implementation makes separate database queries for each group type, which can be inefficient for teams with many group type mappings.

Part of https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/40472

## Changes

- Added a new feature flag `optimized-related-groups-query` to A/B test the optimization
- Implemented `_query_related_groups_optimized()` method that uses a single query with `ARRAY JOIN` to fetch all related groups at once, instead of separate queries per group type
- Evaluate experiment's feature flag in the client and pass variant via request, so that we can target persons instead of orgs (reasoning [here](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C09BDQLM8KA/p1773930970041059))
- Fire `related actors loaded` event with request timings to be used in the experiment
- Added query tagging for monitoring test vs control performance

The optimized query uses ClickHouse's `ARRAY JOIN` with `arrayFilter` to process multiple group types in a single database round-trip, reducing the number of queries from N (number of group types) to 1.

## How did you test this code?

Added comprehensive test coverage for both control and optimized query paths:
- `TestRelatedPersonsQuery` tests the person-related functionality with both variants
- `TestRelatedGroupsQuery` tests the group-related functionality with both variants  
- Tests verify correct results, exclusion of unrelated/old data, and edge cases like teams with no group mappings
- Snapshot tests capture the actual SQL queries generated by both implementations
- Parameterized tests ensure both control and test variants produce identical results

Also tested manually in the UI locally, making sure the events were being fired as expected

## Publish to changelog?

No - this is an internal performance optimization behind a feature flag.

## Docs update

No documentation changes needed as this is an internal optimization.
author: arthurdedeus

title: chore(flags): add management command to invalidate team flags auth cache
body:
## Problem

When a team's auth tokens need to be re-validated by the Rust feature-flags service (e.g., after a security incident, token rotation, or debugging cache staleness), there is no way to force cache invalidation for all token types associated with a team. The signal handlers handle individual token changes, but bulk invalidation requires manual Redis operations.

## Changes

Adds a `invalidate_flags_auth_cache` Django management command and the underlying `invalidate_team_tokens()` method on `TokenAuthCache`.

- Collects token hashes from three sources: team secret tokens, project secret API keys, and personal API keys that have access to the team (scoped by team and org membership)
- Batch-deletes the corresponding Redis cache entries so the Rust service re-validates against Postgres on the next request
- Supports `--dry-run` to preview what would be invalidated without deleting
- Does not revoke tokens, only clears cache entries

```
python manage.py invalidate_flags_auth_cache --team-id 12345
python manage.py invalidate_flags_auth_cache --team-id 12345 --dry-run
```

## How did you test this code?

- 20 unit and integration tests across two test files covering all token types, PAK scope filtering (different team, different org, target team, empty scopes), dry-run behavior, error conditions, and count parity between dry-run and actual invalidation
- Manually ran the command against a local dev environment

no
author: haacked

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adds a Django management command to bulk-invalidate the Rust feature-flags service’s per-token auth cache for a given team, enabling forced re-validation against Postgres after incidents/rotations/debugging. **Changes:** - Added `invalidate_flags_auth_cache` management command with `--team-id` and `--dry-run`. - Implemented `TokenAuthCache.invalidate_team_tokens()` to collect token hashes from team secret tokens, project secret API keys, and personal API keys associated with the org/team scoping rules. - Added unit/integration tests covering the new invalidation behavior and command output/error cases. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | posthog/storage/team_access_cache.py | Adds `invalidate_team_tokens()` to collect and delete relevant Redis auth-token cache keys for a team. | | posthog/management/commands/invalidate_flags_auth_cache.py | New management command wrapper around `token_auth_cache.invalidate_team_tokens()`, with output formatting and error handling. | | posthog/storage/test/test_team_access_cache.py | Adds test coverage for `invalidate_team_tokens()` behavior across token types and scoping cases. | | posthog/management/commands/test/test_invalidate_flags_auth_cache.py | Adds command-level tests for invalidation, dry-run, and error scenarios. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(ci): deduplicate hogql-parser version reminder comments
body:
## Summary

Trying to clean up some bot noise in PRs; saw this bot post in my unrelated PR (https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/51670) and wanted to see if we could scope it down a bit!

- Remove `since_last_remote_commit: true` from `changed-files` in both `hogql-parser` release workflows — this compared against the last push instead of the PR base branch, causing false positives on unrelated PRs (e.g. #50117, #51670) when master received hogql_parser changes between pushes

- Deduplicate bot comments: check for an existing comment before posting, edit it in place on subsequent pushes
instead of creating a new one (PR #50353 accumulated 13 duplicate comments)

- Fix literal `\n` rendering in comment bodies by using `$'\n'` for real newlines

## Test plan

- [ ] Open a PR that touches `common/hogql_parser/` without bumping the version — should get exactly one comment per package (PyPI + npm), not duplicates on subsequent pushes
- [ ] Open a PR that only touches workflow files or unrelated code — should not get the comment

title: feat(cymbal): add typed frame resolution metrics
body:
## Problem

Cymbal lacked centralized metrics for frame resolution outcomes, making it hard to monitor resolution success rates and diagnose failure patterns (missing symbol sets, network errors, invalid data, etc.).

## Changes

- Store `Option<FrameError>` directly on `Frame` instead of separate `Option<String>` + `Option<&'static str>` fields — the typed error provides both the human-readable message (via `Display`) and a metric-friendly reason (via `metric_reason()`)
- Add `metric_reason()` methods to all resolution error enums (`JsResolveErr`, `HermesError`, `ProguardError`, `AppleError`, `FrameError`) returning categorized labels: `no_reference`, `no_symbol_set`, `symbol_not_found`, `network_error`, `invalid_data`
- Emit `cymbal_frame_resolved` and `cymbal_frame_not_resolved` counters with `lang` and `reason` labels in the centralized `RawFrame::resolve()` method
- Add `warn!` log on frame resolution failures with structured `lang`, `reason`, and `error` fields
- Add `Clone`, `PartialEq`, `Eq` derives to `FrameError` and all child error enums (+ `SymbolDataError`) so they can live on the `Frame` struct
- Custom serde for `resolve_failure` field: serializes `FrameError` as its `Display` string for backward-compatible JSON output

## How did you test this code?

- `cargo check -p cymbal --tests` passes
- All non-DB unit tests pass (57 passed, DB-dependent tests skip without local Postgres)
- Snapshot format unchanged (custom serializer preserves string representation)

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: hpouillot

reviews.body: I think a test is still failing but the approach looks great!

commits.message:
feat(cymbal): add typed frame resolution metrics with error categorization

Store FrameError directly on Frame instead of a string, enabling typed
metric reasons (no_reference, no_symbol_set, symbol_not_found,
network_error, invalid_data) without fragile string matching. Adds
cymbal_frame_resolved and cymbal_frame_not_resolved counters with lang
and reason labels, plus a warn log on resolution failures.
fix(cymbal): fix rustfmt and remove unused deserialize function
fix(cymbal): mark None branch as unreachable in frame_error_serde
fix(cymbal): use debug log for expected resolution failures, warn for unexpected

fix(cymbal): fix snapshot test and rustfmt for frame resolution metrics

Use raw JSON extraction in handles_missing_sourcemap test to avoid
losing resolve_failure during typed deserialization round-trip. Also
fix rustfmt formatting in frame_error_serde.
fix(cymbal): update snapshot for alphabetical field ordering

title: perf(dashboard): avoid spurious edit mode when clicking text card markdown
body:
## Problem

Clicking inside dashboard text tiles (TipTap readonly / LemonMarkdown) could still route through the "enter edit mode from card body" `mousedown` handler. The ignore list matched `p` and `[contenteditable="true"]`, but readonly ProseMirror uses `contenteditable="false"`, and many nodes (`li`, `span`, headings, etc.) are not `p`. That unintentionally called `setDashboardMode(Edit)` and forced a full-dashboard repaint.

React Scan / interaction timing showed **~23ms React render** and **~38ms non-render JS**, but **~1323ms from DOM commit to frame presented** — a compositor/GPU-heavy path consistent with switching the whole dashboard into edit mode, not with missing `memo()` on `TextCard`.

## Changes

- Extend the `closest()` selector in `DashboardItems.tsx` to include `.ProseMirror` and `.LemonMarkdown` so clicks on readonly rich text do not trigger edit mode from the body drag-handle path.

## How did you test this code?

Not manually tested in the browser (agent). Please verify: on a dashboard with "enter edit from edge" behavior, click list items, headings, and inline spans inside a text card — the dashboard should stay in view mode unless you use the intended affordances (e.g. edge overlay, Edit layout, shortcut).

## Publish to changelog?

no — internal performance fix

## Docs update

N/A
author: MattPua

reviews.body: Trivial CSS selector addition to prevent edit mode from triggering when clicking ProseMirror/LemonMarkdown elements. No logic changes, no data model impact, author is on the owning team.

commits.message: perf(dashboard): Improve perf on layout mode for text card elements

title: perf(dashboard): Memoize part of text card tiles for perf improvements with edit mode
body:
## Problem

Using React Scan, noticed some performance bugs caused by the text card.

## Changes

Memoized part of content for tiptap to avoid re-renders each time, and memoize the text card itself

## How did you test this code?

Locally.
## Publish to changelog?



No.

 ## 🤖 LLM context 

```

## What was going wrong

**React Compiler:** Off — only `@vitejs/plugin-react()` in `vite.config.ts`, no `babel-plugin-react-compiler`.

**Why “other” ≫ React (255ms vs 75ms):** `TextContent` uses TipTap’s `useEditor` via `useRichContentEditor`. TipTap’s `EditorInstanceManager.compareOptions` treats `content` as **reference equality** (see `useEditor.ts` in `@tiptap/react`). You passed `initialContent: markdownToTextCardDoc(text)` on **every** render, so the parsed doc was a **new object** even when `text` was unchanged. That made `compareOptions` fail and **`editor.setOptions`** run after commits — ProseMirror/DOM work that does **not** show up as React render time.

**Why 8 renders / 14ms on `TextContent`:** Parent churn still re-renders; memo + stable doc address the expensive path. If props are stable, `memo` skips the subtree.

## What we changed

In `TextCard.tsx`:

1. **`useMemo(() => markdownToTextCardDoc(text), [text])`** so `content` only changes when `text` changes.
2. **`useEffect` deps** use that stable `initialDoc` instead of re-parsing in the effect body.
3. **`memo(TextContentImpl)`** so identical `text` / `className` / `closeDetails` do not re-run TipTap when a parent re-renders.

```39:66:frontend/src/lib/components/Cards/TextCard/TextCard.tsx
function TextContentImpl({ text, closeDetails, className }: TextCardBodyProps): JSX.Element {
    // TipTap's useEditor compares `content` by reference; a fresh JSON object each render forces
    // setOptions → expensive ProseMirror work (shows up as "other" time, not React render time).
    const initialDoc = useMemo(() => markdownToTextCardDoc(text), [text])

    const editor = useRichContentEditor({
        extensions: TEXT_CARD_MARKDOWN_READONLY_EXTENSIONS,
        initialContent: initialDoc,
        disabled: true,
    })

    useEffect(() => {
        if (!editor) {
            return
        }

        editor.commands.setContent(initialDoc, { emitUpdate: false })
    }, [editor, initialDoc])
    // ...
}

export const TextContent = memo(TextContentImpl)
TextContent.displayName = 'TextContent'
```

`TextCard.test.tsx` passes.

## What you should do next

Repeat the same interaction and check React Scan’s Optimize tab. You want:

- Lower **other** time (main signal for this fix).
- Fewer unnecessary `TextContent` renders if the parent was the only thing updating.

If **`closeDetails` is a new inline function every render** from a parent, `memo` will still bail — then stabilize that callback or use a custom `memo` comparator. Paste **Formatted Data** from the same interaction so we can confirm it matches this path.

**If it’s still slow:** profile whether other call sites of `useRichContentEditor` pass unstable `content` / options the same way, or whether another heavy hook (e.g. `useEditorState`) is involved.

---

**Sources:** `frontend/vite.config.ts` (React plugin), `@tiptap/react` `src/useEditor.ts` (`EditorInstanceManager.compareOptions`, `onRender` with `deps.length === 0`), `frontend/src/lib/components/RichContentEditor/index.tsx`, `frontend/src/lib/components/Cards/TextCard/TextCard.tsx`.
```
author: MattPua

reviews.body: Trivial perf improvement — memoizes markdown parsing and wraps the component in React.memo. The unresolved bot comment about unstable `closeDetails` references is a valid optimization caveat but not a showstopper; the change is still safe and beneficial in the primary use case.

commits.message: perf(dashboard): Memoize part of text card tiles for performance improvements with edit mode

Unit tests

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Expands HogQL’s “duck/postgres” dialect support to cover remaining DuckDB/Postgres-like SQL constructs, particularly around PIVOT, `GROUP BY ALL`, function-call `ORDER BY`, and richer type-cast type expressions.

**Changes:**
- Add PIVOT AST nodes + parsing, printing, and type-resolution support (postgres dialect only).
- Support `ORDER BY` inside function calls and print/resolve it appropriately.
- Extend parsing/printing for `GROUP BY ALL` and additional CAST type syntaxes (arrays + complex types).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 23 out of 30 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| posthog/hogql/ast.py | Adds `PivotExpr`/`PivotColumn` nodes, extends `Call` with `order_by`, and updates join table union. |
| posthog/hogql/parser.py | Parses `GROUP BY ALL`, function-call `ORDER BY`, array/complex column types, and PIVOT table expressions. |
| posthog/hogql/visitor.py | Traversal + cloning support for new pivot nodes and `Call.order_by`. |
| posthog/hogql/resolver.py | Resolves PIVOT expressions (postgres dialect only) and integrates PIVOT into join resolution. |
| posthog/hogql/printer/base.py | Prints `GROUP BY ALL`, PIVOT, function-call `ORDER BY`, and prints `ExpressionFieldType`. |
| posthog/hogql/printer/postgres.py | Adds validation/printing support for function-call `ORDER BY` in postgres dialect. |
| posthog/hogql/printer/postgres_functions.py | Updates postgres passthrough function allowlist (adds `year`). |
| posthog/hogql/test/_test_parser.py | Parser coverage for function-call `ORDER BY`, `GROUP BY ALL`, PIVOT, and additional CAST type syntaxes. |
| posthog/hogql/test/test_resolver.py | Resolver coverage for PIVOT success cases + error cases, plus function-call `ORDER BY`. |
| posthog/hogql/printer/test/test_printer.py | Printer coverage for PIVOT and function-call `ORDER BY`. |

title: feat: add new subscription delivery workflow with per-asset export    fan-out
body:
## Problem

The subscription system currently uses a single "main" temporal workflow which does all the work. This means that:

- We do not have good support to retry "components" that fail (currently this is all done through `tenacity` retries)
- If there are downstream errors with our infrastructure, and we exhaust the tenacity retries, the workflow will be marked as success, even though the export failed.
    - Whilst we could fix this, we still do not have a good way to retry the specific activity.

Additionally we did not have a consistent way to measure the success of these workflows.

> [!NOTE]
> This PR does not wire up the new flow yet, this is solely the introduction of the new flow. The wiring up will be done through: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/51649

## Changes

The idea in this PR is to tackle the problems outlined above, by introducing the following:

**Modular Export System** (will be needed as I would like to migrate the "one-off" exports to Temporal as well, so we can take out tenacity)

**Refactored Subscription Workflows into composable activities**

**Updated Import Structure** (Renamed `subscription_scheduling_workflow.py` to separate `activities.py` and `workflows.py`)

##### Before (single workflow, activity-level fan-out)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    S[Temporal Schedule] -->|cron| SAW[ScheduleAllSubscriptionsWorkflow]
    SAW --> FETCH[fetch_due_subscriptions]
    FETCH --> EMIT_START["emit_subscription_delivery_started\n(try/except — best effort)"]
    EMIT_START --> FAN{Fan-out activities}

    FAN --> D1[deliver_subscription_report #1]
    FAN --> D2[deliver_subscription_report #2]
    FAN --> DN[deliver_subscription_report #N]

    D1 & D2 & DN --> GATHER[asyncio.gather\nclassify succeeded / failed]
    GATHER --> EMIT_END["emit_subscription_delivery_outcome\n(try/except — best effort)"]

    subgraph "deliver_subscription_report (monolithic activity)"
        direction TB
        LOAD[Load subscription + insights]
        CREATE[Create ExportedAssets]
        EXPORT["Export each asset sequentially\n(tenacity retry x4, in-process)"]
        DELIVER[Send email / Slack]
        UPDATE[Update next_delivery_date]
        LOAD --> CREATE --> EXPORT --> DELIVER --> UPDATE
    end

    D1 --- LOAD

    style EXPORT fill:#fbb,stroke:#333
    style D1 fill:#ffd,stroke:#333
```

##### After (parent + child workflows, per-asset fan-out)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    S[Temporal Schedule] -->|cron| SAW[ScheduleAllSubscriptionsWorkflow]
    SAW --> FETCH["fetch_due_subscriptions\n(retry: 3x, 10s-5min)"]
    FETCH --> FAN{"Fan-out child workflows\n(idempotent ID - dedup on overlap)"}

    FAN --> PSW1[ProcessSubscriptionWorkflow #1]
    FAN --> PSW2[ProcessSubscriptionWorkflow #2]
    FAN --> PSWN[ProcessSubscriptionWorkflow #N]

    PSW1 & PSW2 & PSWN --> GATHER_WF["asyncio.gather\nLog failed IDs + raise if any failed"]

    subgraph "ProcessSubscriptionWorkflow (isolated per subscription)"
        direction TB
        SLO_START["emit_delivery_started\nSLO started - fires before any work\n(retry: 3x, 5s-1min)"]

        subgraph "try block"
            direction TB
            P1["create_export_assets\nBulk-create assets\n(retry: 3x, 10s-2min)"]

            subgraph "Phase 2: Parallel export fan-out"
                direction LR
                E1["export_asset #1\n(retry: 9x)"]
                E2["export_asset #2\n(retry: 9x)"]
                EN["export_asset #N\n(retry: 9x)"]
            end

            GATHER["asyncio.gather\n(return_exceptions=True)"]
            BUILD["_build_outcome_assets\nClassify success/failure\nexception_class + error_trace"]
            P3["deliver_subscription\nEmail / Slack - all assets incl. failed\n(retry: 3x, 10s-2min)"]

            P1 --> E1 & E2 & EN
            E1 & E2 & EN --> GATHER
            GATHER --> BUILD --> P3
        end

        subgraph "finally block (always runs)"
            direction TB
            ADV["advance_next_delivery_date\nSchedule always advances, even on failure\n(retry: 3x, 5s-1min)"]
            SLO_END["emit_delivery_outcome\nSLO completed - fires last\n(retry: 3x, 5s-1min)"]
            ADV --> SLO_END
        end

        SLO_START --> P1
        P3 --> ADV
    end

    PSW1 --- SLO_START

    style SLO_START fill:#e8f4ff,stroke:#333
    style P1 fill:#e8f4ff,stroke:#333
    style E1 fill:#bfb,stroke:#333
    style E2 fill:#bfb,stroke:#333
    style EN fill:#bfb,stroke:#333
    style GATHER fill:#ffd,stroke:#333
    style BUILD fill:#ffd,stroke:#333
    style P3 fill:#e8f4ff,stroke:#333
    style ADV fill:#f0e6ff,stroke:#333
    style SLO_END fill:#f0e6ff,stroke:#333
    style GATHER_WF fill:#ffd,stroke:#333
```

#### Key differences

|  | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Subscription isolation** | Activities in shared workflow - one stuck activity delays others | Child workflow per subscription - fully isolated lifecycle |
| **Export concurrency** | Sequential within single activity, tenacity retry | Parallel fan-out (`asyncio.gather`), Temporal retry |
| **Activity granularity** | Monolithic: load + create + export + deliver in one activity | 4 separate activities, each with independent retry policy |
| **Failure blast radius** | Export failure kills entire delivery | `return_exceptions=True` - deliver all assets incl. failed (placeholder) |
| **Overlap protection** | None - concurrent schedule runs = duplicate deliveries | Idempotent workflow ID rejects duplicate starts |
| **Schedule advancement** | Only on success - broken subscriptions retry forever | Always advances in finally block, even on failure |
| **SLO events** | Raw `posthoganalytics.capture` per subscription (3 events) | SLO framework: 1 started + 1 completed per subscription, with exception_class + error_trace |
| **Error observability** | error_type + error_message strings | exception_class + first 5 lines of traceback per failed asset |
| **Metrics** | Same 8 Temporal counters | Same 8 Temporal counters (preserved) |
| **On-demand delivery** | `HandleSubscriptionValueChangeWorkflow` with own logic | Delegates to same `ProcessSubscriptionWorkflow` |

## How did you test this code?

N/A this is currently not wired up, I've checked several times to ensure that the business logic between the old and new flow is similar (where it makes sense, e.g. certain metrics are dropped as those are migrated to SLO metrics now).

In the next PR I will do the detailed testing and wire it up, this is solely so the old and new can live side by side and to make reviewing it easier / isolated.

Also see the PoW here: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/51649 which uses the newly wired up logic.

## Publish to changelog?

No - this is primarily an internal refactor to improve system reliability and observability without changing user-facing functionality.

## Docs update

No docs update needed as this is an internal system refactor.

reviews.body: looks good!

title: feat(analytics-platform): create internal/test users cohort for new teams
body:
## Problem

Previously, by default we started new accounts with test filters for localhost and email domain, as event properties.

Because they were event properties, they greatly increase the number of bytes that needed to be read by some queries, in some cases slowing them down by a factor of 2x.

This changes them to a cohort, and testing that a person ID is not in a (small) cohort set is fast in comparison.

The localhost regex is not supported by cohorts (until behavioural cohorts are supported), but are replaced by `setInternalOrTestUser()` in posthog-js. With `defaults: '2026-01-30'`, this will be applied automatially for localhost

I did similar work in #46659 but closed it without merging.

## Changes

When a new team is created via `create_with_data`, we now automatically create an "Internal / Test users" cohort with an OR filter:
- `$internal_or_test_user` person property equals `true`
- `email` icontains `@<domain>` from the creating user's email (skipped for generic providers like gmail, hotmail, yahoo, etc.)

The team's `test_account_filters` is set to a single cohort `not_in` filter referencing this cohort, replacing the previous inline property filters.

Key changes:
- `posthog/models/cohort/cohort.py`: Added `get_or_create_internal_test_users_cohort()` with a hardcoded list of ~25 generic email domains to skip
- `posthog/models/team/team.py`: Updated `create_with_data()` to create the cohort early (before demo check) so all new teams get it, and added `transaction.on_commit` safety for the demo Celery task
- `posthog/test/test_team.py`: Updated existing test and added new test for generic email domain handling

## How did you test this code?

Added a couple of new tests for this behaviour

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

n/a

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Code (Opus 4.6). Based on the approach from #46659 with additional email domain matching for the creating user.
author: robbie-c

commits.message:
feat(analytics-platform): create internal/test users cohort for new teams

When a new team is created, automatically create an "Internal / Test
users" cohort matching $internal_or_test_user person property and the
creating user's email domain (skipping generic providers like gmail).
The team's test_account_filters now reference this cohort instead of
inline property filters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address PR review feedback

- Use existing GenericEmails utility (3,781 entries) instead of
  hardcoded 25-entry list for generic email detection
- Remove dead set_test_account_filters method
- Move `import re` to module level in cohort.py
- Remove unused imports (re, UUID, GenericEmails) from team.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(analytics-platform): add cohort kind field for system-created cohorts

Add a `kind` CharField to the Cohort model to distinguish
system-created cohorts from user-created ones. The internal/test
users cohort is now looked up by kind rather than name, making it
resilient to user renames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(visual-review): match oxfmt 4-space indent in baseline YAML
body: PyYAML defaults to 2-space indent but oxfmt enforces 4-space on YAML files. The auto-approve endpoint writes baselines that fail the oxfmt check on the next CI run. One-line fix: `indent=4` on `yaml.dump`.
author: webjunkie

reviews.body: Trivial one-line change adding indent=4 to yaml.dump for formatting consistency. No logic, data model, or API contract changes.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes a formatting mismatch between PyYAML’s default output (2-space indentation) and the repo’s `oxfmt` YAML formatting rules (4-space indentation), preventing auto-generated visual review baseline YAML from failing CI formatting checks. **Changes:** - Set `indent=4` in `_build_snapshots_yaml()` when dumping the baseline YAML via `yaml.dump`. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(visual-review): use 4-space indent in baseline YAML to match oxfmt

PyYAML defaults to 2-space indent, but the repo uses oxfmt which
enforces 4-space on YAML files. Without this, every CI auto-approve
writes 2-space YAML that fails the oxfmt check on the next run.

title: fix(devcontainer): pin uv image to 0.10.12
body:
<!-- mendral:session:action:01KMEE2R3G2ASREH388F6KN40F -->
## Summary

Fixes the Codespaces build failure caused by `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest` resolving to 0.11.0, which violates `required-version = "~=0.10.2"` in `pyproject.toml`.

- **`.devcontainer/Dockerfile`**: pin `--mount=from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv` to `:0.10.12` (latest 0.10.x release satisfying `~=0.10.2`)
- **`bin/check_uv_python_compatibility.py`**: add **Check 4** — validates that the Dockerfile uv image tag is pinned and matches `pyproject.toml`; catches unpinned (`:latest`) or mismatched tags at CI time
- **`.github/workflows/ci-python.yml`**: add `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` to the paths filter so the linter fires whenever the Dockerfile is changed

Relates to insight: _Codespaces Dockerfile: uv:latest (0.11.0) breaks required-version ~=0.10.2_

> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.

title: fix(ci): restore update-snapshots label requirement in storybook CI
body:
**Problem:** 6e64ea2 consolidated `detect-snapshot-mode` into the `changes` job but accidentally dropped the `update-snapshots` label gate. This means every human PR was running in update mode — snapshot mismatches get silently auto-committed instead of failing the check.

**Fix:** Restore the label check so regular human PRs default to check mode and need the `update-snapshots` label to enable auto-updates. Trusted bots keep their existing update-mode allow-list.
author: webjunkie

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Restores the intended Storybook snapshot gating behavior so that human PRs default to **check** mode and require the `update-snapshots` label to enable snapshot auto-updates, while keeping an allow-list for trusted bots. **Changes:** - Reintroduces an `update-snapshots` PR label check to opt into snapshot update mode. - Adjusts mode selection logic so unlabeled human PRs run in check mode and provide a clearer notice message. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(ci): restore update-snapshots label requirement in storybook CI

6e64ea2 accidentally dropped the `update-snapshots` label gate when
consolidating `detect-snapshot-mode` into the `changes` job. Without it
every human PR runs in update mode, meaning snapshot mismatches get
silently auto-committed instead of failing the check.

Restores:
- `HAS_UPDATE_LABEL` env var reading the PR label
- label-detected → update mode branch
- default for regular humans back to check mode

title: feat(notifications): implement backend API with org-scoped SSE and access control
body:
## Problem

The notification model needs backend logic to create events, resolve recipients, publish for real-time SSE delivery, and serve a REST API with access control filtering.

## Changes

- `RecipientsResolver` base class with default resolution for user/team/org/role targets — callers can provide custom resolvers (e.g., approval policy approvers)
- `create_notification()` resolves recipients via resolver, creates one `NotificationEvent` row, publishes to Kafka
- AC filtering at publish time via `filter_by_access_control()` on the resolver — passthrough when org lacks ADVANCED_PERMISSIONS
- Feature flag `real-time-notifications` checked at organization level
- REST API at `/api/environments/{team_id}/notifications/` with:
  - Access control filtering: collects unique resource types, one AC check per type, excludes denied types
  - Read state via `NotificationReadState` create/delete pattern
  - DRF `LimitOffsetPagination` (20 default, 100 max)
  - `@extend_schema(request=None)` on mark_read/mark_unread/mark_all_read
  - Endpoints: list, unread_count, mark_read, mark_unread, mark_all_read
- **Kafka-based notification delivery**: Django produces to `notification_events` topic, Go livestream consumes via `NotificationKafkaConsumer` and publishes to Redis for SSE fan-out (follows `SessionRecordingKafkaConsumer` pattern)
- Go SSE handler subscribes to org-scoped Redis channel `notifications:{organization_id}`, filters by `user_id in resolved_user_ids`, strips internal fields
- `AuthClaims` struct in Go JWT parsing (replaces multiple return-value functions)
- `organization_id` added to livestream JWT claims, TTL lowered to 1 day
- 90-day cleanup Celery task with batch deletion
- Unit tests for logic, API, and AC filtering

## How did you test this code?

- Units

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: yasen-posthog
