title: fix(build): prevent Redis connections during Docker collectstatic build step
body:
<!-- mendral:session:slack:TSS5W8YQZ:C0A7SH71ETY:1774257577.070529 -->
## Summary

- Two separate call sites attempt live Redis connections when Django initialises during `python manage.py collectstatic` in the Docker image build layer, producing `redis.exceptions.ConnectionError: Error 111 connecting to localhost:6379. Connection refused.` tracebacks on every build (both `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64`). The errors are caught so builds don't fail, but the noise is unnecessary.

## Root cause

**Call site 1 — `posthog/apps.py:106`:** `PostHogConfig.ready()` calls `queue_sync_hog_function_templates()` unconditionally for all non-test invocations. That function immediately does `r.setnx(lock_key, 1)` to acquire a distributed Redis lock before queuing a Celery task. No Redis is available during `docker build`.

**Call site 2 — `products/notebooks/backend/kernel_runtime.py:1564`:** `KernelRuntimeService` was instantiated at module level, which registers an `atexit` hook that calls `shutdown_all()` → acquires a Redis lock on process exit. The module is imported transitively via `NotebooksConfig.ready()` → `api/notebook.py` → `kernel_runtime.py`. The atexit fires when `collectstatic` finishes.

## Fix

1. Guard `queue_sync_hog_function_templates()` with the `STATIC_COLLECTION` env var already set by the Dockerfile for this step.
2. Replace the module-level `KernelRuntimeService()` singleton with a lazy getter — the instance (and its atexit/signal hooks) is only created on first real use, not at import time.

Fixes: https://app.mendral.com/insights/01KMD080H98VRD7A9GER5RHWDY


---

> [!NOTE]
> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.
>
> - [View implementation session](https://app.mendral.com/sessions/slack:TSS5W8YQZ:C0A7SH71ETY:1774257577.070529)
> - Triggered by [this Slack message](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0A7SH71ETY/p1774299928959989?thread_ts=1774257577.070529&cid=C0A7SH71ETY)
author: mendral-app

reviews.body:
### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `61f99123bb`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Pull request overview This PR reduces Django import-time side effects in the notebooks kernel runtime module by deferring `KernelRuntimeService` construction until first use, avoiding cleanup hook registration during module import (e.g., during build/collectstatic flows). **Changes:** - Replace the module-level `KernelRuntimeService()` singleton with a lazily initialized getter. - Route `get_kernel_runtime(...)` through the lazy getter to avoid eager initialization. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(build): prevent Redis connections during Docker collectstatic build step

Two call sites attempt live Redis connections when Django initialises
during `python manage.py collectstatic` in the Docker build layer:

1. PostHogConfig.ready() calls queue_sync_hog_function_templates() which
   immediately does r.setnx() to acquire a distributed lock.
   Guard with the STATIC_COLLECTION env var already set by the Dockerfile.

2. KernelRuntimeService is instantiated at module level in kernel_runtime.py,
   registering an atexit hook that calls shutdown_all() → acquires a Redis
   lock on process exit (i.e. when collectstatic finishes).
   Replace the module-level singleton with a lazy getter so the instance
   (and its atexit hook) is only created on first real use.

Both errors were caught and logged so builds did not fail, but they
produced noisy ConnectionError tracebacks on every image build.

title: feat(sessions): add distinct_id chunking for experimental backfill
body:
## Problem

The experimental sessions backfill splits each run into chunks using `team_id % N`. This produces uneven work distribution because some teams have far more events than others, meaning certain chunks process disproportionately more rows.

## Changes

- Add `distinct_id_chunks` config option that splits by `cityHash64(distinct_id) % N` instead
- This gives a much more even distribution since `cityHash64(distinct_id)` is already materialized in the events table ORDER BY key (so no extra computation cost), and distinct IDs are far more uniformly distributed than team IDs
- Keep `team_id_chunks` as a fallback option, but default to `distinct_id_chunks=64`
- Raise if both chunking strategies are specified simultaneously
- Refactor backfill tests to use parametrized snapshots for SQL verification

## How did you test this code?

Parametrized snapshot tests verify the generated SQL for all three modes (distinct_id chunking, team_id chunking, no chunking) and the mutual-exclusion error case.

I'm an agent — I haven't tested this manually against a real ClickHouse cluster.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code. The `cityHash64(distinct_id)` approach was chosen because:
1. The hash is already stored in the ORDER BY key, so the modulo is just a cheap integer op on already-decompressed data
2. The filter is pushed down to remote shards when querying the distributed events table from the sessions cluster
3. Unlike `_part` based splitting, it gives uniform distribution regardless of merge state
author: robbie-c

commits.message:
feat(sessions): use cityHash64(distinct_id) for experimental backfill chunking

Splitting backfill runs by team_id % N leads to uneven distribution since
some teams have far more events than others. cityHash64(distinct_id) is
already materialized in the events table ORDER BY key, so the modulo is
cheap, and it distributes rows much more evenly.

Both chunking strategies are kept available via config, with distinct_id
as the new default for the experimental backfill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat(experiments): add remove metrics from reorder modal
body:
## Problem

It's cumbersome to remove multiple metrics today. You have to click twice (open the metric modal, then delete). And for each one, a refresh of metrics is triggered.

## Changes

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4aa12f32-8f21-4f80-ab73-18c2c579f6c0

- Add a "Remove" button to each metric row in the reorder modal
- Removed metrics show strikethrough styling with a "Restore" option to undo
- On save, removed metrics (both inline and shared) are deleted in a single API call alongside the reorder
- Primary metrics require at least one to remain; secondary metrics can all be removed
- Drag is disabled on removed metrics

## Testing

1. Open an experiment with 2+ primary metrics → click "Reorder metrics"
2. Click "Remove" on a metric → verify strikethrough + "Restore" button
3. Click "Restore" → verify it reverts
4. Remove a metric, reorder the rest, click "Save" → verify both applied
5. Verify you can't remove the last primary metric (button disabled)
6. Repeat with secondary metrics
7. Test with shared metrics
author: andehen

reviews.body: Neat!

commits.message:
feat(experiments): add remove metrics from reorder modal

Allow users to mark metrics for removal directly from the reorder
modal. Removed metrics show strikethrough styling with a restore
option. Deletions are applied alongside reordering in a single save.

fix(experiments): address review feedback on reorder modal

Remove racy loadExperiment call and compute effective badge indices
for non-removed metrics.

title: chore(billing): migrate Salesforce to OAuth
body: The enrichment client used username/password/security_token auth which breaks when the password expires. Switch to OAuth Client Credentials flow using a dedicated Salesforce Connected App, which authenticates via consumer key/secret with no password expiration. The new auth is preferred when SF_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_KEY and SF_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_SECRET are set, with automatic fallback to legacy auth for backward compatibility during rollout. ## Problem We're currently using personal access tokens as credentials for enrichment jobs. I'm migrating this to use an OAuth External App I created ## Changes Migrate to: `SALESFORCE_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_KEY` and `SALESFORCE_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_SECRET` SFDC credentials ## ⚠️ need to deploy: https://github.com/PostHog/charts/pull/9264 first ## How did you test this code? I ran the temporal job locally and updated Infinex: 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ ## Publish to changelog? no ## Docs update
author: abhischekt

reviews.body: looks good, just a couple of minor comments

commits.message:
fix(billing): migrate Salesforce enrichment client to OAuth client credentials

The enrichment client used username/password/security_token auth which
breaks when the password expires. Switch to OAuth Client Credentials
flow using a dedicated Salesforce Connected App, which authenticates
via consumer key/secret with no password expiration.

The new auth is preferred when SF_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_KEY and
SF_INTERNAL_CONSUMER_SECRET are set, with automatic fallback to
legacy auth for backward compatibility during rollout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(billing): fix mypy error in salesforce client test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(billing): list specific missing env vars in error message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(billing): use retry session for OAuth token exchange

The token endpoint was using a bare requests.post() call, bypassing the
retry-backed session that handles transient 429/5xx errors. Thread the
retry session into _get_client_credentials_token() and add a 30s timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(error-tracking): set last_used on symbol set upload
body:
## Problem

Symbol sets created via the Django upload API never had `last_used` set, leaving it `NULL`. The Dagster cleanup job (`symbol_set_cleanup`) only garbage-collects symbol sets with a non-null `last_used` by default, so failed or incomplete uploads accumulated indefinitely.

## Changes

- Set `last_used = timezone.now()` when creating symbol sets in all upload paths:
  - `create_symbol_set()` (deprecated `create` and `start_upload` endpoints)
  - `bulk_create_symbol_sets()` (`bulk_start_upload`)
  - `bulk_finish_upload` and `finish_upload` (upload finalization)
  - `update` (re-upload of symbol set files)
- Add backfill migration (`0014`) to populate `last_used` for existing rows where it's `NULL`, using `now() + randint(0, 15) days` to spread cleanup across days.

## How did you test this code?

Manual code review tracing all symbol set creation paths.

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: hpouillot

commits.message:
fix(error-tracking): set last_used on symbol set upload

Symbol sets created via the Django API never had last_used set,
so the Dagster cleanup job couldn't garbage-collect failed uploads.
Now last_used is set at creation time and updated on upload
finalization. Includes a backfill migration for existing rows.

fix(error-tracking): replace backfill migration with hourly Dagster job

Migration would lock the table too long with 200M rows. Replace with
a Dagster job that updates 300k rows per hourly run, setting last_used
to today. Natural jitter from the schedule spread.
fix(error-tracking): batch backfill job in 10k row transactions

title: chore(messaging): move messaging models to products app
body:
**What:** Move messaging models + API from posthog/models/ and products/workflows/ into a new products/messaging/ product.

**Models moved:** MessageCategory, MessageTemplate, MessageRecipientPreference (3 models, Haven + Mahamad, mid-2025).
**Not moved:** MessagingRecord stays in posthog/models/ — legacy email send-tracking from Oct 2020, used by weekly digest and posthog/email.py.

**API moved from workflows:** message_categories, message_preferences, message_templates viewsets + customerio import service. Workflows no longer depends on messaging.

**How:**
- Product bootstrapped via `hogli product:bootstrap`, scaffolding trimmed
- LibCST-based import rewriting for model imports
- API files moved from products/workflows/ with import updates
- Route registration updated in posthog/api/__init__.py
- Re-exports removed from posthog/models/__init__.py
- Both migrations use SeparateDatabaseAndState with idempotent ContentType handling
- tach.toml: messaging added as non_isolated, workflows dependency on messaging removed
author: webjunkie

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Moves the legacy Messaging-related Django models out of `posthog/models/` into a dedicated `products.messaging` Django app, updating imports/usages and migrations so existing tables remain in place while Django state/app labels change.

**Changes:**
- Introduces the new `products.messaging` product/app (Django app config, models package, migrations, tests, and product workspace scaffolding).
- Updates workflows and core PostHog code to import messaging models from `products.messaging.backend.models.*` instead of `posthog.models.*`.
- Adds migrations using `SeparateDatabaseAndState` to transfer model state and update `ContentType` rows without moving existing DB tables.

title: feat(warehouse-sources): add advance config for sources and add HubSpot property selection
body: ## Problem Users importing HubSpot data into PostHog's data warehouse have no control over which properties are synced. Some users want a specific subset of properties rather than the full default set plus all custom properties. ## Changes I tried to do the new FE as BE configurable as we have everything else **Backend — HubSpot source config:** - Added `SourceFieldSwitchGroupConfig` with per-schema `SourceFieldInputConfig` textarea fields to `HubspotSource.get_source_config`, allowing users to specify comma-separated property lists per endpoint (contacts, companies, deals, etc.) - Added `HubspotCustomPropertiesConfig` to `generated_configs.py` to parse the new config fields - Added `custom_properties` to `job_inputs_allowed_keys` so saved property selections are returned to the frontend on reload - Added `get_default_properties()` method to `_BaseSource` (returns `None` by default); HubSpot source implements it from `DEFAULT_PROPS` **Backend — sync pipeline:** - `get_rows()` accepts optional `selected_properties` list; when provided, validates against HubSpot's property catalog, filters invalid ones with a warning, and requests exactly those properties (no custom property discovery) - When all selected properties are invalid, falls back to default behavior - Backfill of missing properties with `None` (for PyArrow column preservation) works correctly for both selected and default property paths **Bug fixes:** - `ExternalDataSchemaSerializer.update()`: only overwrite `sync_type` when explicitly provided in the request payload - `append_partition_key_to_table()`: handle empty-string date values instead of crashing with `ParserError` ## How did you test this code? Extensive local test Old + new tests pass ## Publish to changelog? NO
author: estefaniarabadan

commits.message: add advance configuration and add hubspot select properties

title: chore(dwh): add extended person properties to demo data
body:
## Problem

We don't have extended person properties available in the demo data by default.

## Changes

Adds an `extended_properties` data warehouse table and joins it onto persons, to serve as extended person properties in the demo data.

Each row is generated from the simulated person and their account state at simulation "now", and includes fields such as:

- `email`, `hedgebox_user_id`
- `company_name`, `industry`, `account_kind`
- `current_plan`, `team_size`, `file_count`
- `used_mb`, `allocation_used_fraction`, `monthly_bill_usd`
- `lifecycle_stage`
- `onboarding_variant`, `file_engagement_variant`
- `watches_marius_tech_tips`, `is_invitable`

The table is written as CSV into object storage, registered as a `DataWarehouseTable`, and joined onto `persons` by `properties.email`.

## How did you test this code?

Used it in a trends insight after resetting the dev environment
author: thmsobrmlr

reviews.body: This is a T1d-complex change (312 lines) with zero reviews and no CODEOWNERS match. It introduces new database model operations (`DataWarehouseJoin.objects.create`) and has a non-trivial side effect: the refactoring adds `csv_allow_double_quotes: True` to all existing demo warehouse table upserts, not just the new `extended_properties` table. While the change is scoped to demo data, the combination of complexity, zero reviews, and behavioral change to existing table operations warrants a human review.

title: fix(replay): search inspector by feature flag name
body:
## Problem

When searching in the session replay inspector, typing a feature flag name (key) doesn't surface `$feature_flag_called` events that contain that flag. The search only matches against the event label ("Feature flag called") and the raw event name, not the actual flag key stored in properties.

Closes #52027

## Changes

Added a `$feature_flag_called` case to `eventToDescription()` in `lib/utils.tsx` that returns the `$feature_flag` property value (the flag key). This makes the flag key both searchable in the inspector and visible as the event description.

Searching for "feature flag called" still works — the search string is composed of the taxonomy label + description, so both match.

## How did you test this code?

Added unit tests for `eventToDescription` covering:
- Feature flag called event with a flag key
- Feature flag called event without the `$feature_flag` property (falls back to event name)

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: TueHaulund

commits.message:
fix(replay): search inspector by feature flag name

The inspector search now matches $feature_flag_called events by the
flag key, not just the event label.

Closes #52027

title: feat(notifications): Go livestream infrastructure for real-time notifications
body:
## Problem

The real-time notifications system needs Go livestream changes to support SSE delivery and Kafka-based notification ingestion.

## Changes

- `AuthClaims` struct replaces multiple JWT extraction functions — extensible, backward-compatible
- `organization_id` added to livestream JWT claims, TTL lowered to 1 day
- `/notifications` SSE endpoint with org-scoped Redis subscription and per-user filtering
- `NotificationKafkaConsumer` bridges `notification_events` Kafka topic to Redis pub/sub (required for cross-pod fan-out across 10+ replicas)
- `Client()` getter on `StatsInRedis` for shared Redis access
- Kafka notification config fields (`NotificationEnabled`, `NotificationTopic`)

**Intended flow:** Django produces notification events to Kafka → Go consumes and publishes to Redis → Go SSE handler delivers to connected browsers.

## How did you test this code?

Go build and tests pass locally. E2E tested with manual Redis publish → SSE delivery to browser.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: yasen-posthog

reviews.body: Approving to unblock. Changes look reasonable to me, but make sure to get review for livestream folks first since this is magic code to me

commits.message:
feat(notifications): Go livestream infrastructure for real-time notifications

- AuthClaims struct in JWT parsing (replaces multiple return-value functions)
- organization_id added to livestream JWT claims, TTL lowered to 1 day
- NotificationsHandler: SSE endpoint with org-scoped Redis subscription,
  user filtering via resolved_user_ids, single-parse filterNotificationForUser
- NotificationKafkaConsumer: consumes from notification_events topic,
  publishes to Redis pub/sub for cross-pod fan-out (10+ replicas in prod)
- Kafka notification config fields (NotificationEnabled, NotificationTopic)
- Client() getter on StatsInRedis for shared Redis access

title: chore(mcp): enabled insights create saved property
body:
## Problem

  Insights created or updated via the MCP server don't appear in the default Insights list view because the
  `saved` field is not exposed in the MCP tool schemas. The Django API defaults `saved` to `false`, and since
  the MCP create/update schemas don't include the field, there's no way for the LLM to set it. Users have to
  manually click "Save" in the UI for the insight to appear in the list.

  Closes [#41089](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/41089)

  ## Changes

  Added `saved` field to the MCP insight Zod schemas in `services/mcp/src/schema/insights.ts`:

  - `CreateInsightInputSchema`: `saved: z.boolean().default(true)` — insights created via MCP are saved by
  default, matching UI behavior
  - `UpdateInsightInputSchema`: `saved: z.boolean().optional()` — allows explicitly toggling the saved state

  No backend changes needed — the Django `InsightSerializer` already accepts `saved` as a writable field.

  ## How did you test this code?

  Manual verification: create an insight via the MCP `insight-create-from-query` tool and confirm `saved: true`
   in the response and the insight appears in the UI Insights list.

  ## Publish to changelog?

  No

  ## Docs update

  No docs update needed.

title: feat(flags): Add integration tests using Django serializers for realistic cohort/flag data
body:
## Problem

Raw DB inserts in Rust integration tests bypass the Django serializers, which means:
- `cohort_type` is always `None` (not computed based on filter complexity)
- Bytecode is never generated for realtime cohorts
- Filter validation doesn't run

This masked a regression in #51002 where realtime cohorts (those with `cohort_type = "realtime"`) were incorrectly routed to `NoOpCohortMembershipProvider` instead of being evaluated via property matching.

## Changes

Add Django integration tests (`posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py`) that:

1. **Create test data via Django API** — Cohorts and flags go through `CohortSerializer` and `FeatureFlagSerializer`, ensuring realistic `cohort_type`, bytecode, and filter validation
2. **Call the Rust `/flags` endpoint** — Verify correct flag evaluation against production-realistic data
3. **Run automatically in CI** — New workflow spins up the Rust service and runs tests on relevant changes

### Tests cover:
- Realtime cohorts with person property filters (the exact regression case from #51002)
- Multiple person property conditions (AND logic)
- Regex and negation filters
- Nested cohort references
- Negated cohort references (IN one cohort AND NOT IN another)
- Date comparison filters
- Super conditions with realtime cohorts
- Static cohort membership

## How to run locally

```bash
# Terminal 1: Start the Rust flags service
cd rust/feature-flags && cargo run

# Terminal 2: Run the integration tests
SKIP_RUST_INTEGRATION_TESTS=0 pytest posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py -v
```

## CI

Tests run automatically via `ci-rust-flags-integration.yml` when changes are made to:
- `rust/feature-flags/**`
- `posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py`
- `posthog/api/feature_flags/**`
- `posthog/models/feature_flag/**`
- `posthog/models/cohort/**`

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: dmarticus

reviews.body: Nice test suite, solid approach. A couple suggestions inline.

commits.message:
feat(flags): Add integration tests using Django serializers for realistic cohort/flag data

This adds integration tests for the Rust /flags endpoint that create cohorts
and feature flags via Django serializers (CohortSerializer, FeatureFlagSerializer)
instead of raw DB inserts.

## Problem

Raw DB inserts in tests bypass the Django serializers, which means:
- cohort_type is always None (not computed based on filter complexity)
- Bytecode is never generated for realtime cohorts
- Filter validation doesn't run

This masked a regression in PR #51002 where realtime cohorts were incorrectly
routed to NoOpCohortMembershipProvider instead of being evaluated via property
matching.

## Solution

1. **create_test_data.py**: Python script that bootstraps Django and creates
   cohorts/flags through the real serializers. Supports $cohort_N placeholders
   for cross-referencing in batch operations.

2. **TestContext helpers**: New methods on TestContext:
   - create_cohort_via_django()
   - create_flag_via_django()
   - create_via_django_batch()

3. **test_flags_with_django_data.rs**: 8 new integration tests covering:
   - Realtime cohorts with person property filters (the exact regression case)
   - Multiple person property conditions (AND logic)
   - Regex and negation filters
   - Nested cohort references
   - Negated cohort references
   - Date comparison filters
   - Super conditions with realtime cohorts
   - Static cohort membership

Person/group creation stays as raw DB inserts (no meaningful serializer effects).
Only cohort and flag creation goes through Django.

fix(flags): Run Django data tests serially with increased timeout

The Django bootstrap takes 30-40s, and when 8 tests run in parallel,
each spawning a Python subprocess, resource contention caused all tests
to timeout at 60s in CI.

Changes:
- Increase subprocess timeout from 60s to 180s
- Add serial_test dependency and #[serial] attribute to all tests
- Tests now run one at a time, avoiding parallel Django bootstraps

feat(flags): Add Django integration tests for Rust /flags endpoint

Add integration tests that verify the Rust /flags endpoint correctly handles
cohorts and flags created via Django serializers (CohortSerializer,
FeatureFlagSerializer).

## Problem

Raw DB inserts in Rust tests bypass Django serializers, which means:
- cohort_type is always None (not computed based on filter complexity)
- Bytecode is never generated for realtime cohorts
- Filter validation doesn't run

This masked a regression where realtime cohorts were incorrectly routed to
NoOpCohortMembershipProvider instead of being evaluated via property matching.

## Solution

Django integration tests that:
1. Create cohorts/flags via Django API (ensuring realistic serialization)
2. Spin up Rust /flags service (or use FEATURE_FLAGS_SERVICE_URL in CI)
3. Call Rust endpoint and verify correct flag evaluation

Tests cover:
- Realtime cohorts with person property filters (the exact regression case)
- Multiple person property conditions (AND logic)
- Regex and negation filters
- Nested cohort references
- Negated cohort references (IN one cohort AND NOT IN another)
- Date comparison filters
- Super conditions with realtime cohorts
- Static cohort membership

To run locally:
  1. Start Rust flags service: cd rust/feature-flags && cargo run
  2. Run tests: SKIP_RUST_INTEGRATION_TESTS=0 pytest posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py -v

In CI, set FEATURE_FLAGS_SERVICE_URL to point to the Rust service container.

ci: Add workflow for Rust flags integration tests

Adds a dedicated CI workflow that:
1. Spins up Postgres and Redis services
2. Builds and starts the Rust /flags service
3. Runs Django integration tests against it

Triggered on changes to:
- rust/feature-flags/**
- posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py
- posthog/api/feature_flags/**
- posthog/models/feature_flag/**
- posthog/models/cohort/**

fix(ci): Use setup_test_environment instead of non-existent sqlx migrations

The feature-flags service uses Django's database schema, not its own
sqlx migrations. Use manage.py setup_test_environment like ci-rust.yml does.

fix(ci): Use test_posthog database consistently

- Change Postgres service to create test_posthog database (not posthog)
- Update all DATABASE_URL references to use test_posthog
- Replace setup_test_environment with migrate --run-syncdb (no ClickHouse needed)
- Rust service now connects to same DB that pytest-django uses

fix(ci): Pin GitHub Actions to full SHA commits

- actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 (v6.2.0)
- astral-sh/setup-uv@eac588ad8def6316056a12d4907a9d4d84ff7a3b (v7.3.0)
- Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 (v2.8.2)

fix: Build proper DATABASE_URL from Django settings

The previous code used settings.DATABASES['default']['TEST']['NAME'] directly,
which is just the database name (e.g. 'test_posthog'), not a full postgres URL.
Now we construct the full URL from the Django database settings.
fix: Use correct cargo command --bin feature-flags

fix: Use TransactionTestCase and add persons database support

- Switch to NonAtomicBaseTest (TransactionTestCase) so data is committed
  and visible to the external Rust server process
- Simplify fixture to require FEATURE_FLAGS_SERVICE_URL instead of
  trying to spawn subprocess (cleaner, matches CI)
- Add PERSONS_READ/WRITE_DATABASE_URL to CI workflow
- Create test_posthog_persons database in CI

Local testing:
  Terminal 1:
    cd rust/feature-flags
    READ_DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog:posthog@localhost:5432/test_posthog \
    WRITE_DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog:posthog@localhost:5432/test_posthog \
    PERSONS_READ_DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog:posthog@localhost:5432/test_posthog_persons \
    PERSONS_WRITE_DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog:posthog@localhost:5432/test_posthog_persons \
    cargo run --bin feature-flags

  Terminal 2:
    SKIP_RUST_INTEGRATION_TESTS=0 \
    FEATURE_FLAGS_SERVICE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 \
    pytest posthog/api/test/test_feature_flag_rust_integration.py -v

Fix mypy errors and add MaxMind DB download for Rust integration tests

- Use Client | APIClient union type for helper functions to fix mypy errors
- Add Download MaxMind Database step before starting Rust service
- The GeoIP database is required for the Rust feature-flags service to start
Fix mypy str-bytes-safe errors by decoding response.content

Address review feedback: add cohort API path, improve comments, standardize docs

- Add posthog/api/cohort.py to CI workflow trigger paths
- Clarify SECRET_KEY comment (Django cryptographic signing for sessions/CSRF)
- Standardize local run instructions in module docstring, reference from fixture

Install sqlx-cli and run persons DB migrations in CI

The conftest.py runs sqlx migrations via subprocess during test setup.
This was failing with FileNotFoundError because sqlx was not installed.

- Add setup-sqlx-cli action (uses cached binary, same as ci-rust.yml)
- Add persons DB migration step using rust/persons_migrations/
chore(ci): fix YAML formatting in ci-rust-flags-integration.yml

fix(ci): move Rust integration tests to separate package to skip ClickHouse

The Rust /flags integration tests were failing because they inherit from
NonAtomicBaseTest which pulls in posthog/conftest.py's django_db_setup
fixture that initializes ClickHouse. The CI workflow only has Postgres
and Redis.

Fix by moving tests to posthog/api/test/rust_integration/ with a custom
conftest that overrides django_db_setup to skip ClickHouse initialization.
Also replace cohort.insert_users_by_list() (which writes to ClickHouse)
with a direct CohortPeople Postgres insert, and suppress on_commit
callbacks to avoid triggering Celery tasks that require ClickHouse.

fix: improve Rust integration test robustness

- Make posthog/conftest.py tolerate missing ClickHouse gracefully,
  eliminating the need for a forked conftest in the integration test
  package
- Remove redundant manual cleanup tracking, relying on
  NonAtomicBaseTest._fixture_teardown for table cleanup
- Remove double skip mechanism (fixture + decorator), keeping only
  @pytest.mark.skipif with a class attribute for the server URL
- Fix test_group_based_flag to create real GroupTypeMapping and Group
  entities and assert on enabled/disabled rather than just key presence

chore: extract _cohort_filters helper to reduce boilerplate

The OR-of-AND cohort filter envelope was repeated 10 times across tests.
Extract a helper so each call site only specifies the property conditions.

chore: spike standalone Rust /flags integration tests

Explore an alternative approach that eliminates Django test framework
dependency entirely. Tests use psycopg2 for person inserts and HTTP
requests for cohort/flag creation and evaluation. A setup script creates
the org/team/user/API key via Django ORM before tests run.

chore: eliminate setup script, fix spike test infrastructure

- Bootstrap test environment (org, team, user, API key) directly in a
  session-scoped conftest fixture via raw SQL instead of a separate
  setup script writing to a JSON file
- Split database connections to match production topology: persons DB
  for persons/distinct IDs/cohort people, main DB for groups
- Rename PersonsDB to TestDB to reflect it manages both connections
- Fix mypy errors: use Generator return types for yielding fixtures
- Add Django server and standalone test step to CI workflow
- Delete setup_test_env.py (no longer needed)

chore: remove old Django-based integration tests

The standalone spike approach (psycopg2 + HTTP requests, no Django test
framework) replaces the old NonAtomicBaseTest-based tests entirely.
Revert the posthog/conftest.py ClickHouse tolerance change since the
spike tests don't use pytest-django at all.

chore: eliminate setup script, fix spike test infrastructure

Replace raw SQL bootstrap with Django ORM in conftest — raw SQL broke
because the organization table schema changed (missing product_subscription
column). The ORM tracks schema changes automatically, which is the same
philosophy driving the whole test approach.

Also fixes mypy errors on Generator return types.

fix(ci): detect early Rust service crashes in readiness loop

Check that the process is still alive on each iteration so we fail fast
instead of waiting the full 60 seconds when the service exits on startup.

fix: unblock Django DB access in session-scoped bootstrap fixture

pytest-django blocks ORM access unless tests use the django_db mark.
Use django_db_blocker.unblock() in the session-scoped env fixture so
the bootstrap can create the org/team/user via ORM while keeping data
committed and visible to the external Rust process.

fix: promote spike to main integration tests, fix scopes and cleanup

- Move tests out of spike/ subdirectory
- Add scopes=["*"] to PersonalAPIKey so API calls aren't rejected
- Fix posthog_cohortpeople cleanup (no team_id column, join via cohort)
- Fix mypy: use Iterator instead of Generator for fixture return types
- Update CI workflow to point at new test path
- Remove setup_test_env.py (bootstrap is inline in conftest)

fix(ci): set TEST=0 on Django server to prevent ClickHouse calls

The global TEST=1 env var causes CELERY_ALWAYS_EAGER=True, which makes
cohort calculation tasks run synchronously during API calls and try to
reach ClickHouse (port 9000). Since ClickHouse isn't in this CI
environment, the cohort API returns 500.

Setting TEST=0 on the Django server step makes tasks enqueue to Redis
without executing, which is the correct behavior for an integration
test where we only need the data in Postgres.

fix: address PR review feedback from haacked

- Fix fixture teardown ordering: db depends on api so cohort people are
  cleaned up before cohorts are deleted
- Add pagination limit and assert on delete status in API cleanup
- Pin actions/checkout to SHA for supply-chain hardening
- Replace raise_for_status with assert to preserve response body in errors
- Pin disabled-flag assertion to check enabled=False specifically
- Add docstring to _cohort_filters explaining empty-args behavior

fix: move pytestmark to test module where it actually takes effect

pytestmark in conftest.py doesn't propagate to test files, so the
skip condition was silently ignored and these tests ran (and failed)
in the regular CI suite where Django and the Rust service aren't
available.

fix(ci): use PERSONS_DB_WRITER_URL for Django persons database routing

Django reads PERSONS_DB_WRITER_URL (not PERSONS_DATABASE_URL) to
configure the persons_db_writer database alias. Without this, the
Django server with TEST=0 and DEBUG=1 defaults to connecting to
posthog_persons instead of test_posthog_persons, causing flag
validation to fail when it queries the persons database.

fix: enable realtime cohort evaluation and fix teardown in Rust integration tests

- Set BEHAVIORAL_COHORTS_READ_DATABASE_URL and
  ENABLE_REALTIME_COHORT_EVALUATION for the Rust service in CI so
  cohort membership is actually evaluated at runtime
- Fix cleanup to use PATCH with deleted=true for feature flags (the
  viewset doesn't expose DELETE, returns 405)
- Fix test_disabled_flag assertion: inactive flags are excluded from
  the response, not returned with enabled=false

fix(ci): disable cohort cache TTL and remove realtime cohort evaluation

Set CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=0 on the Rust flags service so each test request
fetches fresh cohort definitions. Without this, the CohortCacheManager
caches definitions for 5 minutes and tests that create new cohorts after
the first cache load see stale data.

Remove ENABLE_REALTIME_COHORT_EVALUATION and
BEHAVIORAL_COHORTS_READ_DATABASE_URL since the realtime provider queries
the cohort_membership table (not populated in this test setup) and
prevents the evaluate_dynamic_cohorts fallback that evaluates person
properties inline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: insert groups into both main and persons databases

The Rust flags service reads group type mappings and group properties
from the persons database (via PERSONS_READ_DATABASE_URL), but the test
was only inserting into the main database. This caused group-based flag
evaluation to return false because the Rust service couldn't find the
group records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(ci): use debug build for Rust flags integration tests

Release-mode compilation takes ~9 minutes and optimizes runtime
performance, which is unnecessary for integration tests that run
for 35 seconds total. Debug builds should cut this to ~2-3 minutes.

chore(ci): capture Rust service logs on failure and enable backtraces

The debug build caused the Rust service to crash mid-test-run.
Redirect stdout/stderr to a log file and dump it on failure so we
can see the panic message and stack trace.

fix: don't pass empty filters for static cohorts in integration tests

When a static cohort is created with empty filters, the Django
serializer's `_calculate_realtime_support()` returns True (vacuously)
and overwrites `cohort_type` to "realtime". This creates an invalid
state where `is_static=true` and `cohort_type="realtime"`, which
triggers a debug_assert in the Rust service. Omitting filters for
static cohorts avoids this path entirely.

fix: don't pass empty filters for static cohorts in integration tests

Static cohorts define membership by an explicit list of person IDs,
so their cohort_type must never be overridden by the filter-based
realtime calculation. The create() and update() methods now skip
validate_filters_and_compute_realtime_support() for static cohorts.

Also disable debug assertions in the CI debug build, since the
codebase contains debug_assert! checks that crash on data invariant
violations (e.g., a static cohort with cohort_type=realtime).

chore(ci): add process liveness check to Django startup loop

Match the Rust service startup pattern by checking kill -0 on each
iteration, so the workflow fails fast if Django crashes on startup
instead of waiting the full 30 seconds.

title: chore(flags): add doc on evaluation threading mechanism
body:
## Problem

The sequential-vs-parallel flag evaluation architecture (Tokio + Rayon thread pools, semaphore backpressure) introduced in #47457 and followed up by https://github.com/PostHog/charts/pull/8577 is important to understand our current performance state, but it's not documented.

## Changes

Adds an internal doc at `docs/internal/feature-flags/evaluation-threading-architecture.md` covering:
- Why two thread pools exist (Tokio starvation incident)
- Thread pool sizing and oversubscription tradeoffs
- The evaluation threshold and sequential vs parallel paths
- The `RayonDispatcher` backpressure mechanism
- Dependency-level evaluation stages
- Configuration reference and observability metrics

## How did you test this code?

Documentation only — no code changes.

## Publish to changelog?

No.
author: matheus-vb

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Adds internal documentation describing how the Feature Flags Rust service avoids Tokio runtime starvation by splitting CPU-bound batch evaluation onto a bounded Rayon thread pool with semaphore-based backpressure, plus how evaluation stages/promotions and observability tie in. **Changes:** - Documented the motivation for Tokio vs Rayon separation and the starvation incident that led to it. - Documented sizing/oversubscription strategy, sequential vs parallel thresholding, and the `RayonDispatcher` backpressure/timeout behavior. - Added a configuration + metrics reference for tuning and diagnosing parallel evaluation behavior. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
:shipit:

commits.message: add doc on evaluation threading mechanism

title: feat(flags): evolve custom canary workflow
body:
## Problem

Deploying canary builds of the feature-flags Rust service requires manually triggering `rust-docker-build.yml`, waiting for it to finish, then manually running the charts repo canary workflow with the right image tag. This is error-prone and slow.

## Changes

Adds an end-to-end canary deployment workflow triggered from the posthog repo:

**`canary-flags-enable.yml`** — builds the feature-flags image and dispatches to charts:
- Triggered via `/pr-canary` PR comment (with optional `weight=` and `env=` params), `canary-flags` label, or `workflow_dispatch`
- Validates PR approval and team membership (`team-feature-flags` / `team-flags-platform`)
- Builds the Docker image on Depot with the PR head SHA
- Dispatches `enable-canary-flags` to the charts repo with image tag, weight, and environment
- Comment triggers get immediate feedback: `eyes` reaction on receipt, `rocket` + summary table on success, error comment on failure
- Per-PR concurrency group cancels stale builds on new pushes

**`pr-closed.yml`** — adds a `disable-canary-flags` job:
- Dispatches `disable-canary-flags` to charts when a PR with the `canary-flags` label is closed or merged

_Note: requires https://github.com/PostHog/charts/pull/9180_

## How did you test this code?

Manual review of workflow YAML. Will validate end-to-end on a test PR after merge.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A
author: matheus-vb

title: chore(surveys): move survey models and API to products app
body:
**What?** Moves `Survey` and `SurveyResponseArchive` models plus the survey API (`survey.py`, tests) from `posthog/` into `products/surveys/backend/`.

**Why?** Continuing the product isolation effort — surveys already had an app config and backend dir, but models and API still lived in the monolith.

**How?**
- `posthog/models/surveys/` (5 files) → `products/surveys/backend/` with `db_table` to preserve table names
- `posthog/api/survey.py` + tests → `products/surveys/backend/api/`
- 3 migrations using `SeparateDatabaseAndState` — no schema changes, just ContentType update
- Updated ~60 importers codebase-wide, fixed LibCST trailing-comma artifacts, updated mock patch strings and string FK refs
- product_tours M2M FK ref updated from `posthog.Survey` → `surveys.Survey`
author: webjunkie

reviews.body: thank youuuuuuuuuuu

## Pull request overview

This PR continues product isolation by moving Surveys’ Django models (`Survey`, `SurveyResponseArchive`) and the Surveys API from `posthog/` into the `products/surveys/backend/` Django app, while preserving existing database table names and updating ContentTypes/migration state accordingly.

**Changes:**
- Relocates survey models + API into `products/surveys/backend/`, preserving DB table names via `db_table` and `SeparateDatabaseAndState`.
- Updates imports, patch targets, and string model refs across `posthog/`, `products/product_tours/`, and `ee/` to use the new module locations.
- Adds migrations to move model state between apps and update `django_content_type` app labels.

commits.message:
chore(surveys): move Survey and SurveyResponseArchive models to products app

Survey models lived in posthog/models/surveys/ — this moves them into
products/surveys/backend/ to continue the product isolation effort.

Both models get db_table to preserve existing table names, migrations use
SeparateDatabaseAndState so no actual schema changes happen. ContentType
rows are updated from posthog → surveys. ForeignKey string refs and
file_system registration app_label updated accordingly.

Also fixes 7 trailing-comma syntax errors left by the LibCST import
rewriter and updates mock patch strings in test_external_surveys.

chore(surveys): move survey API files to products app

Moves posthog/api/survey.py and its tests into
products/surveys/backend/api/. Updates router registration, url imports,
and cross-module lazy imports (feature_flag, remote_config, survey model).
Also fixes mock patch strings in test files.

fix(surveys): fix migration ordering and add product_tours tach module

Reorder migrations so surveys.0001 (create) runs before posthog.1057
(delete) — otherwise Django's lazy reference checker sees a dangling
ref from product_tours.0003's M2M to posthog.survey.

New order: surveys.0001 → product_tours.0006 → posthog.1057

Also add products.product_tours as a proper tach module (was previously
lumped into <root>) with correct dependency declarations.

chore(surveys): consolidate models into single models.py

Moves Survey and SurveyResponseArchive from separate files
(survey.py, survey_response_archive.py) into models.py to match
the convention used by other products with few models.

fix(surveys): make content type move idempotent and retry-safe

Move the ContentType update from posthog.1063 into surveys.0001 using
the same idempotent pattern as experiments — handles the case where
Django's post_migrate handler auto-creates duplicate content types
before the RunPython executes.

title: fix(experiments): printed hogql respect query configuration.
body: ## Problem > [!IMPORTANT] > PR #51771 adds an explicit limit to the query builder, but this future-proofs the code generation. The HogQL and ClickHouse SQL returned by the experiment query are generated at different stages of the query execution pipeline: while `clickhouse_sql` is created using a `HogQLQueryExecutor` that internally calls `_apply_limit` (before #51771, which only sets the default to `100`), `hogql` is generated using `to_printed_hogql`, which outputs the AST before `_apply_limit` runs. Since the AST lacks an explicit limit, the printer applies `limit_top_select=True` logic, which defaults to the maximum limit of `50000`. ## Changes | Clickhouse query settings | | - | | | | before | after | | - | - | | | | Refactored `get_experiment_query_sql` into `get_experiment_query_debug` that now returns a tuple with both HogQL and ClickHouse SQL, making them go through the same query preparation pipeline and show consistent settings. ## How did you test this code? ```bash pytest posthog/hogql_queries/experiments/test/experiment_query_runner -v ``` ![cat-type](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/48763dc8-aa5f-4566-8409-293f3d914ecc) 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ ## Publish to changelog? do not publish to changelog ## Docs update
author: rodrigoi

commits.message: fix(experiments): printed hogql respect query configuration.
fix(experiments): manual refactor of the generated code.

title: feat(db): add multi-database setup for products (re-land)
body:
Re-land of #51267, reverted in #51754 because migrations failed through PgBouncer.

**What changed since the revert**
- Migrations now use a direct Aurora connection (`PRODUCT_DB_<NAME>_DIRECT_URL`) bypassing PgBouncer, same pattern as the main DB's `default_direct` alias
- The router allows migrations on both `_db_writer` and `_db_direct` aliases

**What's here**
- `ProductDBRoute` dataclass + YAML loader (`posthog/product_db_config.py`)
- Django database router that dispatches reads/writes per app label (`posthog/product_db_router.py`)
- Auto-registration of `DATABASES` entries from route config in `data_stores.py` — env-var driven in prod, convention-based local defaults in dev
- `migrate_product_databases` management command called from `bin/migrate`, using direct Aurora connection
- Postgres init script that creates product databases from the YAML on first `docker compose up`
- System check that catches duplicate writer aliases for the same app label
- Tests for routing, loading, and the system check

**How it works in prod**
Charts repo injects `PRODUCT_DB_VISUAL_REVIEW_WRITER_URL`, `PRODUCT_DB_VISUAL_REVIEW_READER_URL` (PgBouncer), and `PRODUCT_DB_VISUAL_REVIEW_DIRECT_URL` (direct Aurora). If none are set, the route is silently skipped and everything stays on `default`.

**Depends on**
- PostHog/charts#9211 (direct URL env var)
- PostHog/posthog-cloud-infra#7164 (PgBouncer SG fix)
author: webjunkie

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR re-lands the “product app → separate Postgres database” infrastructure, driven by `products/db_routing.yaml`, and updates the first routed product (`visual_review`) to be compatible with cross-database constraints. It also introduces a dedicated migration path intended to bypass PgBouncer via a “direct” connection alias.

**Changes:**
- Add YAML-driven product DB route loading + a Django DB router for per-app read/write routing.
- Auto-register product DB aliases in settings (writer/reader + a direct alias) and run product DB migrations from `bin/migrate`.
- Update `visual_review` models/tests/migrations to remove cross-DB ForeignKeys (Team/User) and run tests against the product DB aliases.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 23 out of 24 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_tasks.py | Use pytest-django `databases=` to include product DB aliases. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_presentation.py | Allow Django `TestCase` DB access to product DB aliases. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_models.py | Update tests for `team_id` field and product DB aliases. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_logic.py | Update tests for `team_id` and product DB aliases; transaction test updated. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_github_integration.py | Update fixtures/tests for `team_id` + product DB aliases. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/test_api.py | Update API tests to run with product DB aliases. |
| products/visual_review/backend/tests/conftest.py | Centralize `PRODUCT_DATABASES` aliases for visual_review tests. |
| products/visual_review/backend/models.py | Replace cross-DB FKs with integer ID fields (`team_id`, `approved_by_id`, `reviewed_by_id`). |
| products/visual_review/backend/migrations/0001_initial.py

Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

commits.message: fix(db): centralize product database route config

fix(db): remove cross-DB foreign keys and harden product routing

Replace ForeignKey(Team) and ForeignKey(User) in visual_review models
with plain BigIntegerField — cross-database FKs don't work when product
tables live in a separate Postgres database.

Also fixes:
- allow_migrate returning None for non-routed apps on product DBs,
  which caused all 220 tables to migrate into the product database.
  Now returns False explicitly.
- Path vs str type mismatch for settings.BASE_DIR in route loader.
- UniqueConstraint referencing old "team" field name.
- Adds connect_timeout=3 on all product database connections to prevent
  workers blocking for 60-120s on unreachable hosts.

Includes a test script (scripts/test_db_isolation.py) that locks the
product DB and proves core Django endpoints stay responsive.
chore(db): remove test_db_isolation script from repo

feat(db): update product DB routing to PRODUCT_DB_<name>_WRITER/READER_URL convention

Align with charts PR env var convention. Database aliases derived
from the database name in db_routing.yaml — `<name>_db_writer` and
`<name>_db_reader`. Multiple products can share a database.

Simplified db_routing.yaml: just app_label + database, no explicit
aliases or reader_database needed.

fix(db): update visual_review tests for plain integer fields

Replace Repo(team=team) with Repo(team_id=team.id) across all test
files and seed command to match the FK removal in models.py.

Also adds product database documentation section to products/README.md.

fix(db): address review feedback on product DB routing

- Fix LRU cache leak in test: addCleanup(cache_clear) after system check test
- Add type annotation to FAKE_PRODUCT_DATABASES
- Tighten allow_relation: only permit relations between models in the
  same product database, not across product/default boundaries
- Use PG_HOST/PG_PORT in fallback URL instead of hardcoded localhost:5432
- Use POSIX [[:space:]] in awk instead of \s for BusyBox compat in Docker
- Validate db_name in init script against [a-z0-9_] pattern
- Log warning on incomplete route entries in db_routing.yaml

chore(db): simplify migrate command and auto-register product DB routes

- Remove custom "product-dbs" scope from bin/migrate, just run under
  "postgres" like everything else
- Drop ensure_database_exists from migrate_product_databases — Docker
  init script handles dev, infra handles prod
- Auto-add db_routing.yaml entry in hogli product:bootstrap so new
  products get separate databases by default

fix(db): auto-create product database only in dev

Ensure product database exists before migrating, but only when
DEBUG=1. In prod the database must be provisioned via infra —
auto-creating on a misconfigured URL could create unexpected
databases on shared clusters.
chore(db): document how to opt out of separate product databases
chore(db): prompt for separate database during product bootstrap

fix(db): bind transaction.atomic to product database alias

transaction.atomic() without using= defaults to the "default" database.
With the router sending writes to visual_review_db_writer, the atomic
block was wrapping the wrong connection — partial commits on the product
DB could persist even if the atomic block rolled back.

chore(db): allow sharing a database with another product in bootstrap

Show existing databases during product:bootstrap so related products
from the same team can co-host on one database while still being
separate Django apps.

fix(db): configure product database aliases in test mode

Tests were running without product database aliases, so
transaction.atomic(using=WRITER_DB) failed with
ConnectionDoesNotExist. Now:

- Settings register product DB aliases in TEST mode (pointing at a
  test database in the same Postgres instance, like persons_db)
- conftest.py auto-includes all *_db_writer/*_db_reader aliases in
  the databases set
- WRITER_DB falls back to "default" if the alias isn't configured
  (safety net for envs where the route is intentionally skipped)

fix(db): configure product test databases with proper Django TEST settings

Three issues causing test failures:

1. Product DB aliases registered in TEST mode but without
   TEST: {"DEPENDENCIES": []} — Django didn't create tables.
   Reader mirrors writer in tests.

2. pytest-django's @pytest.mark.django_db defaults to
   databases={"default"}, blocking access to product DB aliases.
   Added pytest_collection_modifyitems hook in visual_review conftest
   to inject the product databases into all django_db markers.

3. conftest auto-includes product DB aliases in TestCase.databases
   for class-based tests.

fix(db): configure product test databases with proper Django TEST settings

Product databases now work in tests with real multi-DB routing:

- Register product DB aliases in TEST mode with proper TEST settings:
  writer gets DEPENDENCIES=[] (independent creation + migration),
  reader gets MIRROR=writer (shares the writer's test database)
- Router returns writer alias for reads in TEST mode so reads share
  the same connection and transaction as writes — otherwise reads
  can't see uncommitted data within the same test
- All visual_review test classes explicitly declare
  databases=PRODUCT_DATABASES on their django_db markers
- WRITER_DB in logic.py falls back to "default" when the product
  alias isn't configured (safety net for non-product test runs)
- conftest auto-includes product aliases in TestCase.databases for
  class-based tests (APIBaseTest etc.)

All 112 visual_review tests pass with real multi-DB routing.

fix(db): update router test for TEST mode read routing

In TEST mode, db_for_read returns the writer alias so reads share
the same connection as writes. Update the test assertion to match.

fix(db): don't add product DB aliases to global TestCase.databases

Adding product aliases globally made every core test try to connect
to the product database. Only product-specific tests should declare
their product database aliases — they already do via explicit
databases=PRODUCT_DATABASES on markers and class attributes.

fix(db): use direct Aurora connection for product DB migrations

PgBouncer in transaction mode doesn't support DDL properly, causing
migration timeouts. Register a _db_direct alias using
PRODUCT_DB_<NAME>_DIRECT_URL (falls back to writer URL if not set).
Migration command uses the direct alias to bypass PgBouncer.

fix(db): address review feedback on direct alias and hardcoded WRITER_DB

- Only register _db_direct alias when PRODUCT_DB_<NAME>_DIRECT_URL is
  explicitly set — in dev/test there's no PgBouncer, so migrations use
  the writer alias directly
- Add _db_direct to _product_db_aliases so allow_migrate blocks
  non-product apps from migrating into it
- Update conftest to also rewrite _db_direct NAME for test databases
- Derive WRITER_DB from app label constant instead of hardcoding the
  full alias string

111 visual_review tests pass locally (1 skip needs MinIO).

title: chore(experiments): Keep step_sessions in stored results, move utils out of activities
body:

Stored experiment results strip `step_sessions` before saving. Soon we want to start using stored results for the main experiment view, which needs step_sessions.

## Changes

- Move utility functions (`get_metric`, `check_significance_transition` and helpers) from `activities.py` to `utils.py`

## How did you test this code?
Tests pass

title: fix(flags): scope HyperCache verification to teams with flags
body:
## Problem

The flags cache verification Celery task takes ~11-12 minutes because it iterates over every team, even though around 89% have never created a feature flag. This wastes compute and delays detection of genuine cache issues.

## Changes

- Scope HyperCache verification/fix to teams that have (or had) feature flags, dramatically reducing the verification surface.
- Add `get_teams_queryset_fn` to `HyperCacheManagementConfig` for pluggable team-scoping on both management commands and Celery tasks
- Verify both flag definition cache variants (with-cohorts and without-cohorts) independently so stale data in either variant is detected and fixed

Next steps, update the warm path accordingly.

## How did you test this code?

- Existing and new unit tests for team-scoping in the base command, flags cache, and Celery task
- Ran `python manage.py verify_flags_cache --verbose` locally and confirmed scoped team count
- Ran `python manage.py verify_flags_cache --verbose --fix` and confirmed fixes apply outside the grace period
- Will monitor Celery task duration in production after deploy

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: haacked

reviews.body: I revised it and I didn't find anything wrong. It looks good to me 🚀

commits.message:
fix(flags): scope HyperCache verification to teams with flags

Only verify/fix cache entries for teams that have (or had) feature
flags, reducing the verification surface from ~238K teams to ~24K.
Also verify both flag definition cache variants (with-cohorts and
without-cohorts) independently so that stale data in either variant
is detected and fixed.

Key changes:
- Add get_teams_queryset_fn to HyperCacheManagementConfig for
  pluggable team-scoping on both management commands and Celery tasks
- Replace partition-based optimization (team IDs + empty_cache_value)
  with queryset-level scoping that skips irrelevant teams entirely
- Verify both flag definition variants independently in the Celery
  task, with per-variant error isolation
- Remove --variant CLI argument from verify_flag_definitions_cache
  (always verifies both)
- Warm path intentionally still warms all teams to prevent cache
  misses falling through to database lookups

fix(flags): create flags in sample test so teams pass scope filter

The test_verify_with_sample test created teams without flags, but the
verification command scopes to teams with flags via
get_teams_with_flags_queryset(). The sample was drawn from 0 eligible
teams, so verification ran on 0 teams instead of 3.
fix(flags): create flag in same loop as team creation

title: feat(flags): skip PostgreSQL fallback for team token lookups
body:
## Problem

We're seeing a huge volume of requests for a project token that doesn't exist in our database, putting unnecessary load on PostgreSQL. We have a negative cache for invalid tokens, but it can only do so much — each cache eviction still triggers a PG query for a token that will never resolve. We could enlarge the cache and extend the TTL, but that would only make us more susceptible to cache poisoning.

As HyperCache (Redis/S3) matures as the source of truth for team tokens, we no longer need to fall back to PostgreSQL on cache misses. Skipping the fallback eliminates this database pressure entirely.

## Changes

- Add `SKIP_PG_TEAM_FALLBACK` env var (default `false`) to gate the behavior for safe rollout
- When enabled, HyperCache misses return `TokenValidationError` instead of querying PostgreSQL — this is the same error used for genuinely invalid tokens, which triggers negative caching and a 401 response
- Add `flags_pg_team_fallback_skipped_total` metric to track how often the fallback is skipped
- Thread the new config through `FlagService::new` and both API entry points

## How did you test this code?

Added three new integration tests:
- `test_skip_pg_fallback_still_resolves_token_from_hypercache` — tokens in HyperCache resolve normally
- `test_skip_pg_fallback_rejects_unknown_token_without_pg` — tokens only in PG are rejected when skip is enabled
- `test_skip_pg_fallback_disabled_still_finds_team_in_pg` — confirms default behavior (PG fallback) is unchanged
- Manual test. Made request with valid token, then two requests with invalid token:

<details>

Relevant part of the two log entries

> 2026-03-20T18:19:55.649453Z  INFO  **error_code="invalid_token"**
> 2026-03-20T18:20:14.946661Z  INFO **team_cache_source="negative_cache" error_code="invalid_token"**

</details>

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: haacked

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR introduces an opt-in mechanism to skip PostgreSQL fallback when resolving teams by project API token, treating HyperCache (Redis/S3) as the source of truth to reduce PostgreSQL load from repeated lookups of non-existent tokens. **Changes:** - Add `SKIP_PG_TEAM_FALLBACK` config (default `false`) and thread it into request entry points and `FlagService`. - When enabled, HyperCache misses no longer fall back to PostgreSQL; a new `flags_pg_team_fallback_skipped_total` metric tracks skipped fallbacks. - Add/extend tests to cover behavior with fallback enabled vs disabled. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

title: feat: send feature flags with AI analytics events
body:
## Summary

- Adds `send_feature_flags=True` to all AI analytics event capture calls so active feature flags appear as `$feature/` properties on server-side events like "chat with ai", "ai tool executed", "ai mode executed", and "ai deep research executed"
- Adds a `send_feature_flags` parameter to `report_user_action` (defaults to `False` so existing callers are unaffected)
- Wraps `report_user_action` and direct `posthoganalytics.capture` calls with OTel trace spans (`posthog.event` + `posthog.send_feature_flags` attributes) to monitor the latency impact of the extra `/decide` call

## Context

AI events fired from the server side don't automatically pick up active feature flags the way frontend SDK events do. This makes it hard to break down AI usage by flag variants. Adding `send_feature_flags=True` causes the Python SDK to resolve flags via `/decide` and include them as `$feature/` properties on the event.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify AI events in PostHog now include `$feature/` properties
- [ ] Check OTel traces for `report_user_action` and `posthoganalytics.capture` spans to confirm latency is acceptable


🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: pauldambra

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR updates server-side AI analytics event capture so that active feature flags are resolved via `/decide` and attached to events as `$feature/` properties, and adds OTel spans to measure the latency impact of this additional flag-resolution step. **Changes:** - Added `send_feature_flags` (default `False`) to `report_user_action`, and forwarded it to `posthoganalytics.capture`. - Enabled `send_feature_flags=True` on key AI events (`chat with ai`, `ai tool executed`, `ai mode executed`, `ai deep research executed`). - Wrapped relevant analytics capture paths in OTel spans with `posthog.event` and `posthog.send_feature_flags` attributes. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | posthog/event_usage.py | Adds `send_feature_flags` to `report_user_action`, forwards it into capture, and adds an OTel span for instrumentation. | | ee/hogai/core/runner.py | Ensures AI conversation state reporting uses `send_feature_flags=True`. | | ee/hogai/core/agent_modes/executables.py | Wraps AI “tool executed” and “mode executed” captures in OTel spans and enables `send_feature_flags=True`. | | ee/hogai/chat_agent/runner.py | Enables feature-flag sending and tracing on initial AI “mode executed” capture. | | ee/hogai/research_agent/runner.py | Enables feature-flag sending and tracing on AI “deep research executed” capture. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started</

An unresolved P1 bot comment correctly identifies that direct `posthoganalytics.capture(..., send_feature_flags=True)` calls inside `async def` functions (chat_agent/runner.py, research_agent/runner.py, executables.py) will block the event loop during the synchronous `/decide` HTTP call to resolve feature flags. While `runner.py` correctly uses `database_sync_to_async`, the other three files don't apply the same protection. This is a real production performance risk in AI streaming code.
Thanks!

commits.message:
feat: send feature flags with AI analytics events

Add send_feature_flags=True to posthoganalytics.capture calls for AI
events (chat with ai, ai tool executed, ai mode executed, ai deep
research executed) so active feature flags are included as $feature/
properties on these server-side events.

Also adds OTel trace spans around report_user_action and the direct
capture calls to monitor the latency impact of the extra /decide call.

title: feat(phrocs): Batch PTY output, CPU bars, memory warnings, and mprocs compat
body: ## Changes - **Fix PTY backpressure**: `readLoop` was sending one bubbletea message per line, causing the message queue to back up and throttle child processes (visible as capture becoming slower when its tab was open). Now lines are read into a buffered channel and flushed as a single notification every ~16ms — burst output that generated thousands of messages now coalesces into ~60 updates/sec. - **CPU usage bars in sidebar**: Each sidebar row shows an htop-style background fill proportional to CPU usage, with color tinting by intensity (green/yellow/red). - **High memory warning**: Processes using ≥ 2 GB RSS show a ⚠ icon in the sidebar (replacing the status dot) and a warning banner at the top of the info panel. - **`p` key → htop**: Opens htop (or btop/top) filtered to the selected process's PID. Only enabled for non-docker processes. - **`d` key → lazydocker**: Opens lazydocker with the compose file from the selected docker-compose process. Only enabled in docker mode. - **mprocs config compat**: Added `cmd`, `stop`, `hide_keymap_window`, and `proc_list_width` fields. `stop` supports `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL`/`hard-kill`. Config is now passed as a struct to the TUI so all fields are wired through. - **Info panel**: Scroll indicator hidden in info mode. Warning banner spans full viewport width with `ColorBarMid` background. ## How did you test this code? 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ - [x] `go build ./...` compiles - [x] `go vet ./...` passes - [x] All tests pass (`go test ./...`) - [x] `TestReadLoop_batchesOutput` verifies 500 lines coalesce into far fewer OutputMsgs - [x] New config tests cover `cmd`, `stop` signals, `hide_keymap_window`, `proc_list_width` - [x] Manual: run `bin/start`, verify sidebar CPU bars render and update - [x] Manual: select a high-memory process, verify ⚠ icon appears and info panel shows warning banner - [x] Manual: press `p` on a running process, verify htop opens filtered to its PID - [x] Manual: select docker-compose, press `d`, verify lazydocker opens ```sh for i in $(seq 1 1000); do curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST http://localhost:8010/e/ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"event":"test_event","properties":{"distinct_id":"test","token":"sTMFPsFhdP1Ssg"},"timestamp":"2026-03-23T00:00:00Z"}' & done; wait ``` ## Publish to changelog? no ## Docs update skip-inkeep-docs
author: gantoine

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR enhances the `phrocs` TUI to better handle high-volume PTY output, improve sidebar observability (CPU bars + high-memory warnings), add external tooling shortcuts (process viewer / lazydocker), and expand mprocs config compatibility by wiring more config through to the TUI/process runner.

**Changes:**
- Batch PTY output notifications to reduce Bubble Tea message backpressure and keep child processes responsive.
- Add CPU usage “background bar” rendering in the sidebar and a high-RSS warning icon/banner.
- Extend config support (`cmd`, `stop`, `hide_keymap_window`, `proc_list_width`) and add `p`/`d` key actions.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tools/phrocs/main.go | Updates TUI constructor call to pass full config. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/styles.go | Adds warning icon styling and CPU usage bar rendering in sidebar rows. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/search.go | Removes per-line OutputMsg incremental helper; keeps per-line helper for docker log streaming. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/render.go | Adds high-memory warning behavior and respects configurable sidebar width. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/keys.go | Adds proc viewer and lazydocker key handlers + command selection logic. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/keymap.go | Adds new keybindings (`d`, `p`) and help integration. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/docker.go | Adapts sidebar row rendering signature for containers. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/app.go | Wires new config fields into Model; adjusts sizing, footer behavior, and key enabling. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/tui/app_test.go | Updates tests for new TUI constructor signature and OutputMsg semantics. |
| tools/phrocs/internal/process/proc.go | Implements OutputMsg batching, adds metrics accessors, supports `cmd` + configurable s

works great! only thing is that I instinctively want to `r` (restart) the processes that have the warning symbol (even though they are running fine) 😅 a bit stranger to see the warning symbol when the CPU pressure gauge is low, like here

commits.message:
fix(phrocs): batch PTY output to prevent backpressure throttling services

readLoop was sending one OutputMsg per line through bubbletea's message
queue. Each message triggered a full viewport rebuild, and when the queue
backed up, send() blocked the read loop, filling the PTY buffer and
causing the child process to block on stdout writes — visibly throttling
services like capture.

Decouple reading from UI notifications: a scanner goroutine reads lines
into a buffered channel as fast as possible, and the main loop flushes a
single OutputMsg every ~16ms. Burst output that previously generated
thousands of individual messages now coalesces into ~62 updates/second.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
display cpu usage as background in sidebar
add p key for htop view
add high ram usage status icon

When heatmaps aren't enabled on a project, the heatmaps page shows a warning banner with a "Configure" button. Clicking it opens a settings side panel, but it navigates to the **Autocapture** settings section instead of the **Heatmaps** section. No

No docs update needed.

title: feat(mcp): descriptions for the insight tools
body:
## Problem

The query wrapper tools (trends, funnel, retention) had brief, generic descriptions that didn't provide sufficient guidance for AI agents on how to properly use these tools. This made it difficult for agents to understand the specific capabilities, requirements, and best practices for each query type.

## Changes

- Added comprehensive prompt files for each query wrapper tool:
  - `query-trends.md` - Detailed guidance for trends queries including aggregation types, formulas, breakdowns, and time intervals
  - `query-funnel.md` - Complete funnel analysis instructions covering conversion rates, exclusion steps, and step ordering
  - `query-retention.md` - Retention query guidelines for analyzing user return behavior and engagement

- Enhanced the YAML configuration system to support external description files:
  - Added `description_file` field to tool configurations as an alternative to inline `description`
  - Updated validation to ensure `description` and `description_file` are mutually exclusive
  - Modified the generation script to resolve file-based descriptions relative to the YAML file location

- Updated `query-wrappers.yaml` to reference the new prompt files instead of using brief inline descriptions

- Regenerated tool definition schemas to include the comprehensive descriptions

Each prompt file includes:
- Clear use case examples
- Detailed property filtering guidelines with supported operators
- Specific configuration options and best practices
- Critical reminders about requirements and limitations
- Minimalist approach guidance to avoid over-complication

## How did you test this code?

I verified the changes through:
- Code review of the TypeScript generation script to ensure proper file resolution logic
- Validation that the YAML schema correctly enforces mutual exclusivity between `description` and `description_file`
- Confirmation that the generated JSON files contain the full prompt content from the markdown files
- Review of the prompt files to ensure they provide comprehensive guidance while maintaining clarity

As an AI agent, I have not performed manual testing of the actual query execution, but the code changes follow established patterns and maintain backward compatibility.

## Publish to changelog?

No - this is an internal improvement to AI agent tooling that doesn't affect end-user functionality.

## Docs update

Skip - these are internal prompt improvements for AI agents, not user-facing documentation changes.
author: skoob13

commits.message: Merge branch 'feat/code-generated-tools-prompts' of github.com:PostHog/posthog into feat/code-generated-tools-prompts

title: fix(metalytics): fix metalytics 5xxs
body:
## Problem

Metalytics endpoint is the single largest source of 504s across platform features endpoints. 

The metalytics frontend fires 3 requests simultaneously per page view:
- A `POST /metalytics/` (viewed metric) — **awaited** despite being fire-and-forget telemetry
- Two HogQL queries with `force_blocking` — each ties up a Django worker until ClickHouse returns

## Changes

- Switch both HogQL queries from `force_blocking` to `async` refresh — returns cached results instantly, refreshes in background
- Make the viewed metric POST fire-and-forget (`void` instead of `await`) — response is unused

## How did you test this code?

Code-only change with no behavioral impact beyond caching strategy. View counts may be a few minutes stale instead of real-time, which is acceptable for internal telemetry.

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: yasen-posthog

commits.message:
fix(metalytics): reduce worker pressure from metalytics requests

- Switch HogQL queries from force_blocking to async refresh to avoid
  tying up Django workers waiting on ClickHouse
- Make the viewed metric POST fire-and-forget (void instead of await)
  since the response is unused telemetry

title: fix(replay): use SECRET_KEY env var instead of DJANGO_SECRET_KEY
body:
## Summary

- Renames `DJANGO_SECRET_KEY` → `SECRET_KEY` in the recording-rasterizer config and dev startup script
- Companion to PostHog/charts PR for the Helm values fix

The posthog-app chart's ExternalSecret template creates the k8s secret key using the env var name (`DJANGO_SECRET_KEY`), but the deployment template mounts it using the property name (`SECRET_KEY`). This mismatch causes `CreateContainerConfigError` on all rasterizer pods. Using `SECRET_KEY` for both sides resolves it.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter=@posthog/nodejs build` passes
- [x] Verified no other references to `DJANGO_SECRET_KEY` in rasterizer code
