title: fix(dashboard): fixes how overrides are determined on the dashboard for insights
body: ## Problem Insight tiles on dashboards could show **“Discard overrides to edit the insight”** (e.g. on the legend) even when the dashboard date control looked like there was **no meaningful override**. That was confusing next to copy like **“No date range override.”** ### Root cause `hasOverrides` in `insightLogic` (and the matching logic in `insightSceneLogic`) treated any non-`{}` filter object as an override using **`!isEmptyObject()`**. Tile/dashboard filter payloads can still carry keys with **no real constraint** (e.g. `{ explicitDate: false }`, or null-ish date fields). Those objects are **semantically empty** but not `Object.keys`-empty, so we incorrectly set `hasOverrides` and blocked editing affordances. ## Changes - Add `isDashboardFilterEmpty` in `frontend/src/scenes/dashboard/dashboardFilterEmpty.ts`, matching how insight URL state already defines “empty” filters. - Use it for `hasOverrides` in `insightLogic` and for `hasOverrides` + URL `setSceneState` normalization in `insightSceneLogic`. - Keep the helper under **dashboard** (filter payload semantics), with insights importing it where needed. Before: Visit local url `http://localhost:8010/project/1/dashboard/1?query_filters=%7B%22date_from%22%3Anull%2C%22date_to%22%3Anull%2C%22explicitDate%22%3Afalse%7D%20` - Create query filters by setting a date range override for the page, and then removing them. - Observe that after removing the query filters, we "null" the fields in the query filters - Observe that even with no overrides for the dashboard or tile set, the tooltips still are indicating that theres filters enabled, and we cant update the legend status After: With "null" filters enabled, we dont see the tooltip. Observe when dashboard filters are enabled, its shows disabled correctly Observe when insight overrides are enabled, it shows disabled correctly ## How did you test this code? Locally. ## Publish to changelog? No.
author: MattPua

commits.message: fix(dashboard): fixes how overrides are determined on the dashboard for insights

title: fix(scim): skip nested group members in SCIM group sync
body:
## Problem

Customers using Entra ID with nested groups get 400 errors in their provisioning logs. When a parent group contains nested sub-groups, Entra ID sends the nested group's SCIM ID as a member reference. 
The `User.objects.get(id=<uuid>)` throws a `ValueError` (not `User.DoesNotExist`) because user.id is an integer field. 

[More context](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C09B6HYA6Q5/p1773326607369709?thread_ts=1762279729.310209&cid=C09B6HYA6Q5). 

## Changes

No major SaaS provider supports SCIM nested groups, the spec allows but doesn't require it. So...

Users from nested sub-groups are still provisioned individually and added to their own roles, only the parent-child group relationship is ignored. 

## How did you test this code?
Tests

## Publish to changelog?
No

## Docs update
No
author: a-lider

commits.message: fix(scim): skip nested group members in SCIM group sync

fix(scim): add warning log for skipped non-user member IDs

Split ValueError into separate except with logger.warning for
observability when non-user member references are skipped.

title: docs(internal): document hogli product:lint content validations
body:
## Summary

Documents the new content validations added to `hogli product:lint` in #51921.

## Changes

### `products/README.md`
Added documentation after the `hogli product:lint` command section explaining what the lint validates:
- **Presence**: `backend:test` must exist; isolated products must also have `backend:contract-check`
- **Absence**: Non-isolated products must NOT have `backend:contract-check` (prevents turbo-discover misclassification)
- **Script content** checks:
  - No `|| true` or `|| exit 0` (swallow test failures)
  - No no-op scripts when test files exist
  - Pytest paths must exist and contain tests

### `products/architecture.md`
Added a bullet point clarifying that non-isolated products must **not** declare `backend:contract-check`, as turbo-discover uses this key to identify isolated products.

## Related
- Follows #51921

docs: document non-isolated product backend:contract-check restriction

PR #51921 added a validation in `hogli product:lint` that non-isolated
products must NOT declare `backend:contract-check` in their package.json.
This key is used by turbo-discover to classify products as isolated, and
its presence on a non-isolated product causes the full Django test suite
to be silently skipped.

title: feat(feature_flags): enable 6 missing MCP tools for feature flags
body:
## Problem

The feature flags product has several useful API endpoints that aren't exposed through the MCP server, limiting what agents can do with feature flags. Specifically, agents can't check flag health, inspect dependency chains, assess rollout impact, debug evaluation, view audit trails, or copy flags across projects.

## Changes

Enable 6 previously disabled MCP tools by adding the necessary serializers, `@extend_schema` annotations, and YAML configuration:

- **`feature-flags-status-retrieve`** — check flag health and evaluation status
- **`feature-flags-dependent-flags-retrieve`** — understand flag dependency chains before making changes
- **`feature-flags-user-blast-radius-create`** — assess impact before changing rollout conditions
- **`feature-flags-evaluation-reasons-retrieve`** — debug why a flag evaluates a certain way for a user
- **`feature-flags-activity-retrieve`** — audit trail for a flag
- **`feature-flags-copy-flags-create`** — cross-project flag management

All 6 endpoints already existed as Django views — the work here is wiring them into the MCP layer:
- Added request/response serializers where missing (`UserBlastRadiusRequestSerializer`, `CopyFlagsRequestSerializer`, etc.)
- Added `@extend_schema` decorators so drf-spectacular can generate proper OpenAPI specs
- Added missing `required_scopes` to `user_blast_radius`
- Fixed incorrect operation ID for `dependent_flags` (`_retrieve` → `_list`)
- Ran `hogli build:openapi` to regenerate all TypeScript types, Zod schemas, and MCP tool handlers

## How did you test this code?

- Verified `hogli build:openapi` completes successfully with all 4 pipeline steps passing
- Confirmed tool count increased from 70 → 76 (6 new tools)
- Verified `pnpm --filter=@posthog/mcp run lint-tool-names` passes (all names under 52-char limit)
- Verified `ruff check` passes on changed Python files
- Spot-checked generated TypeScript handlers for correct API paths and parameter wiring

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed — MCP tools are self-describing through the tool definitions.

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code. The implementation followed the [implementing MCP tools](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/master/docs/published/handbook/engineering/ai/implementing-mcp-tools.md) guide.

title: chore(devex): validate package.json script content in product lint
body:
**Problem**

`hogli product:lint` only checked that `backend:test` and `backend:contract-check` keys existed in `package.json` — it never looked at what they contained. This left a few dangerous failure modes undetected:

- A non-isolated product with `backend:contract-check` gets misclassified by `turbo-discover.js` as isolated, silently skipping the full Django test suite when that product changes
- A typo in the pytest path (e.g. `backend/typo_tests`) collects zero tests with no error
- A no-op script like `echo 'No backend tests'` hides the fact that test files actually exist
- `|| true` swallows pytest failures so CI stays green regardless

**Changes**

Four new content validations in `PackageJsonScriptsCheck`, plus 39 tests covering all presence and absence scenarios.

Also removes `|| true` from notifications `backend:test` (no test files exist there anyway — the suffix was unnecessary).
author: webjunkie

ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Pull request overview Enhances `hogli product:lint` so it validates not just the presence of `package.json` scripts, but also their contents, reducing the risk of products being misclassified or silently skipping backend tests in CI. **Changes:** - Add content validations to `PackageJsonScriptsCheck` (no `|| true/exit 0`, detect no-op scripts when tests exist, verify referenced pytest paths exist, forbid `backend:contract-check` on non-isolated products). - Add a focused test suite covering presence/absence/content scenarios for the check. - Update notifications’ `backend:test` script (removes `|| true` and extra flags) and clarify lint output text. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | products/notifications/package.json | Adjusts `backend:test` command (removes `|| true`). | | common/hogli/tests/test_checks.py | Adds unit tests for the new script content validations and helpers. | | common/hogli/product/lint.py | Updates CLI output string to reflect expanded script checks. | | common/hogli/product/checks.py | Implements new package.json script content validation helpers and logic. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
chore(devex): validate package.json script content in product lint

The existing PackageJsonScriptsCheck only verified that required script
keys existed — not what they actually contained. This allowed several
classes of silent CI failures to slip through undetected.

New validations:
- Non-isolated products must not have backend:contract-check (turbo-discover
  uses this key to classify products as isolated, which can cause the full
  Django test suite to be skipped when the product changes)
- Pytest test paths must exist on disk (catches typos that silently
  collect zero tests)
- No-op scripts (echo) flag an issue when test files actually exist
  under backend/
- || true / || exit 0 suffixes flag as swallowing test failures in CI

Also fixes notifications product which had || true on its backend:test
script (no actual test files exist, so it was harmless but wrong).

39 new tests in test_checks.py cover all presence and absence scenarios.

fix(devex): address PR review feedback

- Remove --co from value-consuming flags list (it's --collect-only,
  takes no argument)
- Interpolate actual tail in || detection message (|| true vs || exit 0)
- Use echo no-op for notifications backend:test since it has no test
  files (pytest would exit code 5, breaking CI)

title: fix(notebooks): use controlled query state when setQuery prop is provided
body:
## Summary

- Fixes the "Use person properties from query time" toggle not working when editing an insight from within a notebook (detached insights)
- The root cause was a race condition in the `Query` component: when `setQuery` prop was provided (controlled mode), it still read from stale `localQuery` state instead of `propsQuery`, causing `insightDataLogic.propsChanged` to reset the Kea state with the old query (without the modifier), which then cascaded through `poeFilterLogic` to reset the toggle

## How does this change work?

The `Query` component maintains a `localQuery` via `useState` for uncontrolled usage (when no `setQuery` prop is provided). When `setQuery` IS provided, the parent owns the state, but the component was still reading from `localQuery` (synced via `useEffect`, which is asynchronous). This created a window where stale state could override Kea's internal state.

The fix is a one-line change: when `propsSetQuery` is provided, use `propsQuery` directly (same as the `readOnly` path), since the parent is the source of truth.

## Test plan

- [ ] Open a notebook
- [ ] Add an insight and detach it from the original
- [ ] Edit the insight → Advanced options → Toggle "Use person properties from query time" ON
- [ ] Verify the toggle stays ON and the query updates accordingly
- [ ] Verify regular insight editing outside notebooks still works
- [ ] Verify insight editing on dashboards still works

Closes #51776
author: posthog

commits.message:
fix(notebooks): use controlled query state when setQuery prop is provided

When the Query component receives a setQuery prop, the parent is the
source of truth for query state. Previously, the component used stale
localQuery (from React useState) even when setQuery was provided, which
caused a race condition in notebooks:

1. PoeFilter toggle updates Kea state with new modifiers
2. Kea calls props.setQuery → notebook updates attributes
3. React re-renders, but Query's localQuery is still stale
4. InsightViz receives the OLD query via stale localQuery
5. insightDataLogic.propsChanged resets internalQuery to OLD query
6. poeFilterLogic subscription sees modifiers disappeared → resets toggle

The fix treats the component as "controlled" when propsSetQuery is
provided, using propsQuery directly instead of the stale localQuery.

Closes #51776

title: chore(replay): standalone headless player and rasterizer
body:
## Problem

Session replay video export needs a standalone, headless rasterization pipeline that can run as a Temporal worker — separate from the Django exporter. This enables the AI summary pipeline and future video export features to render session replays to MP4 without depending on the Django request cycle.

## Changes

**Standalone headless player** (`common/replay-headless/`):
- Refactored the browser-side player into a clean protocol-driven architecture: `HostBridge` (in-browser) communicates with `PlayerController` (Node.js) via typed `PlayerMessage` events
- Extracted `PlaybackController`, `ViewportScaler`, `MetadataFooter`, and `ReplayerFactory` as independent modules
- Added `DataLoadError` with retryable classification and per-block retry with exponential backoff
- Runtime validation on block listing API responses

**Rasterizer worker** (`nodejs/src/session-replay/recording-rasterizer/`):
- Restructured into `capture/` (browser-pool, player, capture, recorder) and `temporal/` (worker, activities, codec) subdirectories
- Deterministic virtual-time capture using `HeadlessExperimental.beginFrame` with inline `setpts` filter — single-pass video encoding, no rAF timing dependency
- `BrowserPool` with usage-based recycling, `PlayerController` with timeout/error/progress handling and `dispose()` for cleanup
- Fernet-compatible `EncryptionCodec` matching Python's `EncryptionCodec` for cross-language Temporal payload encryption
- Structured logging via pino, comprehensive Prometheus metrics, robust error classification with `retryable` + `code` fields
- `playerHtmlCache` object replacing fragile module-level mutable singleton

**Python workflow** (`posthog/temporal/rasterize_recording/`):
- `RasterizeRecordingWorkflow`: accepts `exported_asset_id`, reads options from `ExportedAsset.export_context`, dispatches to Node.js activity via cross-language Temporal, finalizes asset with S3 location and metadata
- `build_rasterization_input` and `finalize_rasterization` activities with proper validation (missing session_id, s3_uri prefix mismatch)
- Registered in `start_temporal_worker`, `execute_temporal_workflow`, and `start_temporal_workflow` management commands
- All numeric fields (`playback_speed`, `trim`, `video_duration_s`) use `float` to match TypeScript side

**Tests:**
- 15+ Node.js test files (126 tests): `HostBridge`, `PlaybackController`, `ViewportScaler`, `buildCaptureConfig`, `buildPlayerConfig`, `computeVideoTimestamps`, `BrowserPool`, `EncryptionCodec`, `validateInput`, `capturePlayback`, `PlayerController`, activity error classification, integration
- 10 Python tests: `build_rasterization_input` (happy path, defaults, missing session_id, null context, fractional values), `finalize_rasterization` (happy path, wrong s3 prefix, null context)

## How did you test this code?

- Full end-to-end local test: created `ExportedAsset` → started Python workflow → Node.js rasterizer rendered video → finalized asset with `content_location` and metadata
- Tested at both default and 1920x1080 resolution

title: chore: wire worker_threads var to kafka-deduplicator
body:
## Problem

The kafka-deduplicator's `worker_threads` config (default: 4) was declared but never wired into the tokio runtime. The `#[tokio::main]` macro defaults to CPU core count, making the config ineffective. Additionally, RocksDB batch read/write operations ran directly on the async runtime, risking tokio worker thread starvation and causing random liveness probe failures.

## Changes

- Replace `#[tokio::main]` with an explicit `tokio::runtime::Builder` that uses `config.worker_threads` to size the runtime
- Wrap `batch_read_timestamp_records` and `batch_write_timestamp_records` in `spawn_blocking` to keep RocksDB I/O off the async worker threads

title: feat: chat title comes in earlier, scene tab title get's updated (when tab inactive)
body:
## Problem
1. Title only appeared after streaming finished — The conversation title (generated by a fast LLM call running in parallel) was only sent to the frontend when loadConversation fired after streaming completed. Users saw "New chat" for the entire duration of a response.
2. Tab showed "AI" instead of "New chat" — When starting a new conversation, the tab/breadcrumb showed "AI" as the scene name, giving no indication it was a fresh chat.
3. Inactive tab titles never updated — The titleAndIcon subscription in sceneLogic only tracks the active scene. If you asked a question and switched tabs, the AI tab would stay "New chat" forever, even after the title was generated.

## Changes
> Image shows the inactive tab getting it's title updated after i switch tabs (it's so fast locally i have to press new tab almost immediately after enter) 
![2026-03-23 11 38 32](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6251a901-8ea3-4063-8d4b-37ef117d3f15)

**TLDR**: New chat scene tab title by default, after submitting title comes in super fast.. ai chat titles update on inactive tabs. 

Backend (4 files, +39 lines)

Stream the title as soon as it's generated, rather than waiting for the client to poll after streaming ends:

- ee/hogai/utils/types/base.py — New ConversationTitleAction model added to AssistantActionUnion
- ee/hogai/core/title_generator/nodes.py — After saving the title to DB, emits ConversationTitleAction via LangGraph's custom stream writer
- ee/hogai/utils/helpers.py — extract_stream_update recognizes ConversationTitleAction to prevent tuple-unpacking errors
- ee/hogai/core/runner.py — _process_update detects ConversationTitleAction, sets the title on the in-memory conversation and a _pending_conversation_update flag. The astream loop checks the flag and re-yields a CONVERSATION SSE event with the updated title.

Frontend (1 file, +25 lines)

- Breadcrumbs — Show "New chat" (not "AI") when no conversation is
active; extracted CHAT_TITLE_NEW / CHAT_TITLE_HISTORY constants
- Inactive tab subscription — New chatTitle subscription calls updateInactiveTab when a real title arrives, so background AI tabs get their titles updated
- Search → AI navigation — Calls startNewConversation() (with a streaming guard) when navigating to /ai?ask=... with an existing conversation open, so the old conversation is cleared

Tests (2 files, +141 lines)

- Backend — Title generator emits action to stream writer; runner sets pending flag and yields CONVERSATION event
- Frontend — chatTitle selector returns correct values for all states; breadcrumbs show "New chat" vs "AI" correctly

## How did you test this code?
Locally

## Publish to changelog?
Yes

## Docs update
No

title: fix(ci): remove invalid timeout-minutes from reusable workflow jobs
body:
## Problem

`timeout-minutes` is not a valid key on jobs that call reusable workflows via `uses:`. GitHub Actions treats these as regular jobs when it sees `timeout-minutes`, then fails because `runs-on` is missing and `uses`/`with`/`secrets` are unexpected.

This is the same class of bug fixed in #51930 and #51832 for the Rust workflows — these four files were also affected.

## Changes

Remove invalid `timeout-minutes` from reusable workflow jobs in:
- `call-flags-project-board.yml`
- `ci-hobby.yml`
- `ci-survey-sdk-check.yml`
- `pr-closed.yml`

Timeouts are handled inside the called workflows themselves.

## Related PRs

- #51930 — same fix for `rust-docker-build.yml` (merged)
- #51832 — same fix for `rust-smoke-test-build.yml` (open)
- #51936 — duplicate of #51930 (closed)

commits.message: fix(ci): remove invalid timeout-minutes from reusable workflow jobs

title: feat: extend query coalescing to include force_blocking mode
body: ## Problem When users send `force_blocking` queries, identical concurrent requests all hit ClickHouse independently. Currently, query coalescing only applies to the `blocking` execution mode and has been fully rolled out since 03-17-2026. https://grafana.prod-us.posthog.dev/d/af9h9hoy427swd/query-cache?folderUid=ae39cxpeaic5cd&orgId=1&from=2026-03-19T17:39:23.486Z&to=2026-03-19T23:39:23.486Z&timezone=browser&refresh=5s&var-teamid=2&showCategory=Panel%20options&viewPanel=panel-26 ## Changes - Expanded the coalescing guard in `_try_coalesce` to also accept the `force_blocking` execution mode - Changed `_try_coalesce` return type to `tuple[Optional[Response], ExecutionMode]` so followers can have their execution mode downgraded - When a `force_blocking` follower receives the `DONE` signal, its execution mode is downgraded to `blocking` so it reads the cached result the leader just wrote instead of recalculating ## How did you test this code? - Added a test verifying the force_blocking follower path - Manually tested locally by sending duplicate queries and verified metrics showed coalescing ## Publish to changelog? No ## Docs update N/A

Followers using force_blocking now participate in query coalescing. When a
follower receives the DONE signal, its execution mode is downgraded to

recalculating against ClickHouse independently.

title: feat(alerts): add checks_date_from and checks_limit params to alert retrieve
body:
## Problem

The alert-get API endpoint hardcodes returning only the last 5 check results. This makes it impossible to query alert firing history over a time window (e.g. the last 24 hours) via the API or MCP tools — the only workaround is to re-simulate the detector, which produces approximate rather than actual historical results.

## Changes

Add three optional query parameters to the alert retrieve endpoint:

- `checks_date_from`: relative date string (e.g. `-24h`, `-7d`) for the start of the check history window
- `checks_date_to`: relative date string (e.g. `-1h`, `-1d`) for the end of the window (defaults to now)
- `checks_limit`: max number of check results to return (default 5, max 500)

When date filters are provided without `checks_limit`, up to 500 checks are returned (covering the full time window). Without any params, behavior is unchanged (last 5).

Updated the MCP tool description for `alert-get` to document the new parameters and the 14-day retention window.

## How did you test this code?

5 new unit tests covering:
- Default limit (5) is preserved
- Explicit `checks_limit` param
- `checks_date_from` time-based filtering
- `checks_date_from` + `checks_date_to` window filtering
- `checks_limit` is capped at 500

```
pytest posthog/api/test/test_alert.py::TestAlert::test_retrieve_checks_default_limit
pytest posthog/api/test/test_alert.py::TestAlert::test_retrieve_checks_with_limit
pytest posthog/api/test/test_alert.py::TestAlert::test_retrieve_checks_with_date_from
pytest posthog/api/test/test_alert.py::TestAlert::test_retrieve_checks_with_date_from_and_date_to
pytest posthog/api/test/test_alert.py::TestAlert::test_retrieve_checks_limit_capped_at_max
```

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed — the OpenAPI spec and MCP tool descriptions are updated inline.

feat(alerts): add checks_date_from and checks_limit params to alert retrieve

The alert-get endpoint previously hardcoded returning only the last 5
check results. This makes it impossible to query alert firing history
over a time window (e.g. last 24 hours) via the API or MCP tools.

Add two optional query parameters to the retrieve endpoint:
- checks_date_from: relative date string (e.g. '-24h', '-7d') to filter
  checks by creation time
- checks_limit: max checks to return (default 5, max 500)

When checks_date_from is provided without checks_limit, up to 500 checks
are returned. Without either param, behavior is unchanged (last 5).

feat(alerts): add checks_date_to param for explicit time windows

Add checks_date_to alongside checks_date_from so callers can query a
specific window of check history (e.g. checks_date_from=-7d&checks_date_to=-1d).
Defaults to now when not specified, preserving existing behavior.

  negative and zero values

title: fix(ci): filter timing update workflow to master branch runs only
body:
## Summary
- The weekly timing update workflow's GitHub API call didn't include a `branch=master` filter, so it returned PR runs first (which are more frequent). PR runs never upload timing artifacts (upload condition requires `refs/heads/master`), causing the workflow to find 0 artifacts on all 5 candidate runs and fail.
- Adds `&branch=master` to the API query to only consider master branch runs
- Increases timing artifact `retention-days` from 2 → 7 as a safety net for the weekly Sunday schedule

Fixes https://app.mendral.com/insights/01KKQTWTCVY8SKE7DXKGD36Z9W

The timing update workflow's GitHub API call didn't include a branch
filter, so it picked up PR runs (which never upload timing artifacts

title: chore: simplify Stripe service catalog to free-only
body:
## Problem

The Stripe Projects service catalog currently includes a `pay_as_you_go` paid plan and a `free` plan, with the `analytics` deployable as a component of both. When users run `stripe projects upgrade posthog/analytics`, Stripe collects payment and tries to call `update_service` - but the SPT billing integration isn't complete yet, so the upgrade fails.

## Changes

- Remove `free` and `pay_as_you_go` plan services from the catalog
- Simplify `analytics` deployable from a component (with free/paid options) to a simple free service
- Update `VALID_SERVICE_IDS` to only include `analytics`
- Update tests to reflect simplified catalog

The catalog now returns a single service:
```json
{
  "id": "analytics",
  "description": "PostHog - ...",
  "categories": ["analytics", "feature_flags", "ai"],
  "pricing": {"type": "free"},
  "kind": "deployable"
}
```

Plans will be re-added once SPT billing integration is complete (see #51946).

## How did you test this code?

- Updated unit tests in `test_services.py`, `test_resources.py`, `test_rotate_credentials.py`
- Verified syntax and linting pass

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Code.
author: MattBro

reviews.body:
If you look at the other providers, is "analytics" a good name? We offer way more than that. I'm also afraid agents will puck the "analytics" bit too heavily.

Can we instead call it `posthog` or possibly `base`? Also, how does it work with already-provisioned services. Will Stripe get lost? Will it know how to upgrade? Should we communicate this change to them?

commits.message:
chore: simplify Stripe service catalog to free-only analytics service

Remove the pay_as_you_go plan and free plan from the service catalog.
The catalog now returns a single free deployable (analytics) instead of
two plans + a component deployable. This prevents users from hitting
the upgrade flow before SPT billing integration is complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(alerts): add window param to all PyOD detectors to fix false positive anomaly alerts
body:
## Problem

PyOD-based anomaly detectors (isolation_forest, knn, lof, ocsvm, pca, ecod, copod, hbos) were firing false positive alerts because the alert checker fetched far too little historical data. The `DETECTOR_MIN_SAMPLES` guard (e.g. 10 for isolation forest) was accidentally the *only* thing driving the data fetch size, causing models to train on as few as 9 data points and producing garbage anomaly scores (score=1.0 on perfectly normal data).

Meanwhile, the simulation endpoint used the insight's full date range (e.g. 78 hourly points), producing completely different and correct results. This meant alerts would fire but simulating the same config showed no anomaly.

**Root cause:** Unlike the statistical detectors (zscore, mad, iqr) which have an explicit `window` parameter that drives data fetching, the PyOD detectors had no such parameter. The `min_samples` guard was serving double duty as "how much data to fetch" instead of just being a lower bound.

## Changes

- Add `window` field to all 8 PyOD detector config schemas (TypeScript + Python)
- Add `WindowSizeInput` to all PyOD detector config UI components in `DetectorSelector.tsx`
- Include interval-based defaults matching existing zscore/mad/iqr behavior (hourly=168, daily=90, weekly=26, monthly=12)
- Extract shared `_compute_min_samples_for_detector()` helper used by both the alert checker and simulation endpoint so they always agree
- Factor preprocessing config (`lags_n`, `diffs_n`) into the min_samples calculation since these consume usable training data
- Keep `DETECTOR_MIN_SAMPLES` as a pure lower-bound guard, not the data fetch driver

**Before/After for hourly isolation_forest with preprocessing (diffs=1, lags=3):**
| | min_samples (data points fetched) |
|---|---|
| Before | 10 (trained on 9 points) |
| After (no window set) | 35 (default window 30 + headroom) |
| After (window=168) | 173 (7 days of hourly data + headroom) |

## How did you test this code?

- All 105 existing detector tests pass (`pytest posthog/tasks/alerts/detectors/`)
- Verified `_compute_min_samples_for_detector()` output for isolation_forest, zscore, threshold, ecod, and lof configs
- Regenerated schemas via `hogli build:schema` and `hogli build:openapi`
- I am an agent and have not manually tested the UI changes

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

no
author: andrewm4894

commits.message:
fix(alerts): add window param to all PyOD detectors to fix false positive anomaly alerts

PyOD-based anomaly detectors (isolation_forest, knn, lof, etc.) were
firing false positives because the alert checker fetched far too little
historical data. The DETECTOR_MIN_SAMPLES guard (e.g. 10 for isolation
forest) was accidentally the only thing driving the data fetch size,
causing models to train on as few as 9 data points — producing garbage
scores. Meanwhile, the simulation endpoint used the insight's full date
range (e.g. 78 points), producing completely different (correct) results.

- Add `window` field to all 8 PyOD detector config schemas (TS + Python)
- Add WindowSizeInput to all PyOD detector config UI components
- Include interval-based defaults (hourly=168, daily=90, weekly=26, monthly=12)
- Extract shared `_compute_min_samples_for_detector()` helper used by
  both the alert checker and simulation endpoint
- Factor preprocessing config (lags_n, diffs_n) into min_samples calc
- Keep DETECTOR_MIN_SAMPLES as a pure lower-bound guard

chore(alerts): extract DEFAULT_THRESHOLD constant (0.95) for all detectors

Replaces duplicated 0.9 threshold literals with a single constant in
both frontend (DEFAULT_THRESHOLD) and backend (BaseDetector.DEFAULT_THRESHOLD).
Bumps default from 0.9 to 0.95 to reduce false positive noise.
chore(alerts): fix misleading comment and add parameterized tests for _compute_min_samples_for_detector

title: feat(llma): add engagement and impression tracking to sentiment cards
body:
## Problem

We need implicit feedback signals from the sentiment tab to train a `p_interesting` ranking model that will eventually order message cards by relevance.

## Changes

- Capture `llma sentiment card engaged` events when a user expands a card or clicks "View trace" (positive training examples)
- Capture `llma sentiment card impressed` events for a random sample of up to 5 other visible cards per interaction (negative training examples)
- All events from the same interaction share an `interaction_id` for joining in the training pipeline
- Events include `message_text_snippet` (up to 2000 chars) for embedding-based features, plus model predictions, card position, and filter state

## How did you test this code?

Code review of event capture logic. The tracking is additive (new `posthog.capture` calls in existing kea listeners) with no behavioral changes to existing functionality.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: andrewm4894

commits.message:
feat(llma): add engagement and impression tracking to sentiment cards

Capture positive (expand/trace click) and negative (impression) signals
on sentiment cards for training a p_interesting ranking model.

fix(llma): add filter state to impressed events

Include sentiment_filter and intensity_threshold in impressed events
to match engaged events, as the filter context matters for training.

title: feat(workflows): Add response value filtering to survey triggers
body: ## Problem Came from [here](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C07TQR0V16U/p1774033933885599). Users want to trigger workflows only for specific survey responses (e.g., NPS score of 6, or a particular answer to a PMF question), but the survey trigger only lets you pick a survey and choose completed vs partial responses. The workaround is to trigger on any response and use conditional branches, but this wastes workflow invocations and adds unnecessary complexity. ## Changes - Added "Only trigger for specific answers" section to the survey trigger UI with property filters - Added quick-add buttons showing each survey question - clicking one pre-populates a filter for that question's response property ($survey_response, $survey_response_1, etc.) - Buttons show truncated question text with full text in a tooltip on hover - Link-type questions are excluded from the quick-add buttons (they don't produce responses) - Question indices are preserved correctly even when link questions are skipped - Improved the taxonomy label for $survey_response_N properties: `Survey Response Question ID: 1` -> `Survey response for 2nd question` - No backend changes needed - the filter compilation pipeline already supports arbitrary event property filters ## How did you test this code? - Added unit tests covering the new changes - Manually tested by sending survey events via the capture API and verifying trigger match/mismatch behavior with different response values ## Publish to changelog? No
author: dmarchuk

reviews.body: Looks good to me, makes sense to offer this option and not just have the customer use conditionals

title: fix: change session_id type to str in QueryTags
body:
## Problem

`POST /login` returns 500 when a request contains a malformed `session_id` query parameter. 

The `QueryTags` model types `session_id` as `Optional[uuid.UUID]` with `validate_assignment=True`, so any non-UUID string causes an unhandled `ValidationError` that crashes the request. This produced 134 Django errors over 48h ([details](https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0AMG1GR4VA/p1773838721887289?thread_ts=1773838699.486789&cid=C0AMG1GR4VA))

## Changes
 - Change `session_id` to `Optional[str]` in `QueryTags`, matching how the rest of the codebase treats session IDs

## How did you test this code?

Tests

## Publish to changelog?
No

## Docs update
No
author: a-lider

commits.message: fix: change session_id type to str in QueryTags

title: fix(cdp): Use truthy check for distinct_id in batch workflow person lookup and postHogCapture
body: ## Problem Batch workflow executions (e.g. sending emails to a matched audience) broke after #49658 (Lazy load persons). Two bugs caused person data to never load for batch invocations. Batch invocations set `distinct_id: ''` (empty string) because there's no real event - they're triggered for a list of persons. The lazy loading code used `??` (nullish coalescing) which doesn't catch empty strings, only `null`/`undefined`. Before: After: ## Changes - `cdp-cyclotron-worker-hogflow.consumer.ts`: Changed `??` to `||` so empty `distinct_id` falls through to `personId`. This restores person lookup for batch invocations. - `hog-executor.service.ts`: Added `globals.person?.id` as a fallback for `postHogCapture` distinct_id resolution. Without this, any workflow step using `postHogCapture` after the email action throws "missing distinct_id". ## How did you test this code? - Added unit tests for both bug fixes - Manually triggered batch workflows with an email action - before the fix emails failed, after the fix they sent successfully ## Publish to changelog? No
author: dmarchuk

reviews.body: unblocking

title: chore(mcp): add feature/tool names validations
body:
## Problem

  MCP tool names had incomplete validation — only YAML-defined tools were checked for length (52 chars), while
  handwritten tools in JSON definitions and the runtime `TOOL_MAP` had no validation at all. There was also no
  character pattern validation anywhere, meaning invalid names could slip through and silently break in MCP
  clients like Cursor (which filters out tools exceeding its 60-char combined limit) or OpenAI API (which
  rejects names not matching `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`).

  ## Changes

  - **Added pattern validation constants** in `yaml-config-schema.ts`:
    - `TOOL_NAME_PATTERN` — enforces lowercase kebab-case (`[a-z0-9-]`, no leading/trailing hyphens)
    - `FEATURE_NAME_PATTERN` — enforces lowercase snake_case (`[a-z0-9_]`) for the `feature` field
  - **Added Zod schema validation** on `CategoryConfigSchema` — tool name keys and `feature` field are now
  regex-validated at build time
  - **Extended `lint-tool-names.ts`** to validate all three sources (YAML definitions, handwritten JSON
  definitions, generated JSON definitions) for both length and pattern
  - **Added vitest test** (`tool-name-validation.test.ts`) that validates every entry in `TOOL_MAP` and
  `GENERATED_TOOL_MAP` at test time — checks map key matches factory `.name`, plus length and pattern
  constraints
  - **Updated skill and handbook docs** with naming constraints, client compatibility table, and CI enforcement
   info

  ## How did you test this code?

  - `pnpm --filter=@posthog/mcp lint-tool-names` — passes with no violations
  - `pnpm --filter=@posthog/mcp test -- --run tests/unit/tool-name-validation.test.ts` — all tests pass
  - `pnpm --filter=@posthog/mcp typecheck` — no TypeScript errors

  ## Publish to changelog?

  No

  ## Docs update

  Internal docs updated in this PR (implementing-mcp-tools handbook page and skill).

  ## 🤖 LLM context

  Co-authored with Claude Code. Research on MCP client limits was provided by the author.
author: VojtechBartos

reviews.body: Should we also fix the existing tools in this PR? The CI will break, no?

title: fix(hogql): track used cte aliases
body:
# Problem

Queries that mixed `SELECT *` with additional unaliased expressions could fail in ClickHouse when the expanded `*` already exposed a generated column name matching the later expression.

In the reported case, a CTE produced an unaliased `toDate(period_end)` column, and the outer query also selected `toDate(period_end)`. Our ClickHouse printer auto-assigned the same synthetic alias to the later expression, which caused ClickHouse to reject the query because two different expressions shared the alias `toDate(period_end)`.

## Changes

Updated the ClickHouse printer to avoid auto-applying a generated alias to an unaliased call when that alias has already been used earlier in the same `SELECT`.

Added HogQL regression coverage for:

- a CTE with an unaliased expression selected through outer `SELECT *`
- the duplicate generated-name case from the bug report, where both the expanded column and a later expression resolve to `toDate(period_end)`

## How did you test this code?

CI tests

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

no
author: mariusandra

title: ci(browserslist): set HUSKY=0 at job level to skip pre-commit hooks
body:
<!-- mendral:session:action:01KK99QGDHGPYBFX4C37T22WJ9 -->
## Summary

- Adds `HUSKY: 0` as a job-level env var in the Browserslist scheduled workflow so the pre-commit hook is bypassed when `c2corg/browserslist-update-action` internally runs `git commit`.

## Context

The Browserslist workflow has been failing 100% of the time since Feb 2, 2026 (7 consecutive failures). The `c2corg/browserslist-update-action` runs `git commit` internally, which triggers the husky pre-commit hook → lint-staged → `bin/hogli format:yaml` → fails with `/usr/bin/env: 'uv': No such file or directory` because `uv` is not installed in this CI runner.

Setting `HUSKY: 0` at the job level is the established pattern in this repo (already used in `.github/workflows/update-ai-costs.yml`) and is inherited by all subprocesses including the external action's git commit.

Closes insight: Browserslist workflow: pre-commit hook fails due to missing uv

title: fix(ci): add healthcheck to Elasticsearch service in docker-compose.base.yml
body:
<!-- mendral:session:action:01KM5CHT8NX64X7Q9XRXHGPTRR -->
## Summary

Fixes a recurring CI reliability issue where the `temporal` container fails to start because Elasticsearch isn't ready when Temporal tries to connect.

**Root cause:** The `elasticsearch` service had no healthcheck defined. Temporal's `depends_on` used `service_started` (container exists), not `service_healthy` (container is ready). On resource-constrained CI runners, Elasticsearch's JVM startup can be slow — the container may exist but not yet be accepting connections, causing Temporal to exit immediately on connect failure.

**Changes:**
- Added a healthcheck to the `elasticsearch` service that polls `/_cluster/health` and waits for `green` or `yellow` status (up to 30 retries × 10s = 5 min, with a 60s start period for JVM init)
- Changed Temporal's `depends_on.elasticsearch.condition` from `service_started` → `service_healthy`

This ensures Docker Compose waits for Elasticsearch to be fully ready before starting Temporal, eliminating the race condition.

Closes insight: *Product tests: Temporal container never starts — Elasticsearch startup failure (widespread, recurring)*

Related CI runs: [23272542870](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/actions/runs/23272542870), [23237316641](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/actions/runs/23237316641)

The Link model was sitting in `posthog/models/` with a KLUDGE comment — management commands couldn't discover models in `products/`, so it was left behind. The product migration tooling (AppConfig + `db_table`) handles this properly now.

**Changes**
- `git mv` link.py to `products/links/backend/models/`
- Rewrite all imports (9 files) to new path
- `db_table = "posthog_link"` preserves existing table
- File system registration app_label updated from `posthog` to `links`
- Re-export removed from `posthog/models/__init__.py`
- SeparateDatabaseAndState migrations — no schema changes, just ContentType update
author: webjunkie

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Moves the Django `Link` model out of `posthog/models/` into the `products/links/backend` app while preserving the existing `posthog_link` table and updating ContentTypes/app labels so model discovery and file-system registration work correctly under the product app structure.

**Changes:**
- Relocate `Link` model to `products/links/backend/models/` and update Python imports across backend code/tests/admin.
- Preserve the existing DB table via `db_table = "posthog_link"` and introduce state-only model creation in the `links` app.
- Add a `posthog` migration to update `django_content_type` app_label from `posthog` → `links` and remove the model from `posthog` state.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| products/links/backend/test/test_api.py | Updates model import to new `products.links` location in API tests. |
| products/links/backend/models/link.py | Adds `db_table` to keep using the existing `posthog_link` table after app move. |
| products/links/backend/models/__init__.py | Exposes `Link` from the `models` package. |
| products/links/backend/migrations/max_migration.txt | Marks latest `links` app migration. |
| products/links/backend/migrations/0001_migrate_links_models.py | Adds state-only `Link` model definition in `links` app (no DB schema ops). |
| products/links/backend/api.py | Updates Link import for links API. |
| products/links/backend/admin.py | Updates Link import for Django admin registration. |
| posthog/test/test_link.py | Updates Link import path in core tests. |
| posthog/models/file_system/unfiled_file_saver.py | Updates Link import used in file-system mixin model registry. |
| posthog/models/__init__.py | Removes `Link` re-export from `posthog.models`. |
| posthog/migrations/max_migration.txt | Bumps max migration pointer to the new
Very nice! Thank you for your service 🖖

chore(links): move Link model to products/links/backend

The Link model was kept in posthog/models/ with a KLUDGE comment because
management commands couldn't discover models in the products/ directory.
The product migration tooling handles this properly via AppConfig and
db_table, so the workaround is no longer needed.

- git mv posthog/models/link.py to products/links/backend/models/
- Rewrite all imports (9 files) to new path
- Add db_table = "posthog_link" to preserve existing table
- Update file_system registration app_label from "posthog" to "links"
- Remove re-export from posthog/models/__init__.py
- SeparateDatabaseAndState migrations (no schema changes)
- ContentType update from posthog to links app

fix(links): make ContentType migration idempotent and multi-DB safe

Apply the same safeguards from the experiments migration (110ac8e):
handle duplicate ContentTypes when code deploys before migration runs,
and use schema_editor.connection.alias for multi-DB safety.

chore(links): merge master, renumber migration, move model test

- Resolve merge conflicts from master (admin imports, max_migration)
- Renumber posthog migration 1056 → 1060 to follow master's 1059
- Move posthog/test/test_link.py to products/links/backend/test/

chore(links): flatten models package to single models.py

Only one model, no need for a directory. Simplifies imports from
`products.links.backend.models.link` to `products.links.backend.models`.

Master advanced to 1061. Rebase dependency chain accordingly.

title: feat: support ai langgraph
body: Replaced the 4-phase RAG pipeline (refine → retrieve → generate → validate) with a LangGraph agent that reuses PHAI stack: SupportAgentGraph, SupportAgentModeManager, focused tools (read_taxonomy, read_data, search, execute_sql), and SupportAgentPromptBuilder for system prompts and CoreMemory/billing context. Ticket and session context are passed as a ContextMessage, the runner is stateless (use_checkpointer=False) and sync (SupportAgentRunner.run()), and each suggest_reply creates a Conversation (type=TOOL_CALL) for tracing. Support AI code now lives under products/conversations/backend/ai/ (suggest.py entry point); old pipeline files, schemas, and obsolete tests were removed. Note: it'll work in the background in the end, so right now that button is only for us to test

title: fix(limiters): replace fixed sleeps with polling in rate limiter integration tests
body:
**Problem**
All integration tests in `global_rate_limiter_integration_tests.rs` use fixed sleeps (300-500ms) before asserting on Redis state. The background pipeline flush can take up to ~800ms under CI load (100ms tick + 500ms write timeout + task startup latency), causing intermittent failures.

**Fix**
Replace all 14 fixed-sleep sites with a `poll_assert!` macro that retries every 100ms up to a 3s deadline. Exits as soon as the condition is met (typically ~200-300ms), so no slowdown in the happy path. Net -40 lines.
author: webjunkie

reviews.body: Test-only change replacing brittle fixed sleeps with a polling macro — no production code touched, no API/data model impact, and the logic is sound.

## Pull request overview This PR improves the reliability of the Rust global rate limiter integration tests by replacing fixed-duration sleeps (which are flaky under CI load) with a polling-based assertion helper that waits until Redis/background-sync state converges. **Changes:** - Added `poll_assert!` + polling constants to retry assertions until success or a deadline. - Added `parse_redis_count` helper to centralize Redis counter parsing. - Updated multiple integration tests to use polling instead of fixed sleeps when asserting on Redis state / post-sync behavior.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **rust/common/limiters/tests/global_rate_limiter_integration_tests.rs:339** * This poll repeatedly calls `check_limit("over", 1, ...)`, which enqueues additional writes on every retry and changes the system state while waiting for the background sync. To keep this test focused on “becomes Limited once the sync reflects Redis”, consider polling with `count=0` (no enqueue) and then doing a single `check_limit("over", 1, ...)` assertion once the sync has completed, or poll a read-only signal (e.g. Redis state) instead. ``` let limiter = GlobalRateLimiterImpl::new(config.clone(), vec![redis_arc]).unwrap(); // Cache miss => allowed (fail open) ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `fffa877e34`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

commits.message:
fix(limiters): replace fixed sleeps with polling in rate limiter integration tests

Background pipeline flush races with fixed sleeps under CI load — tick_interval (100ms) +
global_write_timeout (500ms) means worst-case flush is ~600ms, and background task startup
adds another 100-200ms. Tests sleeping 300-500ms miss the window intermittently.

Replace all fixed sleep + assert patterns with a `poll_assert!` macro that retries every
100ms up to a 3s deadline, exiting as soon as the condition is met.

title: fix(apps): guard against management commands in ready()
body:
<!-- mendral:session:action:01KMD080HV15CC0PSXDE1BPYRV -->
## Summary

- Skips `queue_sync_hog_function_templates()` in `PostHogConfig.ready()` when `STATIC_COLLECTION=1` is set, eliminating the Redis `ConnectionError` traceback that appeared in every Docker image build during `collectstatic`.

## Details

`PostHogConfig.ready()` was unconditionally calling `queue_sync_hog_function_templates()` for all non-test Django invocations. That function immediately tries to acquire a Redis distributed lock (`r.setnx`), but no Redis is available during `docker build`. The Dockerfile already sets `STATIC_COLLECTION=1` for the `collectstatic` step, and this env var is already parsed as a Django setting (`posthog/settings/base_variables.py`), so it's the most targeted guard.

Closes insight: Redis ConnectionError logged during every Docker image build (collectstatic)

## Changes

- [`posthog/apps.py`](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/mendral/guard-management-commands-ready/posthog/apps.py): add `not settings.STATIC_COLLECTION` to the existing `not settings.TEST` guard



---

> [!NOTE]
> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.

title: fix(data-warehouse): refine non-retryable error matching to allow transient connection retries
body:
## Problem

When a Postgres source sync fails with a transient connection error like pool exhaustion, the schema's syncing is permanently disabled (`should_sync` set to `False`). Users must manually re-enable syncing even though the database was only temporarily overloaded.

The root cause is the substring pattern `"OperationalError: connection failed: connection to server at"` in `PostgresSource.get_non_retryable_errors()` (`posthog/temporal/data_imports/sources/postgres/source.py:140`). This pattern is intended to catch permanent connection failures (wrong host, firewall), but it also matches transient errors like Supabase/PgBouncer pool exhaustion:

```
OperationalError: connection failed: connection to server at "52.209.89.87",
port 5432 failed: FATAL: MaxClientsInSessionMode: max clients reached
```

Fixes #51819

## Changes

- Removed the overly broad `"OperationalError: connection failed: connection to server at"` pattern from both Postgres and Redshift `get_non_retryable_errors()`
- Added specific permanent failure patterns (`"Connection refused"`, `"No route to host"`) that correctly identify unreachable hosts
- Other permanent failures (wrong password, DNS resolution, no such database) are already handled by separate entries in the error dict
- Added parameterized tests verifying that transient pool errors are retryable and permanent failures remain non-retryable

## How did you test this code?

Added `TestPostgresSourceNonRetryableErrors` in `test_postgres.py` with parameterized tests covering:
- Transient errors (`MaxClientsInSessionMode`, `remaining connection slots are reserved`, `too many connections for role`) are NOT matched as non-retryable
- Permanent errors (`Connection refused`, `No route to host`, `could not translate host name`, `password authentication failed`, `FATAL: no such database`) remain correctly non-retryable

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude Code). The fix was identified by analyzing the substring matching logic in `get_non_retryable_errors()` against the specific error string reported in #51819.
author: mvanhorn

commits.message:
fix(data-warehouse): refine non-retryable error matching to allow transient connection retries

The broad pattern "OperationalError: connection failed: connection to server at"
was matching transient errors like pool exhaustion (MaxClientsInSessionMode),
permanently disabling schema syncing when the database was merely busy.

Replace it with specific permanent failure patterns ("Connection refused",
"No route to host") that correctly identify unreachable hosts. Other permanent
connection failures (wrong password, no such database, DNS failures) are already
handled by separate entries in the non-retryable error list.

Applied to both Postgres and Redshift sources.

fix(data-warehouse): use realistic error strings in non-retryable tests

Address Greptile review feedback: test inputs for permanent errors now use
full error strings matching real psycopg2/Postgres output rather than bare
dictionary keys, giving more confidence in substring matching behavior.

title: chore(mcp): normalize feature names
body:
## Problem

  MCP server has inconsistent feature naming — handcoded tools use hyphens (`error-tracking`, `llm-analytics`,
  `data-schema`) while generated tools use underscores (`error_tracking`). This means
  `?features=error-tracking` only matches 3 handcoded tools but misses 3 generated ones for the same product.
  Additionally, only 7 of 28 available features were documented.

  ## Changes

  - Normalize all feature names to underscores in schema files (`error-tracking` → `error_tracking`,
  `llm-analytics` → `llm_analytics`, `data-schema` → `data_schema`)
  - Add `normalizeFeatureName()` to the matching logic so existing URLs with hyphens remain backward compatible
  - Document all 28 available features in the README with descriptions and doc links

  ## How did you test this code?

  - All 249 existing MCP tests pass
  - Added test cases verifying both hyphenated and underscored feature names match the same tools

  ## Publish to changelog?

  No

  ## Docs update

  README updated with complete feature list.
author: VojtechBartos

commits.message: chore(mcp): normalize feature names to be snake cased
chore(mcp): update docs with actual features

title: chore(mcp): migrate surveys mcp tools to codegen
body:
## Problem

  Survey MCP tools were hand-written — custom API client methods, manual Zod schemas, and bespoke tool handlers. This made them inconsistent with the codegen-based approach used by other products (feature flags,
  actions, persons) and harder to maintain as the survey API evolves.

  ## Changes

  - **Extend the API docs schema** (`posthog/api/survey.py`) — adds `help_text` to serializer fields so drf-spectacular generates richer OpenAPI descriptions that flow into MCP tool schemas
  - **Extend the codegen framework** to support custom soft-delete field names (`soft_delete: "archived"` in YAML, not just `soft_delete: true` → `{ deleted: true }`) and improve operation ID matching to prefer
  exact matches over `_N` deduplicated variants
  - **Add `products/surveys/mcp/tools.yaml`** — declarative config for all 7 survey tools (CRUD, stats, global stats) using the codegen pipeline
  - **Generate survey tools from OpenAPI** — `src/generated/surveys/api.ts` (Zod schemas from OpenAPI) and `src/tools/generated/surveys.ts` (tool handlers) replace all hand-written code
  - **Delete hand-written survey code** — removes `src/schema/surveys.ts`, `src/tools/surveys/` (7 files), and the `surveys()` method from `ApiClient`
  - **Fix `ApiClient.fetchJson`** — handle empty response bodies as `undefined` instead of `{} as T`, and gracefully fall back to raw text when JSON parsing fails
  - **Rewrite integration tests** — consolidated from 21 `it` blocks to 14 focused tests covering CRUD with soft-delete semantics, multi-question types, branching logic, targeting filters, flag linking, stats with
  and without date filters, search/pagination, and error paths
  - **Fix unused import** lint error in `schema.feature-flag-filters.test.ts`

  ## How did you test this code?

- Tests (unit/integrations)
- Manual tests following testing scenarios
```markdown
# Survey MCP Prompt Testing Scenarios

  ## Basic CRUD

  ### S1: List active surveys

  **Prompt:** Show me all active surveys in my project

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `surveys-get-all` with `archived: false`. Returns a list of surveys, each with id, name, type, description, and created_at. No archived surveys appear in the results.

  ### S2: List archived surveys

  **Prompt:** Show me all archived surveys

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `surveys-get-all` with `archived: true`. Every returned survey has `archived: true`.

  ### S3: Search surveys by name

  **Prompt:** Find surveys with "onboarding" in the name

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `surveys-get-all` with `search: "onboarding"`. Results are filtered to surveys whose name contains "onboarding".

  ### S4: Create a basic survey

  **Prompt:** Create a popover survey called "Onboarding Feedback" that asks users to rate their onboarding experience on a scale of 1-10

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with `name: "Onboarding Feedback"`, `type: "popover"`, and a `questions` array containing one rating question with `scale: 10`. Response includes the created survey
  with an `id` and the rating question preserved.

  ### S5: Get survey details

  **Prompt:** Show me the details of survey {id}

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-get` with `id` parameter. Returns full survey including name, description, type, questions, appearance, conditions, targeting, and timestamps.

  ### S6: Update a survey

  **Prompt:** Update survey {id} to change the description to "Updated for Q1"

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-update` with `id` and `description: "Updated for Q1"`. Response shows the updated description. Other fields remain unchanged.

  ### S7: Delete a survey

  **Prompt:** Delete survey {id}

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-delete` with `id`. Response shows `archived: true`. The survey is soft-deleted, not permanently removed. It still appears when listing archived surveys.

  ## Question Types

  ### S8: Open text question

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with an open text question asking "What do you like most about our product?"

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a question of `type: "open"`. The question text matches the prompt.

  ### S9: Single choice question

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with a single choice question about which feature users prefer, with options: Analytics, Feature Flags, Session Replay, and Surveys

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a question of `type: "single_choice"` and `choices` containing all four options.

  ### S10: Multiple choice with open option

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with a multiple choice question where users can select all improvements they want, with an "Other" open choice option

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a question of `type: "multiple_choice"` and `hasOpenChoice: true`.

  ### S11: Emoji rating

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with a 1-5 emoji rating question asking "How was your experience?"

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a rating question having `scale: 5` and `display: "emoji"`.

  ### S12: Mixed question types

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with three questions: an open text question, a single choice question, and a rating scale

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with three questions. Response contains exactly three questions with types `open`, `single_choice`, and `rating` in order.

  ## NPS and Branching

  ### S13: NPS survey with branching

  **Prompt:** Create an NPS survey that shows different follow-up questions to promoters and detractors. Detractors should see "What could we improve?" and promoters should see "What do you love about us?"

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a 0-10 rating question that has `branching: { type: "response_based" }` with `responseValues` mapping detractors and promoters to different follow-up question
  indices. At least two follow-up open text questions are included.

  ### S14: Conditional follow-up

  **Prompt:** Create a survey where the second question only appears if the user rates 3 or below on the first question

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with a rating question containing branching logic that routes low scores to the follow-up and high scores to the end or a different path.

  ## Targeting and Scheduling

  ### S15: Target by user property

  **Prompt:** Create a survey targeted at premium users only

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with `targeting_flag_filters` containing a person property filter for a plan/subscription property matching "premium". Response includes a `targeting_flag` object.

  ### S16: Complex targeting

  **Prompt:** Create a survey that targets users with email containing @company.com who are on the premium or enterprise plan, shown to 75% of matching users

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with `targeting_flag_filters` containing a group with two property filters (email `icontains` "@company.com" and plan `exact` ["premium", "enterprise"]) and
  `rollout_percentage: 75`.

  ### S17: Recurring schedule

  **Prompt:** Create a weekly recurring survey that runs 4 times with a 100 response limit per iteration

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with `schedule: "recurring"`, `iteration_count: 4`, `iteration_frequency_days: 7`, and `responses_limit: 100`. All scheduling fields are preserved in the response.

  ### S18: Link feature flag

  **Prompt:** Link feature flag {flag_id} to survey {survey_id}

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-update` with `id` and `linked_flag_id`. Response shows `linked_flag_id` matching the specified flag.

  ## Appearance and Conditions

  ### S19: Custom appearance

  **Prompt:** Create a survey with a custom appearance: dark background (#1a1a2e), white text, and a blue submit button saying "Send Feedback"

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-create` with `appearance` containing `backgroundColor: "#1a1a2e"`, `textColor: "#ffffff"` (or similar white), `submitButtonColor` set to a blue hex, and `submitButtonText:
  "Send Feedback"`.

  ### S20: URL and device conditions

  **Prompt:** Update survey {id} to only show on pages containing "/product" on desktop and mobile devices

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-update` with `id` and `conditions` containing `url: "/product"`, `urlMatchType: "icontains"`, and `deviceTypes: ["Desktop", "Mobile"]`.

  ## Stats

  ### S21: Survey response count

  **Prompt:** How many responses has survey {id} received?

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-stats` with `id` parameter. Returns an object with `survey_id` matching the requested id and a `stats` object containing response counts.

  ### S22: Stats with date range

  **Prompt:** Show me survey response stats for survey {id} from January 1 to March 31 2025

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `survey-stats` with `id`, `date_from: "2025-01-01..."`, and `date_to: "2025-03-31..."`. Returns stats scoped to that date range.

  ### S23: Global stats

  **Prompt:** Give me an overview of survey response rates across all my surveys

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `surveys-global-stats`. Returns aggregated `stats` and `rates` across all surveys in the project.

  ### S24: Global stats with date range

  **Prompt:** Compare response stats for all surveys in the last 30 days

  **Expected behavior:** Calls `surveys-global-stats` with `date_from` set to 30 days ago and `date_to` set to today. Returns aggregated stats for the period.

  ## Workflows

  ### S25: Create, target, and check stats

  **Prompt:** Create a customer satisfaction survey, set up targeting for premium users, then check how many responses we've received

  **Expected behavior:** Three tool calls in sequence: (1) `survey-create` returns a new survey with an id, (2) `survey-update` adds `targeting_flag_filters` for premium users, (3) `survey-stats` returns response
  counts. The assistant threads the survey id through all three calls.

  ### S26: Create, inspect, and update

  **Prompt:** Create an NPS survey, show me its details, then update it to add a follow-up open text question

  **Expected behavior:** Three tool calls: (1) `survey-create` with NPS rating question, (2) `survey-get` returns the created survey, (3) `survey-update` adds an open text question to the existing questions array.
  The final survey has both the original rating and the new open text question.

  ### S27: List, find, and get stats

  **Prompt:** List all surveys, find the one about onboarding, and show me its response stats

  **Expected behavior:** (1) `surveys-get-all` (possibly with `search: "onboarding"`) returns survey list, (2) assistant identifies the onboarding survey from results, (3) `survey-stats` with that survey's id
  returns response statistics.
```

  ## Publish to changelog?

  No

  ## Docs update

  No docs changes needed — MCP tool definitions are auto-generated.
author: VojtechBartos

commits.message: chore(survey): extending the API docs schema
chore(mcp): soft delete and operation matching tweaks
fixup! chore(mcp): migrate surveys to codegen

title: perf: pause pinned tabs storage listener when page is hidden
body:
## Summary

- The `storage` event listener for cross-tab pinned tab sync fires for every `localStorage` write from other tabs, keeping background tabs awake unnecessarily
- Pauses the listener when the page becomes hidden via the Page Visibility API
- Resumes on visibility with a catch-up sync to pick up any changes that happened while hidden

## Test plan

- [ ] Open two PostHog tabs, pin a tab in one, verify it syncs to the other
- [ ] Switch the receiving tab to background, pin/unpin in the active tab, switch back — verify the state catches up
- [ ] Verify no storage event listener is registered while the tab is in the background (DevTools > Elements > Event Listeners)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: pauldambra

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR aims to reduce background-tab work by pausing the cross-tab pinned-tabs `storage` listener while the page is hidden, then resuming it on visibility with a catch-up sync. **Changes:** - Wrap `storage` listener registration in helper functions so it can be added/removed dynamically. - Add a `visibilitychange` handler to remove the `storage` listener when hidden and re-add it when visible. - Trigger a catch-up pinned-tab sync when the page becomes visible again. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

No showstoppers. The change correctly pauses the storage listener when the page is hidden and catches up on page-visible using a synthetic event; the handler only reads `event.key` and then fetches state directly from storage, so the synthetic event is safe. Cleanup is handled properly.

commits.message:
perf: pause pinned tabs storage listener when page is hidden

The storage event listener for cross-tab pinned tab sync fires for
every localStorage write from other tabs, keeping background tabs
awake unnecessarily. Pause the listener when the page is hidden and
resume on visibility, with a catch-up sync to pick up any changes
that happened while hidden.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: extract syncPinnedTabsFromStorage to avoid synthetic StorageEvent

Extract the shared sync logic into a dedicated function instead of
constructing a synthetic StorageEvent to call the handler. The storage
listener calls it after the key guard, and the visibility handler
calls it directly on becoming visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: rely on disposablesPlugin for pause/resume instead of manual visibilitychange

The disposablesPlugin already tears down and re-runs setup functions
on page hidden/visible transitions (pauseOnPageHidden defaults to
true). Remove the redundant manual visibilitychange listener and
instead call syncPinnedTabsFromStorage() at setup time so it runs
on both initial mount and each resume from hidden.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(error-tracking): use decompressed byte count for cymbal cache memory accounting
body:
## Problem

After zstd compression was added to the symbol_data container format (#51484), cymbal's symbol set cache started tracking **compressed** byte sizes while storing **decompressed and parsed** objects. This caused the cache to underestimate actual memory usage by 5-20x, preventing eviction from triggering in time and leading to OOM events.

## Changes

- Added `read_symbol_data_with_byte_count` to the `posthog-symbol-data` crate, which returns the decompressed payload size alongside the parsed data
- Changed the `Caching` layer to measure byte count on the **parsed** type (`P::Set: Countable`) instead of the fetched data (`P::Fetched: Countable`)
- Each parsed symbol type now stores the decompressed byte count and implements `Countable`:
  - `OwnedSourceMapCache` (JS sourcemaps)
  - `ParsedAppleSymbols` (dSYM)
  - `ParsedHermesMap` (React Native Hermes)
  - `FetchedMapping` (Proguard)
- Removed the now-unused `Countable` impl for `Saveable`

## How did you test this code?

- All existing cymbal tests pass (19 tests)
- All posthog-symbol-data tests pass (7 tests)

## Publish to changelog?

no
author: hpouillot

commits.message: fix(error-tracking): use decompressed byte count for cymbal cache memory accounting

title: fix(replay): reduce session recording API rate limits to better match steady state and bursts we see
body:
## Problem

Session recording rate limits need to be adjusted to better align with usage patterns and system capacity. The current rate limits may be too permissive for free tier users and need rebalancing across all tiers.

## Changes

- Reduced free tier snapshot and listing rates from 60/minute to 12/minute for burst traffic
- Reduced free tier sustained rates from 300/hour to 60/hour  
- Reduced paid tier burst rates from 90/minute to 60/minute
- Reduced paid tier sustained rates from 500/hour to 300/hour
- Reduced enterprise tier burst rates from 120/minute to 100/minute
- Reduced enterprise tier sustained rates from 600/hour to 400/hour
- Updated corresponding test expectations to match the new rate limits

## How did you test this code?

The existing parameterized tests for `TestSnapshotRatesConfig` and `TestListingRatesConfig` were updated to verify the new rate limits are correctly applied across all tiers and scopes. The tests validate that the rate configuration returns the expected values for each combination of tier (free/paid/enterprise) and scope (burst/sustained).

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

Rate limit changes may need documentation updates if these limits are publicly documented.
author: pauldambra

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adjusts the default session replay (snapshot + listing) API rate limits across free/paid/enterprise tiers to better match observed steady-state and burst traffic, and updates the existing throttle configuration tests accordingly. **Changes:** - Reduced default free tier burst limits for snapshots and listings to 12/minute and sustained to 60/hour. - Reduced default paid tier burst limits to 60/minute and sustained to 300/hour. - Reduced default enterprise tier burst limits to 100/minute and sustained to 400/hour, and updated parameterized test expectations. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | posthog/settings/session_replay.py | Updates the default environment-backed rate limit constants for snapshot and listing throttles across tiers. | | posthog/session_recordings/test/test_session_recording_throttle.py | Updates the parameterized config tests to match the new default rate strings. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(replay): reduce session recording API rate limits to cut costs

Free tier: 12/min burst, 60/hour sustained (from 60/min, 300/hour)
Paid tier: 60/min burst, 300/hour sustained (from 90/min, 500/hour)
Enterprise: 100/min burst, 400/hour sustained (from 120/min, 600/hour)

title: fix: guard trackFileSystemLogView against missing team ID during login
body:
## Problem

- After Google OAuth or 2FA login, users hit `Error: Team ID is not known.`
- `sceneLogic.setScene` calls `trackFileSystemLogView()` on every scene transition, including login/2FA scenes
- `trackFileSystemLogView` → `ApiRequest.environmentsDetail()` calls `ApiConfig.getCurrentTeamId()` which throws because `teamLogic` hasn't loaded yet
- [Error trace](https://us.posthog.com/project/2/error_tracking?searchQuery=Team%20ID%20is%20not%20known)

## Changes

- Add `ApiConfig.hasCurrentTeamId()` non-throwing check
- Guard `trackFileSystemLogView` to bail when team ID isn't available — fire-and-forget telemetry, safe to skip

## How did you test this code?

Traced from stack trace, verified TS compilation. Guard clause on fire-and-forget call — no behavioral change for authenticated users.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: yasen-posthog

commits.message: fix: guard trackFileSystemLogView against missing team ID during login
fix: combine guard conditionals in trackFileSystemLogView

title: fix: redirect to login with error instead of 500 on expired OAuth invite
body:
## Problem

- When a user completes Google OAuth with an expired/revoked invite, `process_social_invite_signup` raises `ValidationError` which python-social-auth doesn't handle, resulting in a 500 crash page (~43 occurrences in 48h)

## Changes

- Replace `raise ValidationError` with `return redirect("/login?error_code=invalid_invite")`, matching the existing pattern for other social auth failures
- Add `invalid_invite` error message to the login page

## How did you test this code?

Traced from production logs. Follows the same redirect pattern used by `jit_not_enabled` and `no_new_organizations` error codes.

## Publish to changelog?

No
author: yasen-posthog

commits.message: fix: redirect to login with error instead of 500 on expired OAuth invite
fix: add return type for redirect path to satisfy mypy
fix: propagate redirect at both call sites to satisfy mypy
fix: don't redirect existing users on stale invite, only new signups
fix: simplify redirect check at new-user call site
fix: return False from invite lookup, let caller handle redirect
test: verify process_social_invite_signup returns False for invalid invite
fix: use separate variable to satisfy mypy type narrowing
fix: use Optional[User] instead of Union[User, bool] for cleaner types

title: feat(replay): add confirmation modal when changing recording retention
body:
## Problem

When users change their session replay retention settings, the change is applied immediately with no warning. Users may not realize that changing retention only affects **future** recordings — existing recordings keep their original retention period.

## Changes

Added a `LemonDialog` confirmation modal that opens when a user selects a different retention period. The modal:
- Warns that the change only affects recordings starting from that point forwards
- Shows the specific retention period they're changing to (e.g. "Change retention to 90 days")
- Requires explicit confirmation before applying the setting
- Allows canceling to leave the setting unchanged

## How did you test this code?

Manual testing in the session replay settings page.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: pauldambra

Learn how to get started.
