title: fix(replay): add server-side cache-control to config endpoints
body:
## Problem

The `/array/{token}/config` and `/array/{token}/config.js` endpoints rely on Cloudflare's proxy layer (via `eu-assets.i.posthog.com`) to add `cache-control` headers. Customers using CNAME-based reverse proxies bypass Cloudflare entirely, so browsers fall back to heuristic caching and may hold stale config for hours or days — causing SDK settings (recording conditions, feature flags, sampling rates) to stop updating.

## Changes

- Add `add_config_cache_headers()` helper that sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` and `Vary: Origin, Referer`
- Apply it to `RemoteConfigAPIView` (JSON config) and `RemoteConfigJSAPIView` (JS config)
- `RemoteConfigArrayJSAPIView` (array.js) already has its own cache headers via `add_cache_headers()`

## How did you test this code?

- Added `test_config_includes_cache_control` and `test_config_js_includes_cache_control` tests
- All tests pass: `pytest posthog/api/test/test_remote_config.py` (3 passed)

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

Companion PR updating reverse proxy docs to route `/array/*` to the assets origin: PostHog/posthog.com#16081 (merged).

## LLM context

Discovered while investigating a customer support ticket where a Cloudflare CNAME proxy wasn't getting cache-control headers. Root cause: all three EU origins (`eu.i`, `eu-proxy-direct.i`, `eu-assets.i`) point to the same AWS ELB — only `eu-assets.i` has Cloudflare proxying enabled, which is what adds the cache-control header. CNAME proxies resolve to `proxy-direct` (DNS-only), bypassing Cloudflare entirely.
author: TueHaulund

commits.message:
fix(replay): add server-side cache-control to config endpoints

The /array/{token}/config and /array/{token}/config.js endpoints relied
on Cloudflare's proxy layer (via eu-assets.i.posthog.com) to add
cache-control headers. Customers using CNAME-based reverse proxies
bypass Cloudflare entirely, so browsers fall back to heuristic caching
and may hold stale config for hours or days.

Add Cache-Control: public, max-age=300 directly in Django so all
request paths get consistent caching behavior.

title: feat: Backfill backend refactoring for Exports
body:
## Problem

Although pausing is nice, we need to give users a way to recover after unpausing. This adds the option to backfill when unpausing. The backfill will execute the data missed during the time we were paused.

NOTE: Backfills are **not** using the Temporal API. This is **not** how I originally coded it, so I will be refactoring the `backfill_exports` method too.
UPDATE: DONE with the refactoring.


TODO: Frontend changes. There is a PR open to add a button to trigger a historical export which uses the backfill endpoint that will need to be refactored: #16157.
UPDATE: I added my own frontend changes here: #16286

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

## Changes

Unpausing endpoint now takes a `backfill` body param that can be set to `true` to trigger a backfill from the moment the schedule was paused.

Backfills are now properly done via the Temporal API. This is better for several reasons:
* It actually works. Really. In contrast, existing implementation is buggy:
  * It's passing `batch_export.pk` as `team_id`.
  * It's not passing `data_interval_start`, so the backfill is only backfilling an hour/day. 
* We can overwrite the schedule overlap policy for backfill runs. This allows us to run all backfills as soon as possible, but we can also limit concurrency in the future. The sky is the limit.
* The runs created will look just as if the schedule had been running during the backfill. This means the same id and batch time bounds.

<!-- If there are frontend changes, please include screenshots. -->
<!-- If a reference design was involved, include a link to the relevant Figma frame! -->

Added multiple tests for pausing/unpausing.

title: fix(search): guard against non-string person properties in search res…
body:
Person properties like `email` and `name` can be complex objects at runtime (e.g. Clerk auth objects) rather than plain strings. This caused the search UI to render [object Object] instead of a meaningful name.

## Problem
What we got (Clerk object):

```
"email": {
      "id": "idn_xxxxxx",
      "destroy": {},
      "linkedTo": [],
      "pathRoot": "/me/email_addresses",
      "toString": {},
      "emailAddress": "xxxxxxx@gmail.com",
      "verification": {
          "error": null,
          "status": "verified",
          "strategy": "email_code"
      },
      "attemptVerification": {},
      "prepareVerification": {},
      "matchesSsoConnection": false
}
```

What we expected:

```
"email": "xxxxxxx@gmail.com"
```

## Changes
* added a safeStr guard that checks typeof val  === 'string' before using email or name as the display name. If either property is an object (or anything non-string), we skip it and fall through to the next fallback: email → name → personId. 

## How did you test this code?
Seeing error (on prod), copying network for search, finding issue and patching it (best i could locally)

## Publish to changelog?
No
author: adamleithp

commits.message:
fix(search): guard against non-string person properties in search results

Person properties like `email` and `name` can be complex objects at
runtime (e.g. Clerk auth objects) rather than plain strings. This caused
the search UI to render [object Object] instead of a meaningful name.

title: refactor(logs): decouple data fetching and analytics from scene-level URL sync
body:
## Problem

Data fetching and analytics tracking in the logs viewer were tightly coupled to `logsSceneLogic` via URL sync. This meant `LogsViewer` embedded outside the scene (e.g. in a modal) couldn't auto-refetch when filters changed.

Additionally, adding a new empty attribute filter triggered unnecessary query executions.

## Changes

- Moved query execution (`runQuery`) and analytics tracking into `logsViewerDataLogic` via listeners + a `filterGroup` subscription, so any `LogsViewer` instance auto-refetches on filter changes regardless of context
- Introduced `handleQueryChange` action as a single coordination point, data logic fires it, scene logic listens to sync URL + push filter history
- Simplified `logsSceneLogic` to only handle URL sync and filter history (removed ~130 lines of duplicated filter listeners)
- Added `shouldSkipFilterGroupChange` to prevent query execution when a new empty filter row is added, while still allowing edits to existing filters even when other filters are incomplete
- Added `isSyncingUrl` guard to prevent kea-router `urlToAction` feedback loops when syncing filters to URL

## How did you test this code?

- 43 unit tests covering auto-refetch behavior, empty filter skipping, filter completion, editing while another filter is empty, and `shouldSkipFilterGroupChange` edge cases
- Manual testing

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A
author: jonmcwest

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Refactors the Logs viewer so query execution and analytics tracking are handled in `logsViewerDataLogic` (via listeners/subscriptions) instead of being coupled to `logsSceneLogic` URL sync, enabling embedded `LogsViewer` instances (e.g. modals) to auto-refetch when filters change. **Changes:** - Moves filter-change query triggering + analytics capture into `logsViewerDataLogic` and adds `shouldSkipFilterGroupChange` to avoid executing queries when adding a new empty attribute filter row. - Introduces `handleQueryChange` as the coordination action: data logic dispatches it; scene logic listens to sync URL + push filter history. - Adds an `isSyncingUrl` cache guard to prevent `kea-router` `actionToUrl` / `urlToAction` feedback loops. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | products/logs/frontend/logsSceneLogic.tsx | Simplifies scene logic to URL sync + filter history; adds `isSyncingUrl` guard and responds to `handleQueryChange`. | | products/logs/frontend/components/LogsViewer/data/logsViewerDataLogic.ts | Centralizes query execution + analytics; subscribes to `filterGroup` changes and coordinates via `handleQueryChange`. | | products/logs/frontend/components/LogsViewer/data/logsViewerDataLogic.test.ts | Adds unit tests for auto-refetch behavior and `shouldSkipFilterGroupChange` edge cases. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.
